Name: Elinor O’Donovan: Winter Sun
Description:
Winter Sun is a moving-image work by Cork artist Elinor O’Donovan.

The work is a preserved piece of a warm Cork summer, saved for the months when it is most needed. Taking as its inspiration the iconic view from the top of Patrick’s Hill at Bells Field, the video work is a looping film depicting Corkonians sharing a moment of togetherness, connected by their mutual appreciation for a setting summer sun.

Projection-Mapping: Lightscape Studios
3D Artist: Bassam Issa Al-Sabah
Cinematography: Jack Desmond

Part of Island City – Cork’s Urban Sculpture Trail.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: The Flag by artist Emily Aoibheann - The National Gallery of Ireland
Description:
Blending contemporary dance, live brass, sculptural sound and archival research, the work unfolds as a promenade experience that audiences are free to enter, exit, and linger with.

The Flag reflects on national identity as something felt through resonance and tension, exploring how history inhabits bodies, objects and sound. Performed without fixed seating, the piece invites viewers to move through cycles of playful horn calls, resonant tuba solos, layered field recordings, and intricate ribbon choreography.

The Flag transforms the Shaw Room into a living sculpture, where movement and music unfold as a mesmeric process.

The Flag is funded by the Arts Council / An Chomhairle Ealaíon and supported by Dance Ireland.s
Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Sligo Chess and Culture Festival 2026
Description:
Visitors to the Sligo Chess and Culture Festival 2026 can enjoy ten days of world-class chess, vibrant cultural experiences, and family-friendly activities in Ireland’s northwest. Highlights include Ireland’s only nine-round open classical tournament offering master-title opportunities, plus simultaneous exhibitions, workshops, themed social events, an astronomy night, guided nature outings, and traditional music. 

Four tournaments cater to all ages, with student competitions adding energy to the festival. A professional smart-board hall enhances the experience. A community beach cleanup with Clean Coasts and local business partnerships offer visitors meaningful and enjoyable ways to explore Sligo’s hospitality and culture.
Address: Diamond Coast Hotel, Bartragh, Enniscrone, Sligo

Name: Neon Painting: Paint Like Picasso
Description:
Our team will take each person step by step into creating a masterpiece with no experience necessary.

 - Artist (to help you paint)

The bar has alcohol for sale, there is no BYOB option.
Over 18s only, ID required.

Description:
Hailing from County Tyrone, The Whistlin’ Donkeys have been performing together since 2011 and have gained a strong following in Ireland and around the world.

The band are known for their unique blend of traditional Irish music and Celtic rock influences, creating their own distinctive sound that will keep your feet stomping and hands clapping all night long.

Whether you're a fan of Irish folk music or Celtic rock, the Whistlin' Donkeys are a band that you won't want to miss. Make sure to check out their Linktree to stay up to date on their latest releases, tour dates, and more!

We’re proudly returning to The National Opera House and bringing a whole new wave of 80s magic!

You sang with us. You danced in the aisles. And now, it’s time to do it all over again… but this time, we’re turning everything up a notch.

Name: Drawing the Collection
Description:
Drawing the Collection is a monthly, adult-focused workshop held on the last Thursday of each month. Each session takes inspiration from a painting or an exhibition, giving participants the chance to draw directly from works in the galleries with guidance from the facilitating artist. All materials are provided by the Gallery.

This month, we’re taking inspiration from both the AIB Portrait Prize and the AIB Young Portrait Prize. Former Young Prize exhibitor Ross McHale will be leading the session, exploring how to create a portrait and how different elements can be used to reflect social themes and individual personalities in the work.

Name: The Flag
Description:
Blending contemporary dance, live brass, sculptural sound and archival research, the work unfolds as a promenade experience that audiences are free to enter, exit, and linger with.

Emerging from Emily Aoibheann’s research at the National Gallery of Ireland and from parallel explorations undertaken through her Markievicz Award, The Flag reflects on national identity as something felt through resonance and tension, exploring how history inhabits bodies, objects, and sound. Performed without fixed seating, the piece invites viewers to move through cycles of playful horn calls, resonant tuba solos, layered field recordings, and intricate ribbon choreography. 

Thursday 29 January, additional evening performance at 6pm

Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Where the Elements Meet: The Poetic Ground of Seamus Heaney - The Air’s Story
Description:
In the final event of our series, join us for a cloud-skimming adventure at Seamus Heaney: Listen Now Again. As we breeze gently through the exhibition, we will explore Heaney’s relationship with the element of air: a medium of revelation in which memory stirs, sound resonates, and the everyday brushes against the miraculous.

This tour will highlight air as a force of nature, shaping landscapes and human experience; its vital role as a carrier of sound, light, and scent; and its union with the heavenly and transcendent, moments when the ordinary world seems to lift into wonder.

Free, booking advised.

Name: St. Brigid's Medieval Style Candlelit Procession
Description:
This experiential event is an ideal opportunity for both young and old to immerse themselves in a unique experience. It is an ancient style candlelit procession from St. Brigid's Shrine to Faughart Graveyard which includes robed figures, bonfires, uileann pipes, Gregorian chants, candlit ruins and much more. 

This is a celebration of Brigid the Saint, the Goddess, the Woman and the Local. It is for all religions and none. 
An incredible atmosphere on the amazing ancient Faughart Hill. Not to be missed!

Name: A Dinner To Die For
Description:
Begin the evening with a Bloody Mary reception, before exploring Malahide Castle’s historic rooms on a guided tour uncovering stories of thwarted love, broken hearts and romances that ended badly. These tales reveal that not all love stories had a happy ending behind castle walls.

The experience concludes in Nugent’s Hall, where guests will enjoy a hearty, indulgent dinner served in richly atmospheric surroundings – a feast where the secrets are as rich as the gravy.

Entertaining, immersive and full of character, this evening is perfect for those looking for something a little different in February.

Description:
The original Carpenters tribute is back with a brand-new show for 2026! Featuring Gordy Marshall’s, The British Carpenters, starring the fantastic Sally Creedon as Karen Carpenter and with an incredible cast made up of musicians straight from London’s West End. Bringing to life all the hits including, Close to You, Top of the World, Rainy Days and Mondays, Please Mr Postman, Goodbye to Love and many more as we recreate the soundtrack of our lives.

This is a tribute show and is no way affiliated with any original artists/estates/management companies or similar shows.

Name: The King of All Birds
Description:
“I’m going to do a few songs today. In about 60 minutes, we’re going to crown a new king. And then, I’m going to fly.”

We’re obsessed with seeing ourselves from above. We display aerial photos of our homes, we gather in our masses to watch men fly, and, every couple of hundred years, we crown a king.

The King of All Birds is a gig for voice and vocoder. It picks apart our shared history with the sky: the years when it remained untouched, those first ventures into it, and now, our endless climbing: up, and up, and up.

“When you leave the theatre and look up at the sky, it will have you imagining all sorts of stories playing out above our heads” ★★★★ The Irish Times
Address: Civic Theatre, Parthalán Place, Belgard Rd, Tallaght, Dublin

Name: The Magic of Motown
Description:
Seen by millions, The Magic of Motown is back with its 20th Anniversary Tour! Come celebrate as we Reach Out and show you there ain’t no mountain high enough with the biggest Motown party of the year.

Get ready for all the hits, glittering costumes, dazzling dance routines and outstanding musicianship in this breath-taking live concert spectacular. Go down memory lane with all the Motown classics from artists such as: Marvin Gaye, Diana Ross, Stevie Wonder, The Temptations, The Supremes, The Four Tops, Martha Reeves, The Jackson 5, Smokey Robinson, & many, many more. 

This is a tribute show and is no way affiliated with any original artists/estates/management companies or similar shows. Promoter reserves the right to alter the programme.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Hyperactive
Description:
HYPERACTIVE shows a snapshot of contemporary Irish masculinity and diversity. Stretching horseplay and dance to the limit, this high-energy piece features a diverse cast of four virtuosic outstanding male dancers, whose backgrounds range from Broadway to downtown to Afro Brazilian street dance. 

Premiered at Galway Arts Festival and Dublin Dance Festival, Five Lamps Arts Festival and internationally to Festival Racconti di Altre Danze, Livorno, Italy, Espace Culturel Hermine, Sarzeau, France and La MaMa Moves Dance Festival, New York. Irish tour: Visual GB Shaw Carlow, The Dock Arts Centre, Carrick-on-Shannon, Garage, Monaghan, Five Lamps Arts Festival.
Address: Civic Theatre, Parthalán Place, Belgard Rd, Tallaght, Dublin

Name: Crimson & Velvet
Description:
We’re ditching the traditional murder mystery and replacing it with a “scandal hunt” – a playful, light-hearted journey through the castle uncovering the dirty laundry of the aristocracy. As you explore, you’ll encounter tales of the past’s most infamous toxic couples, where passion ran high and propriety was often left behind.

Following the castle experience, guests will settle in to enjoy a luxurious four-course dinner, designed to complement an evening of romance, indulgence and storytelling.

This experience is fun, flirtatious and immersive – history with the gloves off, but never over the top. Expect plenty of craic, beautiful food and an unforgettable Valentine’s night in one of Ireland’s most iconic castles.
Address: Malahide Castle & Gardens, Back Rd, Malahide Demesne, Malahide, Dublin

Name: The Man In The Woman’s Shoes
Description:
The Man In The Woman’s Shoes follows Pat Farnon as he walks to town and back again. It is a beautifully observed piece, utterly simple and widely praised. The show is funny, tender and at times downright daft.

Originally commissioned by The Hawk’s Well Theatre Sligo and Sligo Co. Council Arts Service as part of the Bealtaine Festival 2012, The Man In The Woman’s Shoes has toured to Manhattan and Philadelphia in 2015 as well as a sell out, three week run at the Abbey Theatre on the Peacock stage in Dublin. In 2016 it returned to NYC and also played to full houses over three weeks at the Tricycle Theatre in London. It played again at the Irish Arts Centre in NYC in Sept/ Oct 2018 and has been touring Ireland ever since.
Address: Civic Theatre, Parthalán Place, Belgard Rd, Tallaght, Dublin

Name: Smokie
Description:
"The Irish Collection” show will feature every single hit record Smokie have had and will give you a sample of some current albums they’ve made and which have been big chart hits for them in many European countries. Last year alone, Smokie have toured Australia, South Africa, China and South Korea, bringing their unique Country Rock sound to hundreds of thousands of Smokie fans around the world! 

Performing some of their best loved songs like “Something’s Been Making Me Blue”, “I’ll Meet You At Midnight”, “Don’t Play Your Rock ‘n’ Roll To Me”, If You Think You Know How To Love Me”and “Livin’ Next Door To Alice”. Also “Whiskey in the jar” which they perform everywhere in the world as part of their “Live” set.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Fishamble presents SILENT by Pat Kinevane
Description:
He now dives into the wonderful wounds of his past through the romantic world of Rudolph Valentino. Dare to laugh at despair and gasp at redemption in this brave, bleak, beautiful production for which Fishamble and Pat Kinevane won an Olivier Award in 2016. ​

WINNER Helen Hayes Award Outstanding Performer – Visiting Production, 2020

WINNER Outstanding Achievement in an Affiliate Theatre, OLIVIER AWARD 2016

WINNER Scotsman Fringe First and The Herald Angel, Edinburgh Festival 2011

WINNER Argus Angel, Brighton Festival 2012

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Elinor O’Donovan: Winter Sun
Description:
Winter Sun is a moving-image work by Cork artist Elinor O’Donovan.

The work is a preserved piece of a warm Cork summer, saved for the months when it is most needed. Taking as its inspiration the iconic view from the top of Patrick’s Hill at Bells Field, the video work is a looping film depicting Corkonians sharing a moment of togetherness, connected by their mutual appreciation for a setting summer sun.

3D Artist: Bassam Issa Al-Sabah
Cinematography: Jack Desmond

Part of Island City – Cork’s Urban Sculpture Trail.

Taking as its inspiration the iconic view from the top of Patrick’s Hill at Bells Field, the video work is a looping film depicting Corkonians sharing a moment of togetherness, connected by their mutual appreciation for a setting summer sun.

Projection-Mapping: Lightscape Studios
3D Artist: Bassam Issa Al-Sabah
Cinematography: Jack Desmond

Part of Island City – Cork’s Urban Sculpture Trail.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Daily Lough Ree & River Shannon Cruise
Description:
Departing from Athlone Castle, this cruise travels upstream on the River Shannon to Lough Ree. During the sailing there is commentary on local history, the religious settlements on the islands and tales of Viking treasure. This is a boat trip that can be enjoyed by all ages and is suitable for any family occasion. Don a Viking helmet and prepare for battle or simply sit back with an Irish Coffee. Either way, you will enjoy the beauty of the Inland Waterways and witness everything Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands has to offer.

Check the website for the latest sailing times. Tickets available onboard.

Name: A Field in England [Ben Wheatley Season]
Description:
England during the Civil War and a small group of deserters flee from a raging battle through an overgrown field. They are captured by two men: O'Neil and Cutler. O'Neil (Michael Smiley), an alchemist, forces the group to aid him in his search to find a hidden treasure that he believes is buried in the field. Crossing a vast mushroom circle, which provides their first meal, the group quickly descend into a chaos of arguments, fighting and paranoia, and, as it becomes clear that the treasure might be something other than gold, they slowly become victim to the terrifying energies trapped inside the field.
 
Ben Wheatley will be in attendance at the Triskel Arts Centre on January 28 to present his latest film BULK.

Name: The Flag
Description:
Blending contemporary dance, live brass, sculptural sound and archival research, the work unfolds as a promenade experience that audiences are free to enter, exit, and linger with.

Emerging from Emily Aoibheann’s research at the National Gallery of Ireland and from parallel explorations undertaken through her Markievicz Award, The Flag reflects on national identity as something felt through resonance and tension, exploring how history inhabits bodies, objects, and sound. Performed without fixed seating, the piece invites viewers to move through cycles of playful horn calls, resonant tuba solos, layered field recordings, and intricate ribbon choreography. 

Thursday 29 January, additional evening performance at 6pm

Name: Book Launch: Booleying in Ireland by Dr Theresa McDonald
Description:
The National Museum of Ireland at Turlough Park is pleased to host the launch of a new book by Dr Theresa McDonald of the Achill Archaeological Field School, exploring the ancient farming tradition of booleying. 

The event takes place at 6pm in the exhibition galleries and will be officially launched by Professor Michael O’Connell, Member of the Royal Irish Academy and Distinguished Emeritus Professor of Botany at the University of Galway. The book offers insight into the practice of booleying on Achill Island and draws on extensive research from Ireland, Britain, and Europe.

Free admission, no booking required.

Name: Adrian Crowley
Description:
Adrian Crowley, is one of Ireland’s most revered songwriters and a former Choice Prize winner. He released his tenth album Measure of Joy in February 2025. The album, including the first single Tangled, was produced by long-time friend John Parish (PJ Harvey, This Is The Kit).

It’s an album full of delicate and beautiful nocturnal songs, and features backing vocals from Nadine Khouri. Receiving high praise from Cerys Mathews (BBC 6 music) and NPR, the album also features John Parrish, Jim Barr (Portishead) and Cora Venus Lunny. 

Hot Press described the album as a "wonderfully poetic effort".

The first single from the album Brother Was A Runaway was recently featured on House of Guinness (Netflix).

Name: Celebrating Brigit's Eve
Description:
We will share some of the rich traditions associated with Brigit and the old Celtic festival of Imbolc, including songs, stories and rituals. We will make Brigit’s crosses out of rushes, put out the Brat Bhríde and reflect on the relevance of Brigit as goddess and saint on our lives today.

There will be a short meditation, so the evening is suitable for adults and interested teens.

Brought to you by Carol Barrett, Jenny Beale, Caroline McFadden and Jacinta Corbett.

Tickets: standard €15 contribution to Brigit’s Garden or a supporting contribution of €20, with concession tickets at €10.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Brigit's Garden & Café, Pollagh, Rosscahill, Galway

Name: Cape Clear Island Races
Description:
OUTER EDGE Éire sets out to introduce Ireland's most unique trail running adventures to explore the untouched natural beauty of the Emerald Isle.

Our first introduction into Ireland, a truly unique race and the first of its kind in Éire. The race will take place on Cape Clear Island, West Cork.

The inaugural event will include both a half marathon and 10km races. The routes are interestingly quiet and typical of West Cork.. The paved lanes are fuschia-infused. The single-track trails take you across rough island paths, toward steep climbs and rolling descents which combine to reveal the island’s beauty and far beyond with views to the Fastnet Lighthouse

The ferry is included in the ticket.. and once the raceboat is full, the race is FULL!
Address: Cape Clear Island, Cork

Name: Celtic Whiskey Spring Fest
Description:
Celtic Whiskey Spring Fest returns to the Great Hall at IMMA on Saturday, March 7th 2026, bringing together Irish whiskey, craft spirits and great hospitality in one of Dublin’s most stunning venues. 

This one day celebration welcomes 25 leading producers, offering the chance to meet the makers, sample exceptional spirits and discover new favourites just in time for St Patrick’s season. 

With free sampling included, a tasting glass to take home, a Celtic Whiskey Pop Up Shop on site and food available in the café, it is the perfect way to kick off spring and celebrate Ireland’s spirit scene in style.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Irish Museum of Modern Art, Royal Hospital Kilmainham, Military Rd, Kilmainham, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Chip Wickham
Description:
From his early days in the UK breakbeat scene to a 17-year collaboration with Gondwana Records, Chip has crafted a series of acclaimed solo albums drawing on spiritual jazz, 60s British jazz, and contemporary influences like Jazzanova and The Cinematic Orchestra.

Ages 18+ only.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Sugar Club, 8 Leeson Street Lower, Saint Kevin's, Dublin

Name: Ceramics Tour & Pinch Pottery Workshop
Description:
Join a hand-building pottery class at the National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks on Saturday, 21 February, 11:40 – 13:40. Led by Throwing Shapes, this beginner-friendly workshop teaches simple techniques like pinching and coiling. Your piece will be glazed in your chosen color and ready for collection in 4–6 weeks. All materials included.

Enhance your experience with an exclusive 40-minute ceramics tour at 11am with Dr. Aisling Molloy, exploring Celtic Revival pieces and their cultural impact. Limited to 15 spaces—book early!

Short nails required. Queries: commercialevents@museum.ie.

By joining, you support the National Museum of Ireland—thank you!

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks, Collins Barracks, Benburb St, Stoneybatter, Dublin

Name: Brigit Spirit of Kildare - Fireside Stories with Volkidana
Description:
Volkidana brings a breathtaking fireside storytelling spectacle to Maynooth—where myth blazes to life through puppetry, aerial artistry and elemental performance in the tale of the Raiders of Cooley. Expect a legendary tale reborn in sparks, shadow and soaring imagination, inviting audiences of all ages into a world where folklore roars with fire.

Show times 6pm, 7pm & 8pm

Free outdoor event, no booking required.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Maynooth, Kildare

Name: Brigit: Dublin City Celebrating Women
Description:
Across four days, the city will come alive with a vibrant programme of talks, performances, walks, workshops, music, visual art and shared moments, celebrating women’s creativity, heritage and influence in Dublin today. Rooted in the legacy of Brigit and reimagined for a contemporary city, the festival invites everyone to gather, reflect and celebrate together.

Visit the Dublin.ie website for full programme of events.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Dublin

Name: Brigit x District Gemma Dunleavy
Description:
Her return for Brigit feels timely. Rooted in stories of working-class Dublin and shaped by her upbringing in Sheriff Street, her club-pop songs balance emotional clarity with sharp, understated production.

Set within the intimate surroundings of the Presbyterian Church, this show offers a focused live moment to mark Brigit: Dublin City Celebrating Women [30 Jan – 2 Feb 2026] — a programme that celebrates renewal, creativity and women’s voices at the start of spring.

Presented in partnership with District Magazine.

€20 (Early Bird) €30 thereafter.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Abbey Presbyterian Church, Parnell Square N, Rotunda, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Bulk [Ben Wheatley Season]
Description:
When a scientist pushes his experiments with string theory too far - his brane explodes. That's “brane” not “brain” - but that means someone has to sort out the mess he has created. Our hero is sent in - kicking and screaming - to find the heart of the brane and its creator Anton Chambers. Now manifested as a normal townhouse hosted by the enigmatic and multi-dimensional Aclima – his adventure begins. A house where opening a door from one room to another can lead you to other worlds, not just the kitchen. Echoing the fate of our protagonists the very fabric of the film - the sets - and even the cameras used to make it film are deconstructed throughout.

Director Ben Wheatley will be in attendance to take part in a post-screening Q&A.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Brigit x District Muireann Bradley
Description:
An 18-year-old singer and guitarist from Ballybofey, County Donegal, Bradley specialises in acoustic fingerpicking styles drawn from early country, Piedmont and ragtime blues.

Presented as part of Brigit: Dublin City Celebrating Women   [30 Jan – 2 Feb 2026] — a celebration marking renewal, creativity and women’s cultural contribution — this intimate performance offers a rare opportunity to hear an artist whose work honours the past while confidently shaping its future.

Presented in partnership with District Magazine.

Tickets €20 (Early Bird) €30 thereafter.

Name: Christine Bovill: Piaf Revisited
Description:
This is not a tribute show! Christine Bovill established her reputation as one of Europe's finest interpreters of French song with her smash-hit show, Piaf. She now returns with Piaf Revisited and looks back on her remarkable journey with a self-penned fresh take. At the heart of the story is a young Glasgow girl, reluctant in school French until she heard “that song”. Her journey is interwoven with Edith Piaf's life and those incredible songs.

For ages 12+yrs

Description:
Alas this is not an option so as he looks his forties firmly in the face he has hatched a new plan. To live his life vicariously through his seven-year-old son Harvey. What possibly could go wrong?!?

Pull Hard is a brand-new stand-up comedy show that deals with fatherhood and middle age and the lingering disappointment of lost opportunity. The hour is seasoned with tales of GAA matches, failed diets and expensive babysitters. Above all it’s a show about hope and endeavour.

Over 18s only.

All tools, materials, and expert tuition are provided just wear some old clothes (the polishing can get a little messy!) and bring your enthusiasm and creativity.

No experience necessary just come along, create, and enjoy!

Description:

As the musicians move through a series of shifting themes - some dreamy and melodic, some dance and upbeat - the audience is invited to help conduct the musical improvisations.

Name: Aoife Dunne: Good Grief
Description:
A rising force in Irish comedy and storytelling, Aoife blends humour, heartbreak and razor-sharp insight as she explores loss, finds connection and brings to life the strange and hilarious ways we survive what we never thought we’d face.

Mixing stand-up, theatrical storytelling and moments of profound emotional truth, Good Grief has been praised by audiences and critics alike for its warmth, wit and knockout performance. A comedy show like no other, that will make you laugh, cry and leave feeling joyfully alive.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Olympia Theatre, Temple Bar, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Music Network present the Shuteen Erdenebaatar Quartet
Description:
This hard-swinging yet deeply sensitive ensemble is led by award-winning pianist and composer Shuteen Erdenebaatar, hailed by Jazzwise as “one of the most exciting young talents in jazz”.

Erdenebaatar blends classical precision with jazz to create highly distinctive music full of melodic beauty that speaks directly to the heart. With her incredible artistry and vision, this pianist of the moment is rapidly emerging as one of the leading lights of the international jazz scene – a performer whose star continues to rise.

Shuteen Erdenebaatar piano
Simon Comté saxophone
Nils Kugelmann bass
Amir Bresler drums

Presented by Music Network.
Part of Triskel International Jazz Programme 2026.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Trygve Seim & Tord Gustavsen: Inner Chorals
Description:
Two of the most striking lyrical ‘voices’ on the blooming Norwegian jazz scene and ECM Recording artists get together to play J. S. Bach . Trygve and Tord develop unique musical spaces of glowing stillness, lyrical beauty, and far-reaching expressivity while taking chorals written by – or attributed to – Bach as their point of departure.

The concert uses pieces like “Jesu, meine Freude”, “Auf meinen lieben Gott” and “Befiel du deine Wege”, and treats them with equal amounts of respect and freedom. The Programme will also include compositions by both artists, and perhaps some arrangements of Irish traditional tunes and compositions.

Supported by The River Lee, a Doyle Collection hotel.
Part of Triskel International Jazz Programme 2026.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Teeling Whiskey Distillery at Marsh's Library
Description: 'Join us in Ireland’s oldest public library! Come for a collaboration tour of one of Dublin’s hidden treasures ending in a tasting with Teeling Whiskey Distillery, where they will talk you through their award-winning premium whiskey and give you the chance to sample Teeling Whiskey's famous hot whiskey infusion. You will be given an in-depth tour of Marsh’s history throughout the atmospheric bays of the 18th Century library. When you get to the end of the tour, you’ll be treated to a whiskey tasting in the library, served by our friends from the Teeling Whiskey Distillery. To finish off, we’ll get you warmed up downstairs with a hot whiskey. Teeling Whiskey Distillery’s staff will share their knowledge while you relax and warm up.'
Address: Marsh’s Library, St Patrick's Close, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Nils Petter Molvær Group
Description:
Nils Petter Molvær is one of the most prominent performers within an electroacoustic, improvised/composed musical hybrid that has become a genre of its own.

On his new album, Stitches, he teamed up with his quartet and produced an exciting mix of atmospheric sounds and powerful beats.

Nils Petter Molvær trumpet
Jo Berger Myhre bass
Erland Dahlen drums/sampling
Sven Persson sound engineer

Supported by The River Lee, a Doyle Collection hotel
Part of Triskel International Jazz Programme 2026.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Tonnta
Description:
After two sold-out editions, Tonnta, Galway’s festival celebrating Goddess and St. Brigid and Galway as a bilingual city, is back!

Tonnta—created by Gaillimh le Gaeilge—returns from January 29th to February 1st, 2026, inviting everyone—fluent speakers, learners, and first-timers alike—to engage with the Irish language in a fun, inclusive, and welcoming way.

Tonnta will once again fill Galway’s venues, streets, and creative spaces with a vibrant mix of music, art, workshops, talks, and family fun — all delivered bilingually. Expect a weekend brimming with creative energy, from art and craft experiences and storytelling inspired by Brigid, to late-night trad sessions, bilingual performances, and events for children.
Address: Galway City, Galway

Name: Marc Copland Quartet feat Robin Verheyen
Description:
Marc Copland piano
Robin Verheyen saxophones
Stephane Kerecki bass
Fabrice Moreau drums

‘In the hands of this group, music is magic, and Marc Copland is the master magician… Someday is one of the 10 best CDs of the year.’ – allaboutjazz.com, 9 December 2022

It doesn’t seem right to consider pianist Marc Copland (born 1948) an elder statesman, and indeed he is still going strong. Like no other pianist since the mid-1970s, he has produced sounds, and his achievements as a leader are breathtaking: over 40 critically acclaimed CDs, in solos, duos, trios, quartets and quintets.

Supported by The River Lee, a Doyle Collection hotel.
Part of Triskel International Jazz Programme 2026.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Skyline & Dine
Description:
The GAA Museum is delighted to partner with Dublin One Hotel to spread good cheer this Christmas with two new and exclusive ‘Skyline and Dine’ experiences.

Celebrate Winter in Dublin by enjoying a tour of the city from the roof of Croke Park followed by dinner and drinks at Botanical Restaurant in the nearby Dublin One Hotel.

These dining experiences will run on Thursday afternoons (€50pp) and Friday lunchtimes (€44pp) and must be booked through the GAA Museum by emailing tours@crokepark.ie.

The restaurant is located at Dublin One Hotel, a ten-minute walk from Croke Park. Each Skyline tour lasts 90 minutes.
Address: GAA Museum and Stadium Tour, 28 Jones' Rd, Drumcondra, Dublin

Name: EUREKA DAY by Jonathan Spector
Description:
That is, until a health scare shakes the parents and community to its core.As the executive board attempt to navigate the crisis, we see that mumps might not be the only thing that’s contagious.

This Irish premiere of Jonathan Spector’s timely comedy EUREKA DAY has taken London and Broadway by storm. Roy Alexander Weise directs an international ensemble including Motherland’s Philippa Dunne.

‘The perfect play for our age of disagreement.’ New York Times

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: Gate Theatre, Cavendish Row, Parnell Square E, Rotunda, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Top Hat
Description:
When Broadway star Jerry Travers arrives in London to open a new show, he crosses paths with model Dale Tremont whose beauty sleep is rudely interrupted by Jerry tap dancing in the hotel suite above hers. Instantly smitten, Jerry vows to abandon his bachelor life to win her – but the path of true love never does run smooth. Especially since Dale has mistaken Jerry for his hapless producer Horace, who’s trying to avoid the wrath of his formidable wife Madge, and Dale’s own fiery Italian admirer is planning a trip to Venice for her to showcase his couture gowns…

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: Bord Gáis Energy Theatre, Grand Canal Square, Docklands, Dublin

Name: Prima Facie
Description:
Jodie will play Tessa in this gripping, one-person play which takes us to the heart of where emotion and experience collide with the rules of the game. Along with unanimous rave reviews, Jodie won the Olivier Award when she made her theatrical debut in the production at The Harold Pinter Theatre in London and subsequently also won the Tony Award when the play transferred to Broadway. The NTLive and Empire Street Productions live capture has also enjoyed two record breaking theatrical releases.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: The Gaiety Theatre, King St S, Dublin, Dublin

That is, until a health scare shakes the parents and community to its core.As the executive board attempt to navigate the crisis, we see that mumps might not be the only thing that’s contagious.

This Irish premiere of Jonathan Spector’s timely comedy EUREKA DAY has taken London and Broadway by storm. Roy Alexander Weise directs an international ensemble including Motherland’s Philippa Dunne.

‘The perfect play for our age of disagreement.’ New York Times

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: Gate Theatre, Cavendish Row, Parnell Square E, Rotunda, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Prima Facie
Description:
Jodie will play Tessa in this gripping, one-person play which takes us to the heart of where emotion and experience collide with the rules of the game. Along with unanimous rave reviews, Jodie won the Olivier Award when she made her theatrical debut in the production at The Harold Pinter Theatre in London and subsequently also won the Tony Award when the play transferred to Broadway. The NTLive and Empire Street Productions live capture has also enjoyed two record breaking theatrical releases.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: The Gaiety Theatre, King St S, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Bookbinding (Buttonhole Stitch) with Caroline Byrne
Description:
Beginning with simple structures, participants will learn about paper and other materials and tools needed for creating books. By the end of the day, participants will have created a very versatile book with a wide range of possibilities.

Highlights
Learn a new skill
Expert facilitator
Includes
All Materials
Break for lunch
Address: National Print Museum, Haddington Rd, Dublin

Name: James Joyce Walking Tour
Description:
A fun and exciting walking tour of James Joyce's Dublin offered by the James Joyce Centre.

Though Joyce lived most of his life outside of Ireland, Dublin would provide the backdrop for virtually all of his work. On a stroll around the inner city, our guide will explain the real-life inspiration behind some of Joyce’s most celebrated writing, demonstrating just how central the streetscape of the ‘Hibernian metropolis’ was to the author’s life and art. The tour visits such stops as Belvedere College, the Gresham Hotel, the James Joyce Statue, Davy Byrnes, and the National Library.

This is an excellent and fun introduction to Joyce in a bustling part of the city.

Every Thur, Fri & Sat at 11am. Admission to the James Joyce Centre is included.
Address: James Joyce Centre, James Joyce House, 35 N Great George's St, Rotunda, Basement, Dublin

Name: Cavan Arts Festival
Description:
Cavan Arts Festival 2026 celebrates its 9th year.

The Big Top Tent will rise in Con Smith Park for the weekend, magical musical as well as comic experiences will bring us late into the night at The Town Hall Theatre and as ever a few life-changing artistic experiences thrown in for good measure.

Join us as we bring some of the best artists in Ireland today together for one wild, inspiring weekend in Cavan from Thursday 14th to Sunday 17th May 2026.

Proudly shortlisted for Best Irish Festival 2024, as part of the The LAMA All-Ireland Community and Council Awards, funded by Arts Council and Cavan County Council

Follow us on Instagram and Facebook @cavanartsfestival and our website cavanartsfestival.ie for all updates and full information.
Address: Cavan Town Hall, Town Hall St, Keadew, Cavan

Name: Killeshandra Camino Calling
Description:
The route winds through rolling drumlins, quiet woodlands, and sparkling lakes that catch the sunlight. 

The Camino is not a race — its an invitation to pause, notice, and connect with the landscape. It honours Killeshandra’s unique harmony of water and land, where drumlins rise softly and lakes reflect the magic.
  
Tastes of the region—fresh treats, homemade flavours, and Cavan’s proud food culture—enhance the journey, offering nourishment as well as a deeper connection to the community and the land that sustains it.

Returning to the Community Hall, a well-deserved meal await,  Limited number of tickets available.   Follow us on Facebook - Killeshandra Camino Calling for updates and links to tickets.
Address: Killeshandra Community Hall, Railway Road, Killashandra, Cavan

Name: Rockathon
Description:
Get ready for three epic days of live music, camping and connection as Rockathon 2026 roars into Fairyhouse Racecourse for the June Bank Holiday Weekend.

The ultimate rock music festival, packed with world-class acts celebrating legends like AC/DC, Guns ‘N’ Roses, Queen, Metallica, Iron Maiden, Pearl Jam, Nirvana and more — plus brilliant original Irish rock talent, food, bars, and family fun. 

From the buzz of the crowd to the freedom of camping under the stars, Rockathon is more than a festival — it’s a community of music lovers of every age and background. Whether you come for the day or stay for the weekend, you’ll find great people, great energy, and unforgettable memories!

3 Days. 3 Stages. 3 Nights Camping. May 29th - 31st 2026
Address: Fairyhouse Racecourse, Fairyhouse Rd, Ballybin, Ratoath, Meath

Name: Solas Quartet: Spring String Quartet Series 2026
Description:
Our second Lombardini quartet of the series opens this lovely programme performed by the Solas Quartet. An elegant and conversational first movement gives way to a playful and virtuosic finale.

Milo McCarthy’s new string quartet, written for the Solas Quartet, is inspired by imagined sights and sounds from ancient Ireland and brings together elements of traditional Irish and contemporary classical music.

Purcell’s F major Fantasia from 1680 was written for four viols but also works beautifully for string quartet and will set you up perfectly for Mendelssohn’s magnificent E flat major quartet.

Solas Quartet
Katherine Hunka, violin
Marja Gaynor, violin
Cian Ó Dúill, viola
Aoife Nic Athlaoich, cello
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Though Joyce lived most of his life outside of Ireland, Dublin would provide the backdrop for virtually all of his work. On a stroll around the inner city, our guide will explain the real-life inspiration behind some of Joyce’s most celebrated writing, demonstrating just how central the streetscape of the ‘Hibernian metropolis’ was to the author’s life and art. The tour visits such stops as Belvedere College, the Gresham Hotel, the James Joyce Statue, Davy Byrnes, and the National Library.

This is an excellent and fun introduction to Joyce in a bustling part of the city.

Every Thur, Fri & Sat at 11am. Admission to the James Joyce Centre is included.
Address: James Joyce Centre, James Joyce House, 35 N Great George's St, Rotunda, Basement, Dublin

Name: The Witch of Konotop
Description:
The theatrical sensation "The Witch of Konotop" embarks on its Europe 2026 tour, it starts from Lodz. This unique production by Ivan Uryvskyi, inspired by the novella by Hryhorii Kvitka-Osnovianenko, blends Ukrainian folklore, a mystical atmosphere, and contemporary meaning. The play challenges stereotypes, exploring themes of female power, the battle between Light and Darkness, and faith in humanity. With minimalist scenography, powerful performances, and an eerie sound design, it offers a truly immersive experience. Don’t miss the chance to witness the most anticipated theatrical event of the year!

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: The Convention Centre Dublin, Spencer Dock, N Wall Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Midwinter Festival: The Quartet Extends
Description: The string quartet is no stranger to Galway audiences, in no small part thanks to the tireless work of the ConTempo Quartet. With its 23rd Midwinter Festival running at the Town Hall Theatre from 23rd to 25th of January, Music for Galway seeks to celebrate the versatility of the string quartet and to ‘extend it’ by pairing it up with a variety of different musicians to perform quartets, quintets, sextets and even an octet to finish. A celebration of the great musicians Ireland has to offer, the festival presents a clarinettist, a soprano, a pianist, an uilleann piper and two string quartets. MfG also invites its young Cellissimo Ambassador, Spanish cellist Margarita Guerrero Martínez, to participate in one of the concerts.
Address: Town Hall Theatre, 1 Courthouse Square, Galway

Name: The Irish Consort
Description:
Join Cruit Éireann|Harp Ireland in the beautiful Pepper Canister Church for a unique evening with The Irish Consort, directed by Siobhán Armstrong. This concert explores the role of the early Irish harp at the 17th century English royal court and features the Irish premiere of William Lawes' harp consorts performed on a brass wire strung early Irish harp.

Performers:Bojan Čičić (violin) Reiko Ichise (bass viol) Elizabeth Kenny (theorbo) Siobhán Armstrong (early Irish harp)
Address: Pepper Canister Church, 7 Mount St Cres, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Modern Calligraphy Workshop with Sarah O'Dea
Description: If you are a lover of lettering or want to learn a new skill, we would love you to join us in learning the basic techniques of modern calligraphy. You’ll start by learning how to use your pen holder and nib and what materials work best. Then we will get working on exercises to practice making shapes and strokes, and then move on to letter forms. By the end of the class, you will have started to develop your own style with plenty of time to practice and one-to-one help when needed.
Address: National Print Museum, Haddington Rd, Dublin

Name: Drop-In: The Saints, Scholars, and Scribes of Ireland! Early Medieval Handling Workshop
Description:
Celebrate St. Brigid’s Weekend in our Learning Resource Room and explore life in Early Medieval Ireland. 

Handle replicas of jet crosses, leather shoes, and brooches, enjoy relaxing colouring activities, and browse books on archaeology. 

Afterwards, visit the Museum galleries, The Treasury exhibition, and Glendalough: Power, Prayer and Pilgrimage. 

No booking required. Located on the First Floor (not wheelchair accessible). Families only; children must be supervised by an adult.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street, 35A Kildare St, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Skyline & Dine
Description:
The GAA Museum is delighted to partner with Dublin One Hotel to spread good cheer this Christmas with two new and exclusive ‘Skyline and Dine’ experiences.

Celebrate Winter in Dublin by enjoying a tour of the city from the roof of Croke Park followed by dinner and drinks at Botanical Restaurant in the nearby Dublin One Hotel.

These dining experiences will run on Thursday afternoons (€50pp) and Friday lunchtimes (€44pp) and must be booked through the GAA Museum by emailing tours@crokepark.ie.

The restaurant is located at Dublin One Hotel, a ten-minute walk from Croke Park. Each Skyline tour lasts 90 minutes.
Address: GAA Museum and Stadium Tour, 28 Jones' Rd, Drumcondra, Dublin

Name: Sugar Club Harp Sessions | Michael Rooney & Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh
Description:
Michael Rooney and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh open Cruit Éireann|Harp Ireland's 2026 Sugar Club Harp Sessions. Both artists are leading figures in Irish traditional music and bring a distinctive blend of creativity, musicianship and deep cultural knowledge to the stage. Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh is a renowned sean nós singer from Corca Dhuibhne and a multi award winning artist celebrated for her expressive and powerful performances. Michael Rooney is an influential harper and composer whose innovative style and large scale ensemble works have become central to the modern harp repertoire.

Venue: Sugar Club, 8 Leeson Street Lower, Saint Kevin's, Dublin, D02 ET97
Date: Saturday 28 February 2026
Doors: 7:30pm Concert: 8:00pm
Address: The Sugar Club, 8 Leeson Street Lower, Saint Kevin's, Dublin

Name: Woodtype Poster Printing Workshop
Description: During this workshop with a letterpress/graphic artist, participants will design, typeset, and produce a unique letterpress printed poster. The workshop will utilize the museum’s collection of wood types. It will be a hands-on experience, covering the fundamentals of letterpress printing techniques and some of the craft’s history and background. Beautiful results can be achieved by playing with the layout, ink effects, and the texture of the wood type. All stages including the inking will be done by hand… think big type, short words, and interesting textures.
Address: National Print Museum, Haddington Rd, Dublin

Name: Monoprinting Workshop with Susan LEen
Description:
This one day workshop offers an introduction to monoprinting using a printing press. Monoprint is a versatile printmaking technique, combining inked surfaces, a range of textures and mark-making to create one-off prints. Each print is unique and cannot be repeated, offering an introduction to a fun and expressive way of printmaking.

The course will cover:

inking up plates and colour mixing
making stencils to create shapes to print with
incorporating found textures and patterns as well as pieces from the Museum’s collection
creating a small number of unique prints using the different techniques learned throughout the day
Address: National Print Museum, Haddington Rd, Dublin

Name: Tonnta
Description:
After two sold-out editions, Tonnta, Galway’s festival celebrating Goddess and St. Brigid and Galway as a bilingual city, is back!

Tonnta—created by Gaillimh le Gaeilge—returns from January 29th to February 1st, 2026, inviting everyone—fluent speakers, learners, and first-timers alike—to engage with the Irish language in a fun, inclusive, and welcoming way.

Tonnta will once again fill Galway’s venues, streets, and creative spaces with a vibrant mix of music, art, workshops, talks, and family fun — all delivered bilingually. Expect a weekend brimming with creative energy, from art and craft experiences and storytelling inspired by Brigid, to late-night trad sessions, bilingual performances, and events for children.
Address: Galway City, Galway

Name: Dún Laoghaire Walking Tours, The Dublin Riviera
Description:
The Dublin Riviera is a guided walk back in time to a golden era where local Dún Laoghaire residents and visitors wined and dined in the yacht clubs, restaurants and hotels along the waterfront, and danced in the Pavilion, the ballrooms and the nightclubs. The walk will provide an insight into the symbiotic relationship between the harbour and the town and how this helped Dún Laoghaire to evolve into an elegant and glamorous centre of hospitality, entertainment and luxurious bathing, a “Dublin Riviera”.

Highlights:
Waterfront Yacht Clubs and the Royal Visits
The Pavilion and Town Hall
Royal Marine Hotel and Gardens
Haddington House Hotel
Former Seafront hotels on Victoria and Marine Terrace
Dún Laoghaire Baths (finish)
Venue Name: Meeting Place: Harbour Plaza, Dún Laoghaire
Address: Harbour Plaza, Harbour Rd, Dún Laoghaire, Dublin

Name: Rebel Rising Tour
Description:
This tour tells the story of the key events which shaped modern Irish history including the 1916 Easter Rising, the Irish War of Independence, the Anglo-Irish Treaty, the Irish Civil War, and the
Troubles, including the resulting peace process in Northern Ireland and the signing of the Good Friday Agreement in 1998.

The Rebel Rising tour is approximately one hour in duration. Afterwards visitors are also welcome to enjoy the centrepiece film and the museum at their own leisure.

Available: Monday, Thursday & Saturday at 2.30 pm (please note that we are closed on public holidays)

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: GPO Museum, O'Connell Street Lower, North City, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Conal Gallen – The Last Laugh Tour
Description:
From mad family stories to classic Irish observations, Conal delivers the kind of comedy only he can. It’s cheeky, it’s wild — and it’s pure craic from start to finish.

Book your seats now – you’ll be laughing all the way home!

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Before
Description:
Pontius is inside, trying to choose a gift for his estranged daughter, whom he hasn’t seen for almost 20 years. He will meet her in an hour. This father’s journey is both beautiful and strange, from the isolation of his Midlands home, to the madness of O’Connell Street. Some folk are impossible to buy for…​

Ages 14+ / Run time: 80mins, no interval.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The Man In The Woman’s Shoes
Description:
Just outside of town, cobbler Pat Farnon lives on his own, contentedly aging in the cottage in which he was reared. Join him as he walks the five miles into his “metropolis,” populated with no-necked water diviners, sporting savants, loudmouths, and preachers.

Created with the support of the Sligo County Council Arts Service and The Hawk’s Well Theatre, Sligo as part of the Bealtaine Festival for Creativity in older people, The Man In The Woman’s Shoes has toured extensively in Ireland since 2012. It has played twice in NYC at the Irish Arts Centre in Manhattan. It ran for three weeks at The Tricycle Theatre, London in 2016 to full houses.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: CISEACH
Description:
This stirring new work by Catherine Young Dance (Floating on a Dead Sea and State of Exception) urges us to re-member, to re-connect with our past, with nature and with each other. Collecting fragments of native wisdoms, myths and kinships, CISEACH builds the wattled causeway, the ciseach, to guide us to solid ground.

This pulsating show weaves together a tapestry of dance, rhythm, live music and voice, and seeks to rekindle our broken relationship with the land.

After an incredible response with instant standing ovations from audiences in Longford and Dublin last year, we are thrilled to have CISEACH return.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Padraig Jack
Description:
Padraig Jack returns to Backstage's Canal Studio after last year’s standout show. The bilingual singer-songwriter from the Aran Islands blends Celtic, folk, and contemporary influences, earning acclaim for his RTE Folk Awards–nominated hit “Making Sand,” which topped the RTE Radio 1 Airplay Chart for four weeks. 

Following the release of his second album "These Shores", he performed for 4,000 fans at Galway International Arts Festival and kicked off 2025 with sold-out shows and major festival debuts across Ireland and internationally.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Conversations on a Homecoming
Description:
Over a long drinking session in an East Galway pub, a group of friends gather together after an absence of 10 years. Faced with the changes the years have wrought, they begin the long process of self-discovery.

Tickets €17 / €15

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Kevin Logue & His Band
Description:
Kevin Logue, a country singer-songwriter from Bawnboy, Co. Cavan, channels the true spirit of classic country music. Influenced by legends like John Prine and Kris Kristofferson, he’s known for honest storytelling and a warm, roots-driven sound. 

His songs and performances have taken him across Ireland and beyond, from the Belfast Nashville Songwriters Festival to supporting major artists, with his work even reaching the Grand Ole Opry and film. Down-to-earth and dedicated, he now brings a full band to perform tracks from his new album "Without Compromise," alongside favourites from his rich catalogue.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Over a long drinking session in an East Galway pub, a group of friends gather together after an absence of 10 years. Faced with the changes the years have wrought, they begin the long process of self-discovery.

Name: The Witch Who Couldn’t Sleep
Description:
Being a witch is super exciting. There’s always so much to do and so much fun to be had. And maybe that’s why Natascha The Witch can’t sleep…

Can anyone, or anything, help her drift off?

This charming interactive musical blends storytelling, songs, and animation to create a magical experience for children who will love getting to join in, shout out, and help shape the story as it unfolds.

Based on a story from Steven Lee’s best selling “How The Koala Learnt To Hug and Other Australian Fairytales”, The Witch Who Couldn’t Sleep is a gentle, heartwarming show designed to help children develop literacy, imagination and storytelling skills whilst exploring key PSHE themes.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Conversations on a Homecoming
Description:
Over a long drinking session in an East Galway pub, a group of friends gather together after an absence of 10 years. Faced with the changes the years have wrought, they begin the long process of self-discovery.

Tickets €17 / €15

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Red Line Comedy Club
Description:
Red Line Comedy is bringing the Wild Galway Comedy Club Experience to The Backstage in Longford as Part of their Irish and European Tour. 

The Red Line lads are travelling Europe. Fresh off the boat from Prague and Amsterdam shows, they’re coming home!!(well some of them are) and they have brought trouble!!

Your Host for the night is Mostrim/Longford grown International Comedian & Red Line co-founder Enda Whitney.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Still, We Gather
Description:
Galway Arts Centre is delighted to announce our final exhibition of the year, 'Still, We Gather', bringing together artists working across a wide range of mediums to celebrate the social and political potential of coming together, recognising gathering as a practice of care, joy, protest and collective imagination. 

Developed through an open call, the exhibition brings together a constellation of contemporary Irish artists whose practices emerge from layered geographies and diverse lived experiences and whose works explore how forms of connection and resistance are sustained and reimagined across borders, cultures and times.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Tonnta
Description:
After two sold-out editions, Tonnta, Galway’s festival celebrating Goddess and St. Brigid and Galway as a bilingual city, is back!

Tonnta—created by Gaillimh le Gaeilge—returns from January 29th to February 1st, 2026, inviting everyone—fluent speakers, learners, and first-timers alike—to engage with the Irish language in a fun, inclusive, and welcoming way.

Tonnta will once again fill Galway’s venues, streets, and creative spaces with a vibrant mix of music, art, workshops, talks, and family fun — all delivered bilingually. Expect a weekend brimming with creative energy, from art and craft experiences and storytelling inspired by Brigid, to late-night trad sessions, bilingual performances, and events for children.
Address: Galway City, Galway

Name: Declan Nerney
Description:
Irish country music icon Declan Nerney has been inducted into the Irish Country Music Hall of Fame, marking a major milestone in his four-decade career. Born in Drumlish, he rose from the showband scene to national fame with hits like “The Marquee in Drumlish,” “Stop the World,” and “Anna from Fermanagh.” A beloved performer and storyteller, Declan remains a staple of Ireland’s music scene, continuing to headline major venues and festivals alongside his band and special guests.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The Kings of the Kilburn High Road
Description:
In the mid ’70s a group of young men left their homes in the west of Ireland, took the boat out of Dublin bay and sailed across the sea to England in the hope of making their fortunes and returning home.

25 years later, only one – Jackie Flavin, makes it home in a coffin.

The Kings of the Kilburn High Road takes place on the day that the winners and losers of the group meet up to drink to Jackie’s memory and look at their lives, lost dreams and their place in the new Ireland.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Little Folk – On The Road
Description:
Join him for a foot stomping, hand clapping, and jumping good time.

Featuring his award winning children’s music, catchy kids tunes and proper sing-a-long songs, it’s super fun for for the whole family.  The fun doesn’t stop when the show ends, because each child gets a little musical goody bag to use during the show and then take some music home.

Kyle’s been writing and performing children’s music for over 10 years. He’s toured Ireland and abroad bringing his entertaining and engaging show to thousands of families.

Suitable for 2-7yr olds and their adults.

Name: Dún Laoghaire Walking Tours, The Dublin Riviera
Description:
The Dublin Riviera is a guided walk back in time to a golden era where local Dún Laoghaire residents and visitors wined and dined in the yacht clubs, restaurants and hotels along the waterfront, and danced in the Pavilion, the ballrooms and the nightclubs. The walk will provide an insight into the symbiotic relationship between the harbour and the town and how this helped Dún Laoghaire to evolve into an elegant and glamorous centre of hospitality, entertainment and luxurious bathing, a “Dublin Riviera”.

Highlights:
Waterfront Yacht Clubs and the Royal Visits
The Pavilion and Town Hall
Royal Marine Hotel and Gardens
Haddington House Hotel
Former Seafront hotels on Victoria and Marine Terrace
Dún Laoghaire Baths (finish)
Venue Name: Meeting Place: Harbour Plaza, Dún Laoghaire
Address: Harbour Plaza, Harbour Rd, Dún Laoghaire, Dublin

Name: Antarctica
Description:
Saturday 14th Feb @ 12pm & 2pm

This theatrical experience brings the audience on an immersive sensory expedition through the icy world of Antarctica! The performance, told through music and movement, is inspired by Shackleton’s Antarctic expedition which was struck by disaster when his ship, Endurance, became trapped in the ice and ultimately sank.
Join our crew as they set off on a unique adventure across ice and water, overcoming harsh conditions and unknown perils. It is a journey of survival, with teamwork and comradery becoming much more important than their original goal. Will everyone make it back safely? 

Schools: Tue 10 Feb - Sat 14 Feb @ 10:30am, 12.15pm & 2pm

Original commissioned by Baboró International Arts Festival for Children.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Tom Crean – Antarctic Explorer
Description:
Tom Crean, the intrepid Antarctic explorer and one of Ireland’s unsung heroes, is brought to life in this dramatic and humorous solo performance by Aidan Dooley.

Hear the riveting true stories of Crean’s Antarctic explorations as one of the few men to serve with both Scott and Shackleton and survive the three famous expeditions: Discovery, (1901–1904); Terra Nova (1910–1913); and Endurance (1914–1916).

Crean’s story is a testament of human fortitude against all the elements of the Antarctic. His 36 mile solitary trek to base camp during the Terra Nova expedition to rescue his comrades Teddy Evans and William Lashly has been described by Antarctic historians, as “the finest feat of individual heroism from the entire age of exploration”.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Marc Roberts – A Tribute to the Music of John Denver
Description:
Marc Roberts presents his acclaimed, award-winning show "A Tribute To The Music of John Denver", a multimedia celebration of Denver’s life and songs. Featuring classics like “Annie’s Song,” “Take Me Home Country Roads,” and “Calypso,” Marc pairs powerful performances with immersive storytelling. A platinum-selling artist and Eurovision runner-up, he has released multiple albums, written with top songwriters, and toured the show worldwide, selling out major venues including Dublin’s National Concert Hall.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Lumiere Quartet: Spring String Quartet Series 2026
Description:
The Lumiere Quartet explores the expressive, authentic soundworld created by playing with classical bows on gut strings rather than today’s more practical steel strings. For this tour they present three wonderful classical quartets alongside a short work by Donnacha Dennehy.

Lumiere Quartet
Ioana Petcu-Colan, violin
Elaine Clark, violin
Nathan Sherman, viola
Carina Drury, cello

Programme
Haydn – String Quartet in D minor Op.76 No.2 Fifths [1797]
Donnacha Dennehy – Pushpulling [2007]
Beethoven – String Quartet in F major Op.18 No.1 [1798-1800]

Name: Fiachra Garvey - Piano
Description:
PROGRAMME: 
Ludwig van Beethoven - Sonata no. 30 in E major, op. 109
George Gershwin - Rhapsody in Blue
Sergei Rachmaninov - Sonata no. 1 in D minor, op. 28

Beethoven's late E major sonata Op. 109 is a work of sheer beauty, featuring some of the composer's most heartbreakingly poignant melodies. Gerhswin's Rhapsody in Blue is an audience favourite the world over and an ode to New York. Rachmaninov's Sonata No 1 in D minor is a work which strikes fear into many pianists, due to its colossal scale, technical difficulty and emotional demands. Three works which compliment each other and provide the listener with the full spectrum of the piano's incredible possibilities!
Address: Station Rd, Thomastown, Kilkenny

Name: A Celebration of the Stories of Eamon Kelly
Description:
Pakie O’Callaghan’s A Celebration of The Stories of Eamon Kelly honours the work of Irelands greatest storyteller. O’Callaghan takes Kelly’s best known stories including “The mirror”, “The looking glass” and “The woman who married the Blacksmith” and intersperses them with the haunting music of Sliabh Luachra, beautifully played by Aileen Collins and Catherine Frost to create an evening of spellbinding theatre. 

The stories capture the life, times and happenings of rural Ireland in more innocent times and will evoke evoke memories of a world that moved at its own pace dictated only by the weather and the seasons. O’Callaghan has been described by the Sunday Times as “Peerless at what he does”.
Address: Civic Theatre, Parthalán Place, Belgard Rd, Tallaght, Dublin

Name: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
Description:
No Drama Theatre presents The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time.

The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time tells the story of Christopher John Francis Boone, who is fifteen years old. He stands besides Mrs Shears’ dead dog, which has been speared with a garden fork, and Christopher is under suspicion. He records each fact in a book he is writing to solve the mystery of who killed Wellington. He has an extraordinary brain, however everyday life presents some barriers. He has never ventured alone beyond the end of his road, he detests being touched and distrusts strangers. But his detective work, forbidden by his father, takes him on a frightening journey that upturns his world.

Age suitability: 13+
Address: Smock Alley Theatre, 1662, 6-7 Exchange Street Lower, Temple Bar, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Paddy Critchley—Painter / Painter
Description:
Painter / Painter is an exhibition and performance by Paddy Critchley. In this exhibition, Paddy reflects on recent explorations within his work. This exhibition brings together work that reacts to his lived experience of contemporary folk culture in Ireland, along with references to his work as a painter and decorator. Paddy’s paintings work as a body, each one in dialogue with another. From portraits of friends and flowers to words and meditations, Paddy uses paint as his way of understanding the world.  Paddy’s love of Irish ballads and songs, especially the work of Brendan and Dominic Behan, underpins the work in this exhibition.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Pallas Projects/Studios, 115-116, The Coombe, The Liberties, Dublin

‘Hommage à Eberhard Feltz based on Beckett’s “Roundelay” for Maya Homburger and Barry Guy
Address: The Parade Tower, The Parade, Dukesmeadows, Kilkenny

Name: Paddy Critchley—Painter / Painter
Description:
Painter / Painter is an exhibition and performance by Paddy Critchley. In this exhibition, Paddy reflects on recent explorations within his work. This exhibition brings together work that reacts to his lived experience of contemporary folk culture in Ireland, along with references to his work as a painter and decorator. Paddy’s paintings work as a body, each one in dialogue with another. From portraits of friends and flowers to words and meditations, Paddy uses paint as his way of understanding the world.  Paddy’s love of Irish ballads and songs, especially the work of Brendan and Dominic Behan, underpins the work in this exhibition.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Pallas Projects/Studios, 115-116, The Coombe, The Liberties, Dublin

Name: A Celebration of the Stories of Eamon Kelly
Description:
Pakie O’Callaghan’s A Celebration of The Stories of Eamon Kelly honours the work of Irelands greatest storyteller. O’Callaghan takes Kelly’s best known stories including “The mirror”, “The looking glass” and “The woman who married the Blacksmith” and intersperses them with the haunting music of Sliabh Luachra, beautifully played by Aileen Collins and Catherine Frost to create an evening of spellbinding theatre. 

The stories capture the life, times and happenings of rural Ireland in more innocent times and will evoke evoke memories of a world that moved at its own pace dictated only by the weather and the seasons. O’Callaghan has been described by the Sunday Times as “Peerless at what he does”.
Address: Civic Theatre, Parthalán Place, Belgard Rd, Tallaght, Dublin

Name: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
Description:
No Drama Theatre presents The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time.

The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time tells the story of Christopher John Francis Boone, who is fifteen years old. He stands besides Mrs Shears’ dead dog, which has been speared with a garden fork, and Christopher is under suspicion. He records each fact in a book he is writing to solve the mystery of who killed Wellington. He has an extraordinary brain, however everyday life presents some barriers. He has never ventured alone beyond the end of his road, he detests being touched and distrusts strangers. But his detective work, forbidden by his father, takes him on a frightening journey that upturns his world.

Age suitability: 13+
Address: Smock Alley Theatre, 1662, 6-7 Exchange Street Lower, Temple Bar, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Síomha Callanan: Residency
Description:
Síomha Callanan’s work is inherently linked to her surroundings and, having lived in Cork City, much of her work to date consists of various projects and explorations of the city centre and its peripheries. For this residency and exhibition she has a specific project in mind. Her aim is to walk every street on the island on which Cork City centre stands and take photos of what she encounters.

She'll use the Triskel Sample Project Space as a base from which to go out into the city and make the work. She'll use the space itself to research and plan her walks, to make notes and document her explorations. She'll also use the space to develop these ideas as well as to test out various ways of presenting the work for the culminating exhibition.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Ardgillan Castle Self Guided Tour, January
Description:
Discover the charm of Ardgillan Castle at your own pace with a self-guided tour, designed to offer a flexible and enriching experience. Learn about the fascinating history through informative displays and exhibits throughout the house. Explore the impressive Drawing Room, where the Taylor Family once socialised. Marvel at the decorative woodwork of the Dining Room that evokes the grandeur of formal dining. Lose yourself in the charm of the library, lined with bookshelves filled with literary treasures.

By contrast, visitors can explore the working kitchens, the heart of the house, providing a fascinating glimpse into the culinary preparations and daily operations that sustained the estate during its heyday.
Address: Ardgillan Demesne, Dublin

Name: Síomha Callanan: Residency
Description:
Síomha Callanan’s work is inherently linked to her surroundings and, having lived in Cork City, much of her work to date consists of various projects and explorations of the city centre and its peripheries. For this residency and exhibition she has a specific project in mind. Her aim is to walk every street on the island on which Cork City centre stands and take photos of what she encounters.

She'll use the Triskel Sample Project Space as a base from which to go out into the city and make the work. She'll use the space itself to research and plan her walks, to make notes and document her explorations. She'll also use the space to develop these ideas as well as to test out various ways of presenting the work for the culminating exhibition.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Elinor O’Donovan: Winter Sun
Description:
Winter Sun is a moving-image work by Cork artist Elinor O’Donovan.

The work is a preserved piece of a warm Cork summer, saved for the months when it is most needed. Taking as its inspiration the iconic view from the top of Patrick’s Hill at Bells Field, the video work is a looping film depicting Corkonians sharing a moment of togetherness, connected by their mutual appreciation for a setting summer sun.

Projection-Mapping: Lightscape Studios
3D Artist: Bassam Issa Al-Sabah
Cinematography: Jack Desmond

Part of Island City – Cork’s Urban Sculpture Trail.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Síomha Callanan’s work is inherently linked to her surroundings and, having lived in Cork City, much of her work to date consists of various projects and explorations of the city centre and its peripheries. For this residency and exhibition she has a specific project in mind. Her aim is to walk every street on the island on which Cork City centre stands and take photos of what she encounters.

She'll use the Triskel Sample Project Space as a base from which to go out into the city and make the work. She'll use the space itself to research and plan her walks, to make notes and document her explorations. She'll also use the space to develop these ideas as well as to test out various ways of presenting the work for the culminating exhibition.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Elinor O’Donovan: Winter Sun
Description:
Winter Sun is a moving-image work by Cork artist Elinor O’Donovan.

The work is a preserved piece of a warm Cork summer, saved for the months when it is most needed. Taking as its inspiration the iconic view from the top of Patrick’s Hill at Bells Field, the video work is a looping film depicting Corkonians sharing a moment of togetherness, connected by their mutual appreciation for a setting summer sun.

Projection-Mapping: Lightscape Studios
3D Artist: Bassam Issa Al-Sabah
Cinematography: Jack Desmond

Part of Island City – Cork’s Urban Sculpture Trail.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Ardgillan Castle Self Guided Tour, January
Description:
Discover the charm of Ardgillan Castle at your own pace with a self-guided tour, designed to offer a flexible and enriching experience. Learn about the fascinating history through informative displays and exhibits throughout the house. Explore the impressive Drawing Room, where the Taylor Family once socialised. Marvel at the decorative woodwork of the Dining Room that evokes the grandeur of formal dining. Lose yourself in the charm of the library, lined with bookshelves filled with literary treasures.

By contrast, visitors can explore the working kitchens, the heart of the house, providing a fascinating glimpse into the culinary preparations and daily operations that sustained the estate during its heyday.
Address: Ardgillan Demesne, Dublin

Name: Adam Kay: This is Going to Hurt
Description:
BAFTA-winning comedian Adam Kay shares his uniquely hilarious diaries as a junior doctor in this “electrifying” (Guardian) evening of stand-up. Over 350,000 tickets sold. Based on the multi-million selling book and multi-award-winning TV series.

"Intersperses horror stories from the NHS frontline with a catalogue of sublimely silly spoof songs, and some blissfully brilliant wordplay" ★★★★★ Mail on Sunday

"Hilarious and heartbreaking" Charlie Brooker

Warning: Contains medical themes and stories that some may find distressing.

Age Rating: 16+
Address: VISUAL Carlow, Old Dublin Rd, Carlow

Name: ROBIN AND DAWN  By Michael Hilliard Mulcahy
Description:
Robin and Dawn is the story of a marriage.

Musician, Robin journeys home late from gigs each night. He drives through the wild terrain of the West Kerry mountains, arriving home as dawn is breaking, and Dawn his wife, is beginning her day. After twenty years of marriage, they are ships passing in the night. But when Dawn awakes one morning to find Robin deeply shaken, they are left with no choice but to communicate. 

Funny, moving and spiritual, Robin and Dawn is a beautiful and lyrical new play from the pen of Michael Hilliard Mulcahy.

 60 mins

Age: 14+

Mon-Sat at 1pm
Tickets: Low-price Mon/Tues €10, Wed/Thurs €12, Fri/Sat €15.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: Bewleys Cafe Theatre, Second Floor, Bewley's Cafe, 78-79, Grafton Street, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Reeling in the Showband Years
Description:
Reeling In the Showband Years has been a smash hit for the past 15 years, and we are delighted to say it returns once again to Carlow VISUAL in January 2026 to celebrate it’s amazing 16th year.

And what a line-up it is again in 2026!

RTÉ’s Ronan Collins is Host and Compere.

Featuring performances by:

Gina From The Champions.

Keith and Lorraine McDonald.

Shaun O’Dowd.

Dave Lawlor.

The Conquerors Showband.
Address: VISUAL Carlow, Old Dublin Rd, Carlow

Name: Carlow Little Theatre Society presents Eden by Eugene O'Brien
Description:
Eden is the powerful story of an ordinary relationship on an extraordinary weekend. Billy and Breda are approaching their 10th wedding anniversary. Breda is determined that this will be the weekend that saves their marriage. But Billy is more concerned with Imelda, the beautiful young woman he has his eye on. From the writer of hit RTÉ drama Pure Mule, Eden is a story of stale love and fresh lust in the Irish midlands. Their debut single Bright & Bold is available now, with a full album set for release on 30 January 2026.

In the years since, both have developed singular careers: Noonan through Bell X1 and other projects Printer Clips and HousePlants (with electronic producer Daithí), and Crosby via acclaimed film and television scores and his solo piano and ambient work.
