Name: Ewan McVicar
Description:
Fresh from a monumental summer that saw him headline Creamfields’ HALO stage and deliver a show-stopping final set at Electric Picnic, DJ Mag’s Best British DJ is bringing his unstoppable energy to one of Ireland’s most legendary stages.

From his platinum-selling anthem ‘Tell Me Something Good’ to his club favourites ‘Heather Park’, and ‘Groove Thang’ McVicar continues to blur the lines between underground credibility and main-stage power. His sets are renowned for their intensity, technical mastery, and connection with the crowd - an energy that’s made him one of the most in-demand names in global dance music.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: 3Arena, N Wall Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Top Hat
Description:
When Broadway star Jerry Travers arrives in London to open a new show, he crosses paths with model Dale Tremont whose beauty sleep is rudely interrupted by Jerry tap dancing in the hotel suite above hers. Instantly smitten, Jerry vows to abandon his bachelor life to win her – but the path of true love never does run smooth. Especially since Dale has mistaken Jerry for his hapless producer Horace, who’s trying to avoid the wrath of his formidable wife Madge, and Dale’s own fiery Italian admirer is planning a trip to Venice for her to showcase his couture gowns…

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: Bord Gáis Energy Theatre, Grand Canal Square, Docklands, Dublin

Name: TOGETHERNESS
Description:
With a lead role in the opera Lady Gregory in America, numerous sold out performances and her first tour with Paul Cibis under her belt, 2025 has been a wonderful year for Helen.

The programme on the theme of love includes:
✨ Frauenliebe und -leben (a woman's love and life). 
✨ Greek folksongs (Ravel)
✨ Irish songs by Ina Boyle, Thomas Moore and Criostóir Ó Loingsigh
✨ Musical theatre numbers

TOGETHERNESS was originally entitled Zweisamkeit (Two Togetherness) and was performed by the duo in Germany as part of Zeitgeist Irland 24, a season of Irish arts events curated by Culture Ireland and the Embassy of Ireland, Germany. The album, recorded in Berlin, launched on Parma Recordings Navona Records in September 2025.
Address: St Columba's Church of Ireland, Bindon St, Lifford, Ennis, Clare

Name: Conor Moore Me, Myself and Them!
Description:
Known for his razor-sharp takes on GAA legends and his pitch-perfect impressions of politicians, Conor delivers a new show packed with personality — many personalities in fact! Mary Lou, Micheal, Loughnane, Brolly, Tommy Tiernan and many, many more feature throughout!

Whether it's the chaos of national and international politics or the ever-evolving madness of the new rules in the GAA, nothing is off-limits. Me, myself and them is a show not to be missed! 

Strictly over 16's

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Kevin Burke, Dermot Byrne, Noriana Kennedy & Jim Murray
Description:
Music Network presents another unmissable collaboration featuring four extraordinary artists, accordion maestro Dermot Byrne, legendary fiddler and member of the iconic Bothy Band Kevin Burke, acclaimed singer-songwriter Noriana Kennedy (The Whileaways), and lauded guitarist Jim Murray come together for an exceptional nationwide tour in early 2026. 

This specially curated ensemble reflects the spirit of Music Network’s mission to make high-quality live music accessible to communities throughout Ireland. Audiences can look forward to an evening of remarkable musicianship, rich storytelling and joyful collaboration–in a fitting celebration of Music Network’s four decades of supporting and showcasing Ireland’s most gifted performers.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Imelda May Raised on Songs & Stories
Description:
Born and raised in The Liberties area of Dublin, Imelda May has become one of Ireland’s most celebrated female artists as a singer, songwriter, actor, producer, poet, presenter and storyteller. A dynamic live performer with her unique blend of rockabilly, jazz, rock and blues she has toured across the world and performed with legends such as Jeff Beck, Lou Reed, Bono, Noel Gallagher, Jeff Goldblum & Ronnie Wood. This year she joined Jools Holland on a string of summer shows.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: The Muddy River Band The Songs of Bob Dylan, Neil Young and Van Morrison
Description:
The Muddy River Band presents: The Songs of Bob Dylan, Neil Young and Van Morrison.

Step back in time and experience the magic of three of music’s greatest legends; Neil Young, Bob Dylan and Van Morrison, performed by the fantastic Muddy River Band. This talented group of musicians bring to life the unforgettable melodies, poignant lyrics, and electrifying performances that have echoed through the lives of generations of music lovers. This show will leave you inspired and longing for more.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: One Night of Adele A Stunning Tribute
Description:
Adele fans, get ready! The UK’s No.1 Adele tribute, starring The Voice UK finalist Naomi Johnson, is returning to Ireland in February 2026 for a spectacular tour. One Night of Adele is more than just a tribute—it’s an experience, capturing the magic, the emotion, and the powerhouse vocals of one of the greatest artists of our time.

From the heartbreaking ballads to the anthems that make you want to dance, One Night of Adele brings every iconic hit to life, including Hello, Someone Like You, Rolling In The Deep, Easy On Me, and many more. With remarkable attention to detail, Naomi Johnson embodies Adele’s signature style—the hair, the dresses, the wit, and, most importantly, that unmistakable voice.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Rock Rising Supreme Classic Rock Show
Description:
Expect huge hits, iconic anthems, and jaw-dropping musicianship from a world-class 7-piece band paying tribute to the likes of Pink Floyd, Fleetwood Mac, The Eagles, AC/DC, Journey, Toto, Thin Lizzy and more. 

Get ready for another amazing night out!

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Don Baker – My Story Tour 2026
Description:
Harmonica player, singer-songwriter and actor, he has headlined with Tom Jones and Van Morrison, acted opposite Daniel Day Lewis in the Oscar Nominated In the Name of the Father and been declared by Bono as “The greatest harmonica player in the World!”.

In 2022 Don released his first-ever collection of harmonica instrumentals, recorded over the last 40 years, while TG4 broadcast Don’s life story on their award winning documentary series, Finné.

This legendary performer and consummate entertainer has engaged, enchanted and charmed audiences for almost 60 years!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The High Kings, The Rocky Road Tour
Description:
The High Kings are global ambassadors of Irish heritage and storytelling. They have consistently brought the timeless tradition of folk music to audiences worldwide, earning chart-topping success & acclaim. With their signature blend of classic tradition & modern edge, The Rocky Road Tour promises to be a vibrant showcase of Irish culture, energy, & musicality.

The High Kings have performed for hundreds of thousands across the globe and graced prestigious stages including Glastonbury Festival, The Isle of Wight Festival, Times Square, Trafalgar Square, London & many more. Their live television appearances have spanned the U.S., with performances on Good Morning America, The View, & The Today Show.

“A Folk Juggernaut” - The New York Times
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Beautiful Crazy The Luke Combs Collection
Description:
Brand NEW for 2026, Beautiful Crazy is an exciting and authentic theatrical celebration of one of the biggest country stars on the planet… Luke Combs.

Featuring award-winning country singer Noel Boland and a band led by Sarah Jory, the world’s number one female pedal steel guitar player and BCMA Hall of Fame Inductee, Beautiful Crazy features fan favourites.

Since bursting onto the country scene, singer-songwriter, from North Carolina, has bagged numerous accolades including 11 CMA and 4 ACM awards, as well as becoming a six times Billboard Music Award winner.

With a perfect blend of country & Southern rock, prepare to be lifted by heartfelt lyrics & authentic storytelling through Luke's songs.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Emma Doran’s 'Emmaculate'
Description:
Suitable for age 14+
Smash hit Irish comedian, best-selling author, podcast host and viral online star Emma Doran hits the road once again taking her brand-new stand-up show Emmaculate across the UK and Ireland in 2025/26.

There's no such thing as a perfect family, but she wouldn't want one anyway... The many trials and tribulations of Emma’s blended family are laid bare, womanhood examined, and relationships put to the test in this witty, rambunctious show from one of Ireland’s most beloved stand-ups. Strap in for a night to remember, jam-packed with razor-sharp observations, riotous on-stage antics and conspiratorial gossip mongering. 

Emma currently co-hosts the podcast Keep It Tight alongside fellow stand-up Deirdre O’Kane.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Artist-Initiated Projects 2026
Description:
Between January–December 2026 we will present 8 x 3-week exhibitions of new work by:

Paddy Critchley, Fiona Marron, Finn Nichol, Emma Brennan and Thomas Wells, Ciara Rodgers, Struàn Bell, Christopher Mahon, neonatus.exe

Artist-Initiated Projects at Pallas Projects/Studios is an open-submission, annual gallery programme of 8 x 3-week exhibitions taking place between January and December 2026. This unique programme of funded, artist-initiated projects selected via open call is highly accessible to artists, with a focus on early career, emerging artists and recent graduates. Projects are supplemented with artists' talks, texts, workshops or performances, and gallery visits by colleges and local schools.
Address: Pallas Projects/Studios, 115-116, The Coombe, The Liberties, Dublin

Name: Ardgillan Castle Self Guided Tour, January
Description:
Discover the charm of Ardgillan Castle at your own pace with a self-guided tour, designed to offer a flexible and enriching experience. Learn about the fascinating history through informative displays and exhibits throughout the house. Explore the impressive Drawing Room, where the Taylor Family once socialised. Marvel at the decorative woodwork of the Dining Room that evokes the grandeur of formal dining. Lose yourself in the charm of the library, lined with bookshelves filled with literary treasures.

By contrast, visitors can explore the working kitchens, the heart of the house, providing a fascinating glimpse into the culinary preparations and daily operations that sustained the estate during its heyday.
Address: Ardgillan Demesne, Dublin

Name: Ardgillan Castle Self Guided Tour, January
Description:

Name: McCartney: All the Best
Description:
This immersive concert experience brings to life the unforgettable hits spanning McCartney’s career—from his groundbreaking years with The Beatles, through his chart-topping success with Wings, to his iconic solo work. Audiences will be transported by note-perfect renditions of timeless songs like Live and Let Die, Band on the Run, Hey Jude, and many more. Join us for an unforgettable night honouring one of music’s true legends!

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: An Táin Arts Centre, Crowe St, Townparks, Dundalk, Louth

Name: Gerry Adams: A Ballymurphy Man
Description:
Both vilified and vital to peace, Gerry Adams breaks his silence in this landmark documentary, tracing his journey from teenage activist to key architect of the Good Friday Agreement, and revealing the personal story behind one of modern Ireland’s most controversial and transformative leaders.

Trisha Ziff, Mexico 2025, 117mins 12A

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: An Táin Arts Centre, Crowe St, Townparks, Dundalk, Louth

Name: Brigid of Faughart Festival: Brigid Symbols & Customs Workshop
Description:
This event is part of The Brigid of Faughart Festival 2026. The festival celebrates Brigid of Faughart as Ireland’s matron Saint and pre-Christian Celtic Goddess and her birthplace of Faughart, where the story of Saint Brigid began.

Tickets €12 (+€1.50 booking fee per ticket)

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: An Táin Arts Centre, Crowe St, Townparks, Dundalk, Louth

Name: Soundscape Journey/Sound Bath Concert
Description:
Join us for a 90-min Sound Bath Concert in Drumhierny Woodland Escape in Leitrim. This special event will feature a beautiful artistic collaboration, creating a sound experience unlike any other. You will be enveloped by the soothing vibrations of gongs, singing bowls, and the calming sounds of handpan and flute, designed to promote deep relaxation, stress relief, and overall well-being.

Handpan, gongs, drums, singing bowls, solfeggio pipes, Rav Vast, keyboard, flutes, guitars.

An opportunity to experience the concert in a unique venue. You will relax on your mat while the musicians create gentle, calming sound textures using different instruments.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Drumhierny Woodland Hideaway, Drumhierney Manor, Leitrim Village, Glenville, Leitrim

Name: Crois Belt Weaving
Description:
Under Úna’s expert guidance, you’ll explore the history and techniques behind this ancient craft, using vibrant threads to create your very own wearable masterpiece. Perfect for beginners and craft enthusiasts alike, this workshop is a wonderful way to connect with Irish traditions while developing new skills in a friendly and creative atmosphere.

This is an off-site event and takes place in our second space at Bó Studios, No6 Jocelyn Street.

All materials provided.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: An Táin Arts Centre, Crowe St, Townparks, Dundalk, Louth

Name: TOGETHERNESS
Description:
With a lead role in the opera Lady Gregory in America, an album release, numerous sold out performances and her first tour with Paul Cibis under her belt, 2025 has been a wonderful year for Helen.

Music on the theme of love includes:
✨ Frauenliebe und -leben (a woman's love and life). 
✨ Greek folksongs (Ravel)
✨ Irish songs by Ina Boyle, Thomas Moore and Criostóir Ó Loingsigh
✨ Musical theatre numbers

TOGETHERNESS was originally performed as Zweisamkeit (Two Togetherness) by the duo in Germany as part of Zeitgeist Irland 24, a season of Irish arts events curated by Culture Ireland and the Embassy of Ireland, Germany. The album, recorded in Berlin, is out now on Navona Records and can be streamed/purchased as download.
Address: Goethe-Institut Irland, 37 Merrion Square E, Dublin, Dublin

Description:

Name: Irish Chili Festival
Description:
Join us for a weekend dedicated to all things chili! Taste some of the finest new hot sauces from across Europe (and beyond), savour delicious food with vegan and vegetarian options, come and enjoy this amazing event.

Entry is free but donations accepted.

Well-behaved dogs are welcome, and this is a family-friendly event.

Spicy Market Stalls
International Hot Sauce
Fresh Chilis & Chili Plants
Hot Sauce tasting
Comedy
Spicy Cabaret show
Live Music
BBQ & Food Trucks
Vegan & Vegetarian options
Kids Play Area
Chili Eating Competition
Clifton Chili Club

& much much more!

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Marina Market, Centre Park Rd, Ballintemple, Cork

Name: Carnaval Dublin
Description:
Get ready to experience Carnaval in Dublin! Join the Brazilian community and friends on Sunday, February 8th, starting at 5pm at an access all areas Button Factory takeover.

Enjoy live music, drumming, DJs, dance shows, colourful costumes and a festive atmosphere that will transport you to the streets of Brazil for the world's greatest party!
This is our 13th year running Dublin's leading Carnaval party that's a fun filled festival of celebration, colour and positive vibrations! It's all alegria in the Real Events folia!
Entertainment confirmed so far includes: 353 Samba Club, DJ Samuuh, Morro 16, Jassy Lins Axé Band, Rio Soul Samba and more to be confirmed!

Over 18's. Tickets are non-refundable.
Address: Button Factory, Curved St, Temple Bar, Dublin

Name: Irish Chili Festival
Description:
Join us for a weekend dedicated to all things chili! Taste some of the finest new hot sauces from across Europe (and beyond), savour delicious food with vegan and vegetarian options, come and enjoy this amazing event.

Name: Patrick Feeney
Description:
Known for his heartfelt performances and powerful voice, Patrick Feeney will bring his unique blend of inspirational and uplifting music to audiences across Ireland and beyond.

Patrick is an acclaimed Irish tenor whose heartfelt delivery and powerful voice have made him a fixture in Ireland’s country and gospel music scene. With a career launched in his teens and continued success across live performances, recordings, and television, he’s truly a performer “born to sing.”

Patrick is widely recognised for his remarkable vocal talent and emotional delivery. Over the years, he has built a loyal following through his recordings, television appearances, and sell-out live performances.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Legends Of The 60's Show
Description:
The show with the ultimate feelgood factor will transport you all the way hand clapping, swinging and singing back to that magical golden decade that was the 1960s. Whether you come to relive your childhood memories or you just simply want to see what your parents have been raving about for all these years, this is the No.1 live show to see.

Performing high quality tributes to icons like Elvis, The Beatles, The Monkeys, The Rolling Stones, Roy Orbison, Tom Jones, Cliff Richard, The Beach Boys and the Drifters to name but a small few, all in one night, let us deliver to you an entertaining musical live experience featuring over 40 iconic hit songs never to be forgotten!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The Piano Man
Description:
John Forde and The 52nd Street Band deliver a powerful and energetic performance, captivating audiences with their authentic renditions of timeless classic hits, including “Rocket Man”, “My Life”, “Your Song”, “Uptown Girl,” “New York State Of Mind”, “Crocodile Rock”, and of course… “The Piano Man”.

Having collectively sold over 500 million records and having continued to dominate the world music scene from the ’70s right up to today, Elton John and Billy Joel are undeniably two of the most influential artists in history.

Book early to makes sure you don’t miss out on this sell-out show and an unforgettable night of top-class entertainment.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Legends of American Country Show
Description:
“The Legends of American Country”, this hit country show returns for another fantastic night of toe-tapping Country nostalgia!

The upcoming tour will showcase highly acclaimed tributes to Dolly Parton, Johnny Cash, Don Williams, Patsy Cline, Hank Williams, Tammy Wynette and Kenny Rogers and new tributes to icons Alan Jackson, Shania Twain, Garth Brooks, The Chicks and the new kid on the block Luke Combs with countless other well-known singalong hit songs in this must-see musical extravaganza.

One thing is for sure, if it’s Country music you like then this is the only show in town!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The Love Hungry Farmer
Description:
John Bosco McLaine is a man who has never lain down with a woman, but has had romantic blood pulsing through his veins all his life.

Always an optimist, and virgin in equal measure, he embarks on a search for an ideal companion regardless of age, failure rate or dammed advice..!

And in a community where advice is ample and women few, he follows his heart in an ultimate bid to procure himself a wife.

Seamus O’Rourke brings to life in this production the wistful, blundering, romantic John Bosco McLean in an uproarious evening of laughter, love, hope and desire…
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The Ultimate Bubble Show
Description:
Ray Bubbles, International Bubbleologist and Guinness World Record Holder, is on a mission to master the art of bubble-making and create the ultimate square bubble!

Prepare to be amazed by jaw-dropping bubble sculptures, from volcanoes and carousels to ghosts and tornados—all made of bubbles!!

The show was inspired by Ray’s work with seND schools, adult neurodivergent groups, and science centres across Europe.

The performance is suitable for children and adults with additional needs.
Don’t miss the magic!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Hey! Old Friends!
Description:
Featuring the crème de la crème of local talent — Declan Neville, Dermot Deely, Sean Hussey, Denise McNamara, Patricia Farrell, Riona Lynch, Gemma Cowan-Thomsen, Jacinta Baxter, Jesse Kersh, Aonghus O’Muiri, and many more.

With musical direction by Fintan Farrelly and Emer McGowan, enjoy stunning four-part harmonies on classics like “Dream the Impossible Dream”, “Old Friends”, and “Always Look on the Bright Side of Life”. Expect Disney favourites, a Drifters medley, and performances from The Bronte Stage School and The Innyside Singers.

Directed by Brian Murray, with choreography by Sylvia Johnston, and comedy directed by John Kelly — it’s an uplifting night not to be missed!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Hey! Old Friends!
Description:
Featuring the crème de la crème of local talent — Declan Neville, Dermot Deely, Sean Hussey, Denise McNamara, Patricia Farrell, Riona Lynch, Gemma Cowan-Thomsen, Jacinta Baxter, Jesse Kersh, Aonghus O’Muiri, and many more.

Description:

Name: Philomena Begley & Ray Lynam
Description:
Philomena Begley and Ray Lynam are two household names and true legends of Irish Country music and have both had glittering careers that spans over 6 decades at the very top of the music scene.

They both have enjoyed massive success and have seen their respective careers hit great heights and scoring countless No.1 hit songs like “Blanket on the Ground, Gypsy Joe and Me, The Queen of the Silver Dollar “and regularly playing to packed houses in every hall, festival and theatre from Ireland to Tennessee.

This upcoming “We go together” tour will see both stars duet songs like “My elusive dreams, We Go together and Somewhere between” to name but a small few of their impressive catalogue.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Brendan Kelly Band
Description:
Fronted by Brendan Kelly from Longford, this talented 4-piece blends modern country, rock, folk, and a touch of gospel to create music that is both uplifting and deeply emotional.

Joined by Mark McCormack (guitar, keyboards), Udo Grunewald (bass), and Andrei Calcea (drums), the Brendan Kelly Band delivers a live show filled with heartfelt storytelling, soaring melodies, and infectious energy. Their standout songs, including “Brother,” “I’ll Pick You Up,” and the new single “High Roller” connect with audiences of all ages and musical tastes.

Having captivated crowds across Ireland, the Brendan Kelly Band now brings their passionate, high-energy performance to Longford.

Don’t miss an unforgettable evening of music, emotion and inspiration!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Daily Lough Ree & River Shannon Cruise
Description:
Departing from Athlone Castle, this cruise travels upstream on the River Shannon to Lough Ree. During the sailing there is commentary on local history, the religious settlements on the islands and tales of Viking treasure. This is a boat trip that can be enjoyed by all ages and is suitable for any family occasion. Don a Viking helmet and prepare for battle or simply sit back with an Irish Coffee. Either way, you will enjoy the beauty of the Inland Waterways and witness everything Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands has to offer.

Check the website for the latest sailing times. Tickets available onboard. 

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Name: Viking History & Celtic Mythology Walking Tour of Athlone
Description:
Hear the ancient tales of gods and goddesses who once roamed these sacred waters. From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Booking Essential. Price on request.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Name: Viking History & Celtic Mythology Walking Tour of Athlone
Description:
Hear the ancient tales of gods and goddesses who once roamed these sacred waters. From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Booking Essential. Price on request.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Name: Art for the Heart Doneraile Arts Festival 2026
Description:
Art for the Heart Doneraile presents 2026 Doneraile Arts Fest, Art 4 All from June 25th–28th in the stunning  surroundings of Doneraile Court & Annesgrove Gardens.

A major four-day event, the Doneraile Arts Festival features important En Plein Air competitions in Annesgrove Gardens (27th), Doneraile Par (28th) with a top prize of €1000. 

There are creative workshops and demonstrations from willow weaving to pastels, from life drawing to  drumming with many events at low or no cost. There will be music and performances from the fantastical Art for the Heart Street Performers.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Doneraile Court, Doneraile Estate, Demesne, Doneraile, Cork

Name: Spraoi International Street Arts Festival 2026
Description:
Visitors from all over the world are welcome at Ireland's free summer street party. Spraoi International Street Arts Festival performers and musicians transform this ancient city into a giant stage. Waterford's medieval buildings, streets, and squares create magical venues. 

The Spraoi festival parade features floats and hundreds of costumed performers. Jugglers, acrobats, dancers, musicians, comic performers, fireworks, fun, and food stalls joyously mix at Spraoi. Be a Spraoi Spirit in 2026 - Add that to your bucket list!

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Waterford

Name: Waterford Festival of Food 2026
Description:
Standing as one of Ireland’s largest and longest-running community food festivals - the 2026 programme has something to suit every taste. The main hub for this 3-day event is the scenic harbour town of Dungarvan, Co. Waterford with events also taking place in nearby Lismore, and surrounds.

Highlights from the diverse range of events on this year’s programme include guided taste tours, high profile guest chef dining events, sustainability workshops, and pop-up experiences in unusual locations. There will also be foraging walks, food waste talks, bus tours to farms and mountains, live cooking demos, and the festival’s famous market which is Ireland’s largest outdoor market, in Dungarvan’s market square on the final day.
Address: Dungarvan, Waterford

Name: Analogue Photographic Exhibition Works inspired by Brigid
Description:
A new group exhibition at Creative Spark Downtown Hub, Clanbrassil Street, Dundalk, presents analogue photographic works inspired by Brigid as a symbol of place and transformation. Opening Thursday 29 January at 7pm, the exhibition explores slow, material-led processes shaped by land, light, and time. Using plant-based and sustainable darkroom techniques, the works include site-responsive experiments such as seaweed lumen prints made on Blackrock Beach, highlighting photography as a collaborative, place-based practice rooted in ecology.

Exhibition Runs: Friday 30 January to Thursday 26 February
10am to 4.30pm, Mon to Fri

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Creative Spark Downtown Hub, 42-43 Clanbrassil St, Townparks, Dundalk, Louth

Name: Nature Connection Hike
Description:
Forest Bathing Guide, Cat, from Beneath the Canopy, will guide you through the valley of Glendalough on a gentle 8km hike. Along the way we will stop multiple times to be mindful and connect to and appreciate nature.

We will meet at the free carpark in Laragh (next to Derrybawn Bridge) at 10:00.

This event is donation-based. At the end of the hike, you decide what you pay. All donations go towards the Beneath the Canopy "Fill Your Cup" programme. This programme offers free nature connection events to people who would benefit from nature connection but might not be able to afford it.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Laragh, Brockagh, Laragh, Wicklow

Name: The Big Screen Movie Quiz
Description:
Quizhost Ireland is delighted to announce the new home of the ever-popular Big Screen Movie Quiz.

Kicking off on Thursday 29th January at 8pm, and taking place every 4th Thursday, the quiz will now be hosted in the warm and welcoming surroundings of Cleary’s Pub, right in the heart of Dublin 
 
Expect epic movie clips and soundtracks on the big screen, mind-bending riddles and questionable team names- all washed down with one of the best (and cheapest) pints of Guinness in Dublin.
Assemble your team and book in soon to avoid disappointment!

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Cleary's Pub, 36 Amiens St, Mountjoy, Dublin

Hear the ancient tales of gods and goddesses who once roamed these sacred waters. From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Booking Essential. Price on request.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Hear the ancient tales of gods and goddesses who once roamed these sacred waters. From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Booking Essential. Price on request.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Name: Analogue Photographic Exhibition Works inspired by Brigid
Description:
A new group exhibition at Creative Spark Downtown Hub, Clanbrassil Street, Dundalk, presents analogue photographic works inspired by Brigid as a symbol of place and transformation. Opening Thursday 29 January at 7pm, the exhibition explores slow, material-led processes shaped by land, light, and time. Using plant-based and sustainable darkroom techniques, the works include site-responsive experiments such as seaweed lumen prints made on Blackrock Beach, highlighting photography as a collaborative, place-based practice rooted in ecology.

Exhibition Runs: Friday 30 January to Thursday 26 February
10am to 4.30pm, Mon to Fri

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Creative Spark Downtown Hub, 42-43 Clanbrassil St, Townparks, Dundalk, Louth

Name: Tour: Viking Ireland
Description:
On this Viking Ireland Family Tour see a replica fishing boat, examine the beautiful oval brooches worn by Scandinavian women, and discover ice skates made of bone! Discover what life was like in Viking Dublin, and see the wider impact that the Vikings had on Ireland. 

Sat 17 Jan at 2pm / Wed 21 Jan at 12.30pm.

This tour is not wheelchair accessible. 

No booking required, places allocated on a first come first served basis. Meet in Reception, on the ground floor. Families with children 7+.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street, 35A Kildare St, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Tour: The Saints of Ireland
Description:
On this tour, discover the artefacts and shrines associated with the patron saints of Ireland, St. Patrick, St. Brigid, and St. Colmcille, and see what archaeological evidence can tell us about the impact of the introduction of Christianity to Ireland in the early medieval period.

Sat 31 Jan at 1pm or 3.30pm

This tour will take approximately 25 minutes.
 
No booking required, places allocated on a first come first served basis. Meet in the Reception. This tour will take place in the Treasury exhibition on the ground floor, and the Medieval Ireland exhibition on the first floor. This tour is not wheelchair accessible.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street, 35A Kildare St, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Trinity Trails & Book of Kells Experience
Description:
Start in Trinity’s iconic Front Square where you will meet your expert Trinity Trails guide and embark on an 45 minute walking tour of Trinity’s historic campus.

As you pass through leafy walkways and cobblestone squares, your guide will take you through the ages of Trinity’s 400 year history. Discover hidden gems, learn about famous past students and get exclusive access to the Museum Building, which boasts one of the most stunning interiors in Dublin.

Afterwards, take a journey through the Book of Kells Experience. Learn about the making of the Book of Kells before you view the 1,200 year old manuscript up close.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Trinity College Dublin, College Green, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Tour: Highlights of the National Museum of Ireland
Description:
Join in this tour and see some of the most famous artefacts from the National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street's collection and learn about life from Stone Age to Medieval Ireland. Discover perfectly preserved fish traps from the Mesolithic, gold objects from the Bronze Age and iconic Early Medieval treasures of the ‘Tara’ Brooch and the Ardagh Chalice.
  
No booking required, places allocated on a first come first served basis. Meet in the Reception, on the ground floor. Wheelchair accessible. Children must be fully supervised by a parent or adult who is in loco parentis.

See NMI website for tour details.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Tour: The History of Art in Ireland
Description:
See megalithic art from the Stone Age on the replica passage tomb, discover the techniques in gold of Bronze Age metalworkers on the lunulae and sun discs, learn about the influence of the Iron Age art style, La Tène, and its impact into Early Medieval Ireland on objects including the Ardagh Chalice and the 'Tara' Brooch. 

No booking required, places allocated on a first-come first-served basis. Meet in the Reception, on the ground floor. Wheelchair accessible. Adults, Families with children 8+. Children must be fully supervised by a parent or adult who is in loco parentis.

Fri 16 Jan at 12.30pm / Fri 30 Jan at 12.30pm

Name: Tuesdays with Morrie
Description:
Presenting Tuesdays with Morrie, based on the phenomenally popular first novel by Mitch Albom.

It is a heart-warming autobiographical story of Mitch Albom, an accomplished journalist driven solely by his career, and Morrie Schwartz, his former college professor. Sixteen years after graduation, Mitch happens to catch Morrie’s appearance on a television news programme and learns that his old professor is battling Lou Gehrig’s Disease. Mitch is reunited with Morrie, and what starts as a simple visit, turns into a weekly pilgrimage and a last class in the meaning of life.

“Unforgettable! No matter how well you tell the story, the play makes it more vivid, more shattering, more humorous.” – New York Magazine
Address: The Everyman, 15 MacCurtain Street, Victorian Quarter, Cork City, Cork

Name: Van Gogh - An Immersive Journey
Description:
Running every Friday* from 16th January to 20th March, the Dream Point Experience is transforming into an immersive dreamscape dedicated to renowned post-impressionist artist, Vincent Van Gogh. 

Float through a Van Gogh wonderland, both informative and inspiring - complete with 360 degree floor-to-ceiling immersion, documentaries and a host of Van Gogh themed activities taking place throughout Dream Point’s seven iconic rooms. From the giant balloon room to Ireland’s biggest ball pit, every inch of Dream Point will be dappled with new, artistic life!

Both all-ages AND adults-only sessions are available to book!

All ages admissions will run from 10am - 5pm
Adults-only sessions (Dream Point After Dark) will run from 5:30 - 8pm

*exclude Fri 13 Feb.
Address: First Floor, Theatre of Light, Point Square Shopping Centre, E Wall Rd, North Wall, Dublin

Name: Vampires & The Human Psyche - The Psychology of Horror Movies
Description:
Join Katie Evans in tracing horror through the ages and how it reflects societal anxieties. We will explore why we are drawn to these films, our relationship with fear, and how we connect with the characters and narratives they present. Through a psychological lens, we will also examine how horror gives voice to our often overlooked stories. Whether you are a horror nerd, a film geek, or someone interested in psychology, this talk will offer a unique perspective on the horror genres.

Over 18s only

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Sugar Club, 8 Leeson Street Lower, Saint Kevin's, Dublin

Name: Tullamore TradFest
Description:
Get ready for an amazing weekend of music, craic, and culture in Tullamore this March! Dive into concerts, a lively session trail, workshops, recitals, and over 150 hours of FREE entertainment all weekend long.

Highlights:
Fri 27 Mar, 8pm (€20): Tara Breen, Jim Murray & Pádraig Rynne, plus HUP! Offaly Hall of Fame inductees – The Kinsella Family.

Sat 28 Mar, 5pm (€10 / €20 family): CUAS, the Ryan Family, and a special Group Workshop performance.

Sat 28 Mar, 10pm (€15): Peter Street & guests for a late-night session!

Sun 29 Mar, 3pm (€12): Céilí with The Glenside Céilí Band.

Don’t miss this celebration of tradition, talent, and pure fun – all in the heart of Offaly!
Address: Tullamore, Offaly

Name: The Knitted Bible Project
Description:
We love stories for entertainment, learning, and inspiration. The Bible is a book full of stories, and this exhibition presents a small selection through the Knitted Bible. Each piece has been chosen and designed by its maker, creating an organic and creative exploration of familiar and lesser-known biblical stories. Experiencing these narratives through knitted and textile artworks offers a fresh perspective and invites reflection and curiosity. 

The Knitted Bible exhibition is available during cathedral opening hours and is included in the standard admission price. Please see our website for opening times and further information.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Christ Church Cathedral, Christchurch Pl, Wood Quay, Dublin

Name: The Music of John Denver
Description:
Marc performs all the hits including ‘Annie’s Song’, ‘Leaving On A Jet Plane’, ‘Take Me Home Country Roads’, ‘Some Days Are Diamonds’, ‘Calypso’ and ‘Thank God I’m A Country Boy’, while also narrating John Denver’s life story to a multimedia backdrop.

Marc’s show ‘A Tribute To The Music of John Denver’ has been a huge success, selling out prestigious venues, including the National Concert Hall, Dublin on two occasions and Marc has toured the show in Belgium, Germany, Australia, Sri Lanka and John Denver’s home town of Aspen, Colorado.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Watergate Theatre, Gardens, Kilkenny City, Kilkenny

Name: The Northern Lights - Celebrating Brigid
Description:
Illuminate | Commemorate | Celebrate - join us in celebrating Brigid of Faughart as we take you on radiant journey of light, music and splendour, with four extraordinary audio-visual shows set to take place each evening between 6pm and 7pm on Sunday 1st and Monday 2nd of February at Market Square in Dundalk.

The eight majestic golden arches feature up to 200,000 individual points of light, and decorative symbols inspired by the iconic Brigid of Faughart mural by Irish artist Friz.

Join us at Market Square in Dundalk for this truly enchanting experience!

✨6.00pm
✨6.20pm
✨6.40pm
✨7.00pm

This experience is proudly brought to you by Louth County Council and Dundalk Town.

Enquiries to 0429352842/ 0429352111/ info@dundalkbids.com
Address: Market Square, Dundalk, Louth

Discover perfectly preserved fish traps from the Mesolithic, gold objects from the Bronze Age and iconic Early Medieval treasures of the ‘Tara’ Brooch and the Ardagh Chalice.
  
No booking required, places allocated on a first come first served basis. Meet in the Reception, on the ground floor. Wheelchair accessible. Children must be fully supervised by a parent or adult who is in loco parentis.

See NMI website for tour details.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Kildare Street, 35A Kildare St, Dublin, Dublin

Name: The Skellig Coast Dark Skies Cruise by Starlight Sea Cruise
Description:
Experience the Skellig Coast on a guided voyage shaped by the sea and the stars. Glide through quiet waters, watch the lighthouse sweep the horizon, and discover Kerry’s night skies from the deck of Skellig Bounty. Along the way, hear the stories of sailors who once navigated these waters by starlight, using the same skies above you to find their way across the Atlantic. We recommend dressing warmly so you can relax and enjoy every moment.

Friday 20th March from Cahersiveen Marina, departing at 6.30pm
Saturday 21st March from Knightstown, Valentia, departing at 6.30pm

Name: The Northern Lights - Celebrating Brigid
Description:
Illuminate | Commemorate | Celebrate - join us in celebrating Brigid of Faughart as we take you on radiant journey of light, music and splendour, with four extraordinary audio-visual shows set to take place each evening between 6pm and 7pm on Sunday 1st and Monday 2nd of February at Market Square in Dundalk.

The eight majestic golden arches feature up to 200,000 individual points of light, and decorative symbols inspired by the iconic Brigid of Faughart mural by Irish artist Friz.

Join us at Market Square in Dundalk for this truly enchanting experience!

✨6.00pm
✨6.20pm
✨6.40pm
✨7.00pm

This experience is proudly brought to you by Louth County Council and Dundalk Town.

Name: Tom Crean Antarctic Explorer
Description:
Hear the riveting true stories of Crean’s Antarctic explorations as one of the few men to serve with both Scott and Shackleton and survive the three famous expeditions, Discovery (1901-1904), Terra Nova (1910-1913), Endurance (1914 -1916)

Crean’s story is a testament of human fortitude against all the elements of the Antarctica, his 36-mile solitary trek back to base camp to save his comrades Teddy Evans and William Lashley has been described by historians as, “one of the finest feats of individual heroism from the entire age of Antarctic exploration”.

Name: The Three Amigos
Description:
Join us at the Gleneagle Arena on Thursday, 29 January 2026 for The Three Amigos — a rollicking live show packed with crowd-pleasing hits, sing-along anthems, and a vibrant stage performance that has made them one of Ireland’s favourite party acts. Drawing inspiration from country, pop, rock and classic sing-along favourites, The Three Amigos deliver a fun-filled concert experience designed to get everyone dancing and singing together.

Whether you’re out for a night with friends, a date night, or just love a great live show, this performance promises contagious energy, nostalgic favourites and plenty of good vibes. Don’t miss your chance to start 2026 with a bang — tickets are on sale now!

Name: The Flowers of May
Description:
Blending humour with unflinching honesty, the play explores the emotional terrain of parenting a child with autism—from the chaos of daily routines to the quiet, deeply felt victories that shape family life. At its heart, it's a story of partnership and perseverance, revealing how love, empathy, and resilience sustain two people facing the unknown together.

The play invites audiences into a world where every challenge is matched by moments of grace, where laughter coexists with vulnerability, and where the bonds of family are tested—and strengthened—in profound ways.

Name: Ye Vagabonds
Description:
Rising from the ashes of the 2008 economic collapse, the Mac Gloinn brothers  – Brian and Diarmuid – first performed in the streets together as buskers. They coalesced into Ye Vagabonds while simultaneously creating now-legendary music nights and community-building events in their native Carlow, and later in Dublin.

Ye Vagabonds have since evolved into a robust collective – a sonically unique ensemble featuring layered acoustic and electric guitars, trumpet, upright bass, Moog synthesizer, harmonium, bouzouki, fiddle, and the blood harmony of the siblings’ distinctive, multi-hued voices.

Age 18+ / Standing event.

Name: Joe the Magician Live
Description:
Joe’s show fuses laugh out loud comedy magic with absolutely jaw dropping spectacular illusions live on stage. It’s a rollercoaster theatrical ride that reinvents children’s entertainment.

Joe’s hugely popular TV show 'Dizzy Deliveries' is the most watched show on RTÉjr and the most-streamed show on the RTÉ Player and has over 75 million YouTube views! Joe plays a magical delivery man who uses Làmh sign. Now in its third season.

70mins no interval / ages 4+yrs

Name: My Neighbourhood - Christian Stejskal
Description:
Along the way, I encountered the Zabaleen - Cairo’s unofficial Christian garbage collectors, who live in what is known as Garbage City. They are particularly known for pig farming - a controversial activity in a Muslim country - while living amidst the refuse of the city itself.

I spent five years photographing these people and writing stories about them, while earning my living as a violinist at the Cairo Opera House. I have combined my photographs and stories with my own composed music, which I perform live on the violin.

The result is a 90-minute performance about politics, revolution, religion, pigs, garbage, friendship, and love.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Dubliner: The Luke Kelly Story with Chris Kavanagh
Description:

This exciting stage show featuring Chris Kavanagh, brings to life the legend that is Luke Kelly and captures that unique atmosphere that made Luke so special.

It has even been said by countless audience members that his presence can be felt on stage 40 years on.

Name: Walking with Brigid - History, Stories and a Touch of Magic
Description:
The weekend begins with Along Brigid’s Path: a walk and talk starting beside the Wayside Well. Historian Mario Corrigan will explore the well’s links to St. Brigid, before walking through the Stud. A presentation will follow, highlighting Kildare Town, the Black Abbey, St. John’s Well & the Stud, ending with 4 stories of Brigid for families. Colouring pages will be available.

Then enjoy a guided Nature Walk through St. Fiachra’s Garden, & the afternoon will be filled with live music from local musicians, arts & crafts, and a children’s drawing competition to design St. Brigid’s Cloak.

12:00–1:00: Along Brigid’s Path - walk & talk
1:15: Nature Walk through St. Fiachra’s Garden
12:00–3:00: Live music, arts, crafts & the drawing competition
Address: Tully East, Kildare

Description:
Across the project, the Dublin artist maps the moment when life feels overcrowded with late nights, casual links and scene pressure, and what happens when he finally decides to close the door.

For this special show, Wallfella will dive deep into The Coop is Full in an intimate setting. He is joined by long-time collaborators, Nealo and Flynn Johnson, whose own writing explores similar questions of identity, pressure and self-belief. Expect high energy, sharp lyricism, and a night that looks at chaos, coping and change with clear eyes.

For ages 18+ only.

Description:
Zog is the keenest dragon in Madam Dragon’s school. He's also the most accident-prone, flying into trees, and even setting his own wing alight. Thankfully, a kind girl comes along to patch up his scratches and scrapes – but can she help him with his biggest challenge yet, so that he can finally win a golden star? 

This charming smash-hit show, from the producers of Stick Man and Zog and the Flying Doctors and featuring joyful songs by Joe Stilgoe with beautiful puppets by Little Angel, is a treat for children and adults alike.

Various performance times. Check The Everyman website for more info.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: MacCurtain Street, Victorian Quarter, Cork City, Cork

Name: The Cunning Little Vixen
Description:
Janáček’s opera is no fairy tale, but rather a reflection on the cycle of life. His evocative score weaves rich folk melodies with playful humour, capturing the sounds of nature’s eternal rhythm and its wild, irrepressible spirit.

American soprano Amber Norelai stars as the Vixen, with Irish soprano Jade Phoenix (Die Fledermaus 2025) as the Fox and Irish baritone Benjamin Russell as the Forester. The Cunning Little Vixen is directed by Sophie Motley (Werther 2023) and features the Irish National Opera orchestra and chorus.

135mins inc interval | ages 9+yrs

Tickets €30 / €27 conc

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Town Hall Theatre, 1 Courthouse Square, Galway

Name: Viking History & Celtic Mythology Walking Tour of Athlone
Description:
Hear the ancient tales of gods and goddesses who once roamed these sacred waters. From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Booking Essential. Price on request.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Booking Essential. Price on request.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Name: Eleanor the Great
Description:
In Eleanor The Great, June Squibb brings to vivid life the witty and proudly troublesome 94-year old Eleanor Morgenstein, who after a devastating loss, tells a tale that takes on a dangerous life of its own.

Cert: 12A

Tickets €12 / €10 conc

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Shane Todd: Hold Me Back
Description:
Armed with his signature wit, razor-sharp storytelling, and a complete inability to take himself seriously, Shane’s world tour is coming to Wicklow!

Shane Todd hosts the massively popular Tea With Me podcast and performs in some of the world’s most famous comedy clubs.

Shows at 5pm & 8pm | 120mins approx. including interval

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Music Network: Kevin Burke, Dermot Byrne, Noriana Kennedy & Jim Murray
Description:
Accordion maestro Dermot Byrne, legendary fiddler and member of the iconic Bothy Band Kevin Burke, acclaimed singer-songwriter Noriana Kennedy (The Whileaways), and lauded guitarist Jim Murray come together for an exceptional nationwide tour in early 2026.

Audiences can look forward to an evening of remarkable musicianship, rich storytelling and joyful collaboration – in a fitting celebration of Music Network’s four decades of supporting and showcasing Ireland’s most gifted performers.

Tickets €22 / €20 conc

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: KING
Description:
The play explores prejudice, privilege, and resilience, as Luther struggles to live life to the full.

King is the fifth collaboration between Pat Kinevane and Fishamble, with Jim Culleton as director. The production was developed with the support of Nasc Network Commission and Georganne Aldrich Heller.

Tickets €22 / €20 conc

Duration: 80mins / For ages 14+yrs

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Fridayfest Climate Café - February
Description:
Since many of these conversations involve climate and ecology, the Mermaid Arts Centre decided to support it with a series of talks and informal meetups.

The Climate Café is connected to participatory projects including The Mermaid Garden Project, where local residents without their own green spaces can grow, compost and exchange horticultural knowledge in our urban garden.

Suzie Cahn is an ecological and socially engaged artist, educator, and permaculture practitioner. 

Free, booking essential | Under 18's must by accompanied by an adult.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Rebels and Revolutionaries Women's History Tour
Description:
The Women’s History tour draws attention to those left out of the history books, the ones that have been hidden for many years, and how it is only in modern times that we have been revisiting, rediscovering and revealing these crucial pieces of the puzzle. It is the goal of feminist or women’s historians to bring untold women’s stories to light and challenge the historical representation of women, and it is our goal at EPIC to pass that along to our visitors.

This 1 hour guided tour will run across the St Brigid’s Day weekend at the end of January, running into the February bank holiday, in honour of one of the most notable trailblazing Irish women, and our only female patron saint.

€21 for Adults, €14.50 for Children
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Description:
Shane is the boy from school who was capable of more, the kid who could always do better. Well, just try catching him now. Expect rapid-fire craic and big-hearted feelings in his brand-new show about bellies and bullies.

Ages 14+yrs

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Little Folk - On the Road
Description:
‘Little Folk- On the Road’ is perfectly suited for 2-7yr olds and their adults. It’s a foot stomping, hand clapping, and jumping good time. 

Featuring his award winning children’s music, catchy kids tunes and proper sing-a-long songs, it’s super fun for for the whole family. 

The fun doesn’t stop when the show ends, because each child gets a little musical goody bag to user during the show and then take some music home. 

For 2 - 7yrs | Tickets €12, family of 4 €40

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Mothers of Newbridge
Description:
This thoughtfully curated tour celebrates the lives and stories of mothers throughout Newbridge’s history. From caring nannies and dedicated household staff to elegant socialites, discover the women who lived and worked here and helped shape daily life within the house. Through engaging storytelling, their routines, challenges, and legacies are brought to life.

Perfect as a Mother’s Day gift or a chance to enjoy quality time together, this relaxed and enriching experience offers a wonderful alternative to traditional gifts. It’s an opportunity to slow down, share a moment, and create lasting memories in one of Dublin’s most charming historic homes.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Newbridge House & Farm, Hearse Rd, Newbridge Demesne, Donabate, Dublin

Name: St Brigid's Day House Tours
Description:
St. Brigid’s Day: A Celebration of Craft.

This February, join us for our regular Georgian House tours with a special twist in honour of St. Brigid, the patron saint of craft. Alongside the history of the Cobbe family, we’ll be taking a closer look at traditional Irish knitting designs and heritage craft throughout the house.

It’s a lovely way to explore the estate while celebrating the skilled hands and artistry that have been passed down through generations.

St. Brigid: As the patron saint of weavers and crafters, she represents the transition from the dark of winter to the “new life” of spring -which is exactly when people used to knit new garments for the year ahead.
Address: Newbridge House & Farm, Hearse Rd, Newbridge Demesne, Donabate, Dublin

Name: Little Baker, Big Baker
Description:
Looking for a fun and creative activity to do with your child? Join us for our Little Baker / Big Baker Cupcake Classes – a delightful decorating workshop hosted by Cupcakes and Counting in the charming Courtyard Kitchen at Newbridge House and Farm.

A Deliciously Fun Baking Class for Adults and Kids
This special parent-and-child cupcake decorating class is the perfect bonding experience for one adult and one child to unleash their creativity and sweet tooth together. Guided by expert bakers, each pair will transform plain vanilla bakes into eye-catching edible treats.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Newbridge House & Farm, Hearse Rd, Newbridge Demesne, Donabate, Dublin

Name: Love Blooms | Chamber Choir Ireland and Ellie Slorach
Description:
Ireland's national chamber choir welcomes Ellie Slorach for a Valentine's bouquet of music, expressing the rollercoaster of emotions that go along with romantic love: 

The nervousness and elation that composer Derri Joseph Lewis felt when he first came out to his dad, in his piece 'Something Exciting'; intimate settings of Pablo Neruda's poetry by Shruthi Rajasekar and Song of Songs by Áine Mallon; the fleeting beauty of love and flowers in Gerald Finzi's 'Seven poems of Robert Bridges' and Nathan James Dearden's 'full of sweet days'; the turbulence of Alex Groves’ 'Stream and Pool' for choir and electronics; and Benjamin Britten's 'Five Flower Songs', composed for the 25th wedding anniversary of botanists Leonard and Dorothy Elmhirst.
Address: Pepper Canister Church, 7 Mount St Cres, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Bats, Bees and Otter Things
Description:
Johnstown is a haven for Irish wildlife, offering a remarkable diversity of species across its extensive woodlands, lakes, meadows, and gardens. The estate is home to native mammals such as otters, red squirrels, and pine martens; a wide range of bird life and waterfowl, including swans, ducks, and seasonal migrants; and a wealth of insects and vital pollinators supported by wildflower meadows & restored habitats.

Over the past decade, Harm Deenen and Matt Wheeler have dedicated themselves to monitoring and recording this rich biodiversity.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Johnstown Castle Estate, Museum & Gardens, Oldtown Ln, Johnstown, Wexford

Name: Exhibition on Screen: The Dawn of Impressionism, Paris 1874
Description:
The story is told not by historians and curators but in the words of those who witnessed the dawn of Impressionism: the artists, press and people of Paris, 1874. See the show that changed everything on the big screen. Made in close collaboration with the Musee d’Orsay and National Gallery of Art, Washington D.C.

Tickets €12 / €10 conc

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: Women’s Art Activism: Reflections on WAAG with Dr Alessia Cargnelli
Description:
Taking materials from the Women Artists Action Group (WAAG)’s archive as a cue, the talk will focus on the personal experience of Irish women artists working in the context of 1980s-1990s’ island of Ireland and beyond, looking at their collaborative practice and self-advocacy as a strategy to counteract an hostile art environment that was actively excluding women and other minoritised subjects. 

Free, booking essential.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: The Hans Zimmer Experience
Description:
This unmissable event brings the world of cinematic music to life, paying tribute to the legendary scores of Hans Zimmer, one of the greatest film composers of our time. 

The concert features iconic pieces from Pirates of the Caribbean, Inception, Gladiator, Batman and many others, reimagined and arranged for an ensemble of voice, flute, piano, and strings. Zimmer’s unmistakable melodies, which have captivated and inspired audiences for decades, will be performed in an intimate, evocative setting.

2hrs 30mins inc 20min interval

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: My Sweet Valentine
Description:
Start your evening at 6:00pm with a crisp glass of chilled Prosecco and a selection of decadent treats before the lights go down. Then, step back into the Georgian era for a private, candlelit tour of Newbridge House.

Discover the romance and “hidden histories” of the Cobbe estate. It’s timeless, it’s atmospheric, and it’s a Valentine’s date they’ll actually remember.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Newbridge House & Farm, Hearse Rd, Newbridge Demesne, Donabate, Dublin

Name: The Love Hungry Farmer by John B Keane
Description:
Always an optimist, and virgin in equal measure, he embarks on a search for an ideal companion regardless of age, failure rate or dammed advice!…

Often referred to as the natural successor to John B Keane as Ireland’s masterful present day storyteller, Seamus O’Rourke brings to life in this production the wistful, blundering, romantic John Bosco McLean, matching stride for stride Keane’s fantastic wit and way with words in an uproarious evening of laughter, love, hope and desire.

Tickets €28 / €26 conc

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Mermaid County Wicklow Arts Centre, Main St, Bray, Wicklow

Name: 18 More Ballads after James Joyce's Ulysses Volume II by Val O'Donnell
Description:
Join us at the James Joyce Centre on Friday, 6th February at 7pm for the premiere of 18 More Ballads after James Joyce's Ulysses Volume II by Val O’Donnell.

Adding to the success of his 2024 18 Ballads from James Joyce's Ulysses, Val is at it again with 18 more vibrant, playful and engrossing ballads set to airs of music referred to in Ulysses and Joyce's other work. It includes short quotes from Joyce's work, a note on the music and suggestions for accessing the original sheet music and performances of the airs chosen for the ballads.

Copies of 18 More Ballads will be on sale at the event (€10). The book is published by Printwell Books.

Admission is €10. Doors open at 6pm.
Address: James Joyce Centre, James Joyce House, 35 N Great George's St, Rotunda, Basement, Dublin

Name: Irish Artists Abroad: Art History Reframed - Spring 2026
Description:
Art Historian Dr Matthew Whyte offers a new lecture series, which takes the audience on an art-filled journey through the often beautiful, sometimes scandalous, and always fascinating moments in the development of Western civilisation.

The lectures can be attended as a series, but are also designed as standalone talks, which can be attended individually.

This lecture examines Irish art in the early stages of modernity, focusing on how travel and sustained encounters with European modern art helped shape a distinctly Irish artistic response.

As artists studied, lived, and exhibited abroad, they absorbed new approaches to form, colour, and subject matter, while also negotiating questions of national identity and cultural independence.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Irish Contemporary Art: Art History Reframed - Spring 2026
Description:
Art Historian Dr Matthew Whyte offers a new lecture series, which takes the audience on an art-filled journey through the often beautiful, sometimes scandalous, and always fascinating moments in the development of Western civilisation.

The lectures can be attended as a series, but are also designed as standalone talks, which can be attended individually.

We finish our journey through the history of Irish art by turning our attention to the here and now. In the present day, Irish artists are beset with the question that faces artists the world over – what does it feel like to be alive today?

Today’s lecture will include an artist talk from Cork-based visual artist Lara Quinn, a recent graduate of MTU Crawford College of Art & Design.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: The National Collection in Cork’s Crawford Art Gallery: Art History Reframed - Spring 2026
Description:
Art Historian Dr Matthew Whyte offers a new lecture series, which takes the audience on an art-filled journey through the often beautiful, sometimes scandalous, and always fascinating moments in the development of Western civilisation.

The lectures can be attended as a series, but are also designed as standalone talks, which can be attended individually.

This lecture focuses on the Irish National Collection at Crawford Art Gallery, exploring its rich history through a close examination of the gallery’s sculpture collection and the pivotal role played by historic bequests and donations in shaping its growth.

It invites audiences to reconnect with the collection in fresh ways while reflecting on its enduring cultural significance.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Viking History & Celtic Mythology Walking Tour of Athlone
Description:
Hear the ancient tales of gods and goddesses who once roamed these sacred waters. From the mighty warrior Cuchulainn to the mystical goddess Boann, uncover the secrets and legends that have shaped the landscape for centuries. Walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes as we unravel the history of Athlone town. Learn about its origins, from its humble beginnings as a river crossing to its strategic importance in medieval times and beyond. 

Led by knowledgeable guides steeped in Celtic lore and local history, our walking tour promises to immerse you in the magic of Athlone like never before. Prepare to be spellbound by their captivating storytelling and deep insights into the town’s heritage.

Name: Dragana Jurišić 'The Last Balkan Cowboy'
Description:
The Last Balkan Cowboy is a long-term project by Dragana Jurišić that follows the footsteps of cult Yugoslavian film director, Hari Džekson. Renowned for his interpretation of Wild West genre archetypes, Džekson lived a cowboy fantasy inspired by the rugged landscapes around Bijeljina in present-day Bosnia-Herzegovina. Using local people as his crew and cast, Džekson conjured worlds far from his reality and brought together people of different ethnic backgrounds, whom the Yugoslav Wars would bitterly divide.

Tue to Sat 11am - 6pm / Sun 12pm - 4pm / free to visit.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Temple Bar Gallery + Studios, 5-9 Temple Bar, Dublin, Dublin

Description:
The Last Balkan Cowboy is a long-term project by Dragana Jurišić that follows the footsteps of cult Yugoslavian film director, Hari Džekson. Renowned for his interpretation of Wild West genre archetypes, Džekson lived a cowboy fantasy inspired by the rugged landscapes around Bijeljina in present-day Bosnia-Herzegovina. Using local people as his crew and cast, Džekson conjured worlds far from his reality and brought together people of different ethnic backgrounds, whom the Yugoslav Wars would bitterly divide.

Name: Free Brigit’s Cross-making Workshops
Description:
Celebrate Brigit’s Day, the Bank Holiday, and the coming of spring with a lovely, hands-on tradition for all ages.

Create your own Brigit’s Cross or Brídeog (Brigit doll) at two free Brigit’s cross-making workshops (2pm and 3pm) – a beautiful symbol of Irish heritage, protection, and new beginnings. Your creation is yours to take home and enjoy.

Both workshops will be the same, so simply choose the time that suits you best. All materials will be provided, and no previous experience needed – just come along and join in.

This is a relaxed, family-friendly event and everyone is welcome. No booking required and included with general admission.

Come celebrate Brigit, a favourite tradition, and the return of brighter days together.
Address: Brigit's Garden & Café, Pollagh, Rosscahill, Galway

Name: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
Description:
Joel is shocked to discover that the love of his life, Clementine, has had him erased from her memory, after their relationship turned sour. Hurt and angry, Joel decides to pay her back by undergoing the same medical procedure.

But as his memories of Clementine start to fade, will Joel have second thoughts? And is it too late to change his mind?

Directed by Michel Gondry | 2004 | Cert: 15A

Name: Dry January Festival 2026
Description:
Throughout January, there are 120+ (and counting) plus events designed to help people stay social, meet new people, and have fun without alcohol being at the centre.

The events take place in a mix of spaces. Some are held in pubs, others in art studios, pottery studio, venues and even outdoors.  They include hikes, art classes, charity shop crawls, pottery, speed friending, and lots more. People are not forced to avoid alcohol at these events and can have a drink if they choose. The aim is simply to encourage people to socialise sober, or at least without alcohol being the main focus.
Address: Dublin

What to Expect:

Curated Thrift Stops: Visit Dublin’s best thrift stores, charity shops, and vintage boutiques, handpicked for their unique finds and affordable prices.

Interactive Experience: Shop, share, and create alongside like-minded fashion lovers in a fun, welcoming group environment.

Name: Eddi Reader
Description:
Celebrating over 40 years as a live performer, Eddi Reader has effortlessly developed into one of popular music’s most thrilling and affecting performers. Though first brought into the limelight as front woman for Fairground Attraction, whose #1 single, ‘Perfect’, topped the British charts, it is Eddi’s subsequent albums that have signalled her ability to assimilate different musical styles and make them her own. No two performances are ever the same.

Name: Happy Birthday Mr. Joyce! Free Admission to the James Joyce Centre
Description:
James Joyce was born on 2nd February 1882. As a birthday gift, the James Joyce Centre will be open to the public FOR FREE on Monday, 2nd February from 10.30am to 4.30pm. Browse our exhibitions in our beautiful 18th century Georgian townhouse as we raise a glass to James Joyce and to those he brings together.

See the famous door of 7 Eccles Street. Explore Joyce’s Dublin in Modality of the Visible: Ulysses VR. Write your own poetry inspired by Leopold Bloom Bloom, Haiku, and the Turning of Peace. And so much more!

We will also be running our popular walking tours FOR FREE. Booking is required and spaces are limited.

Celebrate with family, friends, and fellow travellers on this special day. Who knows, there might even be a cake.
Address: James Joyce Centre, James Joyce House, 35 N Great George's St, Rotunda, Basement, Dublin

Name: How To Start Your Own Food Business | Dry January
Description:
Ever want to start your own food business or learn about the industry? Do you have an idea for something?

Name: High Rise [Ben Wheatley Season]
Description: 1975. Two miles west of London, Dr. Robert Laing moves into his new apartment seeking soulless anonymity, only to find that the building’s residents have no intention of leaving him alone. Resigned to the complex social dynamics unfolding around him, Laing bites the bullet and becomes neighbourly. As he struggles to establish his position, Laing’s good manners and sanity disintegrate along with the building. The lights go out and the lifts fail but the party goes on. People are the problem. Booze is the currency. Sex is the panacea. Only much later, as he sits on his balcony eating the architect’s dog, does Dr. Robert Laing finally feel at home… HIGH-RISE is an adaptation of J.G. Ballard’s controversial novel.

Name: Dragana Jurišić 'The Last Balkan Cowboy'
Description:
The Last Balkan Cowboy is a long-term project by Dragana Jurišić that follows the footsteps of cult Yugoslavian film director, Hari Džekson. Renowned for his interpretation of Wild West genre archetypes, Džekson lived a cowboy fantasy inspired by the rugged landscapes around Bijeljina in present-day Bosnia-Herzegovina. Using local people as his crew and cast, Džekson conjured worlds far from his reality and brought together people of different ethnic backgrounds, whom the Yugoslav Wars would bitterly divide.

Name: Daily Lough Ree & River Shannon Cruise
Description:
Departing from Athlone Castle, this cruise travels upstream on the River Shannon to Lough Ree. During the sailing there is commentary on local history, the religious settlements on the islands and tales of Viking treasure. This is a boat trip that can be enjoyed by all ages and is suitable for any family occasion. Don a Viking helmet and prepare for battle or simply sit back with an Irish Coffee. Either way, you will enjoy the beauty of the Inland Waterways and witness everything Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands has to offer.

Check the website for the latest sailing times. Tickets available onboard. 

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Viking Tours Ireland, Castle St, Athlone, Westmeath

Name: St Brigid's Weekend at Collins Barracks
Description:
Take Symbols of Ireland to discover the fascinating range of symbols used to represent Ireland through time, and The Way We Wore to learn about how the clothes we wear reflect our lives and society, with a focus on the history of women's changing roles in Ireland. Learn about the lives and actions of women at the centre of Ireland's revolutionary past in Revolutionary Women.

Booking not required, however spaces are limited and offered on a first-come, first-served basis. Children must be supervised at all times while on the premises.

See Collins Barracks website for tour information.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks, Collins Barracks, Benburb St, Stoneybatter, Dublin
