Name: Stories from Ireland’s Eye – Howth Cliff Cruises
Description:
The experience explores the island’s history, geology, birdlife, the Martello Tower and the tragic murder of Maria Kirwan on Ireland’s Eye, all set against the scenic coastline of Howth and Dublin Bay.

Discover one of Dublin Bay’s most intriguing islands with Stories from Ireland’s Eye, a unique storytelling boat tour from Howth Cliff Cruises. This special sailing offers a deeper look at Ireland’s Eye, blending dramatic coastal scenery with the fascinating stories that have shaped the island over centuries.

As you cruise around the island, expert crew members bring Ireland’s Eye to life through a series of carefully curated tales. Learn how ancient rock formations were shaped by time and tide, hear about the seabirds that have made the island their home, and uncover the history of the striking Martello Tower that still stands proudly above the shoreline. The tour also explores the island’s more haunting past, including the tragic true story of a murder that took place on Ireland’s Eye, adding an atmospheric edge to this unforgettable experience.

Set against panoramic views of Howth Head, Dublin Bay and the Irish Sea, Stories from Ireland’s Eye combines history, nature and storytelling in a way that appeals to curious travellers, locals and visitors alike. Whether you’re interested in maritime history, wildlife or Dublin’s lesser-known stories, this engaging tour offers a fresh perspective on one of the capital’s most iconic coastal landmarks.
Address: West Pier, W Pier, Howth Demesne, Dublin

Name: Spring Dublin Mind Body Experience
Description:
Ireland's largest holistic event returns to Hall 4, RDS, Dublin on the 27th, 28th February & the 1st of March 2026.

Free Admission, 190 Exhibitors, and 160 Complimentary Talks, Workshops & Classes. The event is comprised of four different elements all in one venue, the Mind Body Soul Expo, the Yoga and Meditation Festival, the Dublin Wellness Expo and the Dublin Psychic Fair.

Doors Friday 27th Feb, 16:00 - 20:00, Saturday 28th Feb & Sunday 1st March, 10:00 - 19:00

For More information visit the Dublinmindbodyexperience website.

Set against panoramic views of Howth Head, Dublin Bay and the Irish Sea, Stories from Ireland’s Eye combines history, nature and storytelling in a way that appeals to curious travellers, locals and visitors alike. Whether you’re interested in maritime history, wildlife or Dublin’s lesser-known stories, this engaging tour offers a fresh perspective on one of the capital’s most iconic coastal landmarks.

Name: Rediscover Dublin, Rediscover EPIC
Description:
For centuries, the story of Ireland has been one of journeys — of millions who left their homeland in search of survival, opportunity, and new beginnings. Their experiences shaped not only the nations they settled in, but also the wider world, leaving a legacy felt in music, sport, dance, politics, art, and innovation. From famine and faith to fame and fortune, the Irish spirit has carried resilience, creativity, and joy across every continent.

Entry into EPIC includes admission to our latest temporary exhibition, Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad, as well as all ticket holders to return to the museum within 10 days of their visit, free of charge.

Use code REDISCOVERDUBLIN20 for 20% off your visit.
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Sofia City Ballet Swan Lake
Description:
For the very first time, the Internationally recognised Sofia City Ballet will perform with pleasure its critically acclaimed production of Swan Lake at The National Opera House, Wexford. Set to Tchaikovsky’s delightful score, Swan Lake is one of the most famous and best loved Ballets in the world.

The Sofia City Ballet’s production of Swan Lake is a magical combination of drama and tragedy and the perpetual battle between good and evil. From the splendour of the palace Ballroom to the moon lit lake where Swans glide in perfect formation.

Through the use of sumptuous costumes, wonderful traditional stage sets and decorations, dedication and artistic excellence, this classic Ballet is brought to life right before your very eyes.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Speedfriending | Dry January
Description:
THIS IS NOT SPEED DATING - THIS IS SPEED FRIENDING

Join us at the Dublin Speedfriending Festival in Dublin.

New to Dublin? Or just want to meet some new faces in a friendly place? THIS meetup is for you.

Meet loads of new people in one day 👍
Expect music & prizes.
Expect to meet people of all ages from different countries.😃
This event will have a MC with different tables to ensure you get to know as many people as possible.

We've ran this event lots of times before and average attendance is 30-50.

We started this event in 2019 and have had hundreds of people attend.

Tickets are non-refundable.

Please note this is an event we created to help people make new friends in Dublin. This is not for dating.
Address: Lost Lane, 1-2, Lost Lane, Adam Court, Grafton Street, Dublin

Bringing belly laughs and brilliantly bonkers characters to stages across Ireland and beyond, The Shortt’s– an irreverent and endearing Irish father-daughter sketch comedy act – are redefining what it means to be a comedic double act.Pat Shortt, a veteran of the comedy circuit, and his daughter Faye Shortt, a theatre trained firecracker with a razor-sharp wit.

Name: Shane Todd - Hold me Back
Description:
Join rising comedy star Shane Todd live at the Gleneagle Club on Friday, 30 January 2026 for his highly anticipated new stand-up show Hold Me Back (doors 7:30pm, show 8:30pm).

Known for his quick-fire jokes, hilarious observations and magnetic stage presence, Shane has quickly become one of Ireland’s most exciting stand-ups. In Hold Me Back, he dives into everything from modern life’s absurdities to awkward social moments, family chaos and the universal struggle of trying to keep it all together.

Expect a night packed with laugh-out-loud storytelling, clever humour and interactive crowd moments that’ll have you smiling from start to finish. This is live comedy at its best — raw, relatable and unforgettable!

Name: The Knitted Bible Project
Description:
We love stories for entertainment, learning, and inspiration. The Bible is a book full of stories, and this exhibition presents a small selection through the Knitted Bible. Each piece has been chosen and designed by its maker, creating an organic and creative exploration of familiar and lesser-known biblical stories. Experiencing these narratives through knitted and textile artworks offers a fresh perspective and invites reflection and curiosity. 

The Knitted Bible exhibition is available during cathedral opening hours and is included in the standard admission price. Please see our website for opening times and further information.

Name: The House Must Win
Description:
On the western edge of Ireland, in the 1970s, two brothers live with the odds stacked against them. They take separate paths in a bid to lead the ’good’ life, and try to honour the girl who has both their hearts. 
The people of this town must figure out how to play the cards they’ve been dealt, in a world of backroom poker-playing, secrets and bribes, broken families and broken hearts. 

Starring Tommy Tiernan, Tabitha Smith and more, it is directed by Julie Kelleher, with Liam Robinson (Hadestown) as Music Supervisor and Ciarán Bagnall on Set and Lighting Design. The creative team has worked closely with Mick over the past years, shaping the world he has envisioned through this new script and its twenty-one songs.

Check website for times.
Address: 15 MacCurtain Street, Victorian Quarter, Cork

The Knitted Bible exhibition is available during cathedral opening hours and is included in the standard admission price. Please see our website for opening times and further information.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Christ Church Cathedral, Christchurch Pl, Wood Quay, Dublin

Name: The Best of Cork - Winter Special Series
Description:
Acts will include Paul Crowley, Micheal The B Roche, Byron Murphy, Mark O'Keeffe, Leslie Martinez, Aaron McCarthy, Aaron Chandler, Kevin O'Connell, Ross O'D and many more.
  
City Limits Comedy Club now heading towards its 33rd year, is known to be one of the best comedy clubs in Ireland and is well respected all over the comedy world world.     

City Limits is the perfect size to see a comedy gig, big enough for 200 people, but still intimate enough that you can still see the passion in the comics eyes, come in and see for yourself this great club where comedians such as Tommy Tiernan, Dara O Briain, Colm Murphy and Jason Byrne began their comedy careers.  
    
 "One of the main pillars of Irish stand up comedy"
Address: City Limits - The Cork Comedy Club, Corks Biggest and Longest Running Comedy Club (EST 1993 ), Cork Comedy Club, City Limits, 16 Coburg St, Victorian Quarter, Cork

Name: The Cunning Little Vixen
Description:
Seen through the eyes of a young fox, The Cunning Little Vixen opens with a chance encounter that results in her capture. Through her courage and resilience, the Vixen manages to outwit her captors, and escapes to untamed freedom in the forest. 

Janáček’s opera is no fairy tale, but rather a reflection on the cycle of life. His evocative score weaves rich folk melodies with playful humour, capturing the sounds of nature’s eternal rhythm and its wild, irrepressible spirit. 
American soprano Amber Norelai stars as the Vixen, with Irish soprano Jade Phoenix (Die Fledermaus, 2025) and Irish baritone Benjamin Russell. Directed by Sophie Motley (Werther, 2023), it features the Irish National Opera orchestra and chorus.
Age 12A.
Address: 15 MacCurtain Street, Victorian Quarter, Cork

Name: The Celine Experience
Description:
With iconic replica costumes, breath-taking vocals, this show is like no other, it will take you on a mesmerising ride through Celine Dion's life's works and career as we travel through more four decades of hits both released and covered.

Packed with Worldwide platinum selling tracks including ‘It’s All Coming Back To Me Now’, ‘I’m Alive’, ‘Think Twice’, ‘My Heart will On’, ‘That’s The Way It Is’, ‘The Power Of Love’, ‘River Deep, Mountain High’ and many more hit songs and arrangements, in multiple languages performed by January.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: University Concert Hall (UCH), Foundation Building, Sreelane, Limerick

Name: Sunday Family Drop-in Workshop: Cubist Creations
Description:
Sunday Family Drop-in is a weekly inclusive, accessible, intergenerational workshop. Devised to support creative engagement for children of all ages and abilities, these sessions are led by an invited artist and supported by the Education Team. 

All materials are provided, support is available in English or as Gaeilge, but as activities are rooted in creative expression, with visual cues, models and examples, children of all nationalities can participate fully. 

Free, no booking required. Stay for as long (or as little) as you like.
Families with children of all ages welcome.
All materials provided.
Languages spoken: Irish and English.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Street Art of Dublin | Walking Tour
Description:
Every day we walk past a secret language that every modern city has become fluent in. Whether you are new to graffiti, or simply new to the Dublin scene, our tour is the perfect place to get to know the local talent.

As far back as human beings have had walls to draw on, we’ve been doing it. From cave-painters to street-taggers, Graffiti has a universal attraction and a long love-hate story with city officials. Come delve into the details of Dublin’s graffiti scene and find out just how fuzzy the line can be between the legal murals and ‘illegal’ graffiti that colour the Hibernian Metropolis.

Our tour has over 100 excellent reviews online and is ranked in the top tours on Trip Advisor.
Address: The Grand Social, 35 Liffey St. Lower, North City, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Talk: Animal Care in Ireland
Description:
Join Dr. Michael Doherty for a special talk from 3pm to 4pm on Saturday, 7 February 2026, exploring the fascinating folklore of animal care in Ireland.

Drawing on his recent research, Dr. Doherty will present ‘The Folklore of Cattle and Sheep through an Ethnoveterinary Lens’.

This event is in association with Antique Veterinary Objects, an exhibition of selected objects from a donation dealing with animal husbandry and veterinary science now on display.

Free admission, booking required.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park, Turlough Park House, Gortnafolla, Castlebar, Mayo

Name: Talk: They Walked These Hills Before Us: Ice Age Wildlife
Description:
Do you ever wonder what kinds of ancient animals lived on your doorstep thousands of years ago?

Join Michael Chambers from Wild Nephin National Park for a look back at the wildlife that walked through the valleys of the Wild Nephin National Park and how the landscape has changed since the end of the last Ice Age.

Find out about Ireland's Natural History and more about our brown bear on display.

Admission free, booking required.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park, Turlough Park House, Gortnafolla, Castlebar, Mayo

Name: Talk: Take me to the Church: Love & Marriage in the Calendar Year
Description:
Join keeper of the Irish Folklife Collection, Clodagh Doyle, this St Valentine's Day (14 February) to find out about matchmaking, marriage and the traditions in between!

The celebration of St Valentine’s Day is a modern construct, but love and marriage customs have traditionally played a significant role in Irish society.

Learn more at this talk from 2pm to 3pm on Saturday, 14 February 2026.

Free admission, booking required. For adults & ages 14+yrs

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park, Turlough Park House, Gortnafolla, Castlebar, Mayo

Name: The Drifter Tribute Show
Description:
Enjoy an evening celebrating the timeless sound of The Drifters, with smooth harmonies, Motown classics, and much-loved hits that shaped a generation. Expect soulful vocals, familiar melodies, and a warm, uplifting atmosphere perfect for singing along. This lively live show promises a great night out for music lovers of all ages. Tickets are available from Eventbrite.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Courtmacsherry, Cork

Name: The House Must Win
Description:
On the western edge of Ireland, in the 1970s, two brothers live with the odds stacked against them. They take separate paths in a bid to lead the ’good’ life, and try to honour the girl who has both their hearts. 
The people of this town must figure out how to play the cards they’ve been dealt, in a world of backroom poker-playing, secrets and bribes, broken families and broken hearts. 

Starring Tommy Tiernan, Tabitha Smith and more, it is directed by Julie Kelleher, with Liam Robinson (Hadestown) as Music Supervisor and Ciarán Bagnall on Set and Lighting Design. The creative team has worked closely with Mick over the past years, shaping the world he has envisioned through this new script and its twenty-one songs.

Check website for times.
Address: 15 MacCurtain Street, Victorian Quarter, Cork

Name: The High Kings
Description:
Don’t miss The High Kings live in concert at the Gleneagle Arena on Thursday, 30 January 2026 (doors 7 PM, show 8 PM). Celebrated for their powerhouse harmonies, captivating stage presence and modern spin on traditional Irish folk, this acclaimed ensemble has thrilled audiences across the world with their blend of classic songs and vibrant arrangements.

Expect rich vocal blend and storytelling through beloved folk songs and beloved anthems that span generations. The High Kings consistently deliver memorable, high-energy performances that connect deeply with fans of all ages — whether it’s stirring ballads or rousing sing-alongs.

This is a night designed for lovers of Irish music and great live entertainment.
Address: Gleneagle Arena (INEC), Muckross Rd, Poulnamuck, Killarney, Kerry

Name: Rediscover Dublin, Rediscover EPIC
Description:
For centuries, the story of Ireland has been one of journeys — of millions who left their homeland in search of survival, opportunity, and new beginnings. Their experiences shaped not only the nations they settled in, but also the wider world, leaving a legacy felt in music, sport, dance, politics, art, and innovation. From famine and faith to fame and fortune, the Irish spirit has carried resilience, creativity, and joy across every continent.

Entry into EPIC includes admission to our latest temporary exhibition, Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad, as well as all ticket holders to return to the museum within 10 days of their visit, free of charge.

Use code REDISCOVERDUBLIN20 for 20% off your visit.
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: January Tours of Collins Barracks
Description:
In commemoration of the meeting of the First Dáil on 21st January 1919, join us to learn about the men and women at the centre of Ireland's revolutionary history. Celebrate the harmonious history of music through the National Museum's collection in our Music Makers tours. 

Taking place on various days throughout January, please see NMI website for details on dates and times.

Tours will begin in Reception at Collins Barracks. Booking is not required. However, places are limited and will be offered on a first-come, first-served basis.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks, Collins Barracks, Benburb St, Stoneybatter, Dublin

Name: January 'Prehistoric Ireland' Tours
Description:
On this tour, discover Mesolithic fish-traps, food of Neolithic Ireland, wonderful gold objects of the Irish Bronze Age, and artefacts from Iron Age Ireland. This tour weaves through the changes and continuity of prehistoric Ireland by using key objects from the Museum’s collection to inform us about the people and changes of each period.

No booking required, places allocated on a first come first served basis. Meet in the Reception, on the ground floor. Families with children 7+. Children must be fully supervised by a parent or adult who is in loco parentis.

Bring the family for a fun, hands-on activity exploring nature—no booking required (short wait possible). Allow our team of storytellers to transport you back to a nostalgic time where social media was non-existent and tea and chats reigned supreme. Swap the January Blues for Dublin Views!

This is Afternoon Tea with a Difference. This is Afternoon Tea the Irish Way.

Running throughout January on Thursdays – Mondays with up to 6 tours a day.

Suitable for children.

Vegetarian, vegan, gluten free and dairy free available.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: St Stephen's Green, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Imelda May: Raised on Songs & Stories
Description:
Renowned for her electrifying stage presence and artistic versatility, Blending rockabilly, jazz, rock and blues, Imelda has toured across the world and performed with legends including Jeff Beck, Lou Reed, Bono, Noel Gallagher, Jeff Goldblum and Ronnie Wood.

Imelda May has evolved from her rockabilly roots to a more soulful, poetic sound, earning international praise and a loyal following.

We are thrilled to welcome her back and invite you to join us for a special night of music, art, connection and creativity.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Theatre Royal Waterford, The Mall, Waterford

Name: Indo Sport Rugby LIVE
Description:
Joining them for this special live event will be long-time teammates Luke Fitzgerald & Ian Madigan, as well as former Ireland head coach Eddie O’Sullivan.

Expect an entertaining night of incisive insight and frank analysis around the tournament ahead, as well as a look back at the panel’s own colourful Ireland careers.

For ages 18+ only.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Helix, Collins Ave, Whitehall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Ireland and the Academy: Art History Reframed - Spring 2026
Description:
Art Historian Dr Matthew Whyte offers a new lecture series, which takes the audience on an art-filled journey through the often beautiful, sometimes scandalous, and always fascinating moments in the development of Western civilisation.

The lectures can be attended as a series, but are also designed as standalone talks, which can be attended individually.

This lecture will look at how in the 1600s Irish artists drew on the prevailing academic tradition to create a distinctly Irish identity.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Inland Empire [From the Mind of David Lynch Season]
Description:
Nikki Grace is an actress preparing for her biggest role, a Hollywood movie from an acclaimed director opposite an amorous leading man. When she finds herself falling for her co-star, she realises her life is starting to mimic the fictional film they are shooting. David Lynch’s final feature film is a complex Hollywood nightmare and a surrealist masterwork. 

David Lynch, one of the most original, singular and unique artists of American cinema and television in the last fifty years, sadly died last year. 20th January 2026 would have been his 80th birthday. The term ‘Lynchian’ is used to describe something that has a dreamlike quality with an air of mystery or menace. His work was also laced with a wonderfully off-the-wall sense of humour.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: How to Tackle Your Finances with Eoin McGee
Description:
But it will also look at how you can get your money working harder for you, how you can tackle troublesome debt, and how you can prepare yourself for either your first mortgage or to switch providers.

This event is for you if you want to start being good with money, and you want all the tools needed to get there.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Helix, Collins Ave, Whitehall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: How To Start Your Own Food Business
Description:
Ever want to start your own food business or learn about the industry? Do you have an idea for something?

Join us for a 90-minute masterclass with chef Seany McCleary from Blasta Street Kitchen and Street School.

Sean is a serial entrepreneur and has spent over 15 years working in the food industry. He has broken a world record, been featured on RTE and won numerous awards.

In this masterclass, he will run through his own experiences and provide valuable insights.

After the event there will be a Q&A with the participants where Sean will answer any questions.

Free tea and coffee is included at the event and biscuits too.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: This Must Be The Place, 42 Arran Quay, Smithfield, Dublin

Name: Imbolc 2026 Fair
Description:
From 11:00–17:00, expect music, making, food, stories and hands-on experiences rooted in tradition and reimagined for today – alongside delicious foods and hot drinks.

Drop in for an hour or spend the full afternoon — this is a joyful, inclusive celebration of light, creativity and seasonal change for all ages.

See Dublin.ie website for full programme of activities (charges may apply).

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Meeting House Square, Temple Bar, Dublin

Name: Imbolc Disco with Mockie Ah!
Description:
Audience sing-alongs and games with Mia Gold and Candy plus prizes for best dressed on the night so bring your sparkle to the dancefloor. From Studio 54 to your favourite Irish icons, we want to see it all!

Sunday 1 February from 8pm 'til late.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Levis Corner House, Main St, Ballydehob, Cork

Name: Imbolc at Hill of Uisneach
Description:
Follow the ancient pathways, pause at story stations, and learn how this landscape has marked the turning of the year for thousands of years. Discover Brigid as goddess and saint, keeper of flame, protector of home, animals, and a symbol of new beginnings.

At the summit, gather around the intentions fire, where you will be invited to reflect, release what no longer serves you, and set a hope for the season ahead.

A gentle pilgrimage through myth, landscape, and light - marking the moment when winter loosens her grip and the land begins to wake.

We will then return to the visitor centre for refreshments and music around the fire.

All this is happening on Bank Holiday Monday the 2nd February 2026 at 11.00 am.
Address: The Hill of Uisneach, Loughnavalley, Rathnew, Westmeath

Name: Irish Art in the Age of Enlightenment: Art History Reframed - Spring 2026
Description:
Art Historian Dr Matthew Whyte offers a new lecture series, which takes the audience on an art-filled journey through the often beautiful, sometimes scandalous, and always fascinating moments in the development of Western civilisation.

The lectures can be attended as a series, but are also designed as standalone talks, which can be attended individually.

This lecture explores Irish art in the Age of Enlightenment, a period shaped by new ideas about reason, education, and cultural exchange in the late eighteenth and early nineteenth centuries. This week will illuminate how Irish artists engaged with Enlightenment ideals while negotiating questions of identity, place, and modernity.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Jane Austen Wrecked My Life
Description:

Name: Jason Byrne: Head in the Clouds
Description:
Join “The Outright King of Live Comedy” on his daily commute between having his feet firmly on the ground and his head floating in the clouds. There’s enough crap going on in the world so why not stick your head in the clouds with Jason for a couple of hours.

Tonight’s forecast is cloudy with a chance of fluffy mayhem.

“There is nobody else on the planet who makes me laugh like that. Your world will be a better place after seeing Jason live”. – The Newcastle Chronicle.

‘Full on, doubled up, gasping for oxygen, hilarious’ – Chortle.

Name: Rediscover Dublin, Rediscover EPIC
Description:
For centuries, the story of Ireland has been one of journeys — of millions who left their homeland in search of survival, opportunity, and new beginnings. Their experiences shaped not only the nations they settled in, but also the wider world, leaving a legacy felt in music, sport, dance, politics, art, and innovation. From famine and faith to fame and fortune, the Irish spirit has carried resilience, creativity, and joy across every continent.

Entry into EPIC includes admission to our latest temporary exhibition, Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad, as well as all ticket holders to return to the museum within 10 days of their visit, free of charge.

Use code REDISCOVERDUBLIN20 for 20% off your visit.
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Use code REDISCOVERDUBLIN20 for 20% off your visit.
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Lovesong by Abi Morgan
Description:
Their past and present selves collide in this beautiful tale of togetherness. All relationships have their ups and downs; the optimism of youth becomes the wisdom of experience.

Love is a leap of faith.

Run time 70mins, no interval / Ages 16+yrs

Tickets €23.50 / €21.50 conc

Name: Mack Fleetwood
Description:
Mack Fleetwood bewitch their audience with authentic renditions of all your favourite classic Fleetwood Mac hits. Their love of the material and musical prowess shines through each song.

Europe’s premier tribute to the supergroup Fleetwood Mac, brings you on a magical musical journey from the Peter Green blues era to the multi-platinum selling band that still tours today.

Mack Fleetwood deliver their professional spellbinding show with energy and verve. They are selling out major music venues, enthralling crowds and receiving rave reviews wherever they go.

Book early to be sure not to miss this opportunity for a truly brilliant time with one of the best tribute bands ever.
Address: VISUAL Carlow, Old Dublin Rd, Carlow

Name: Mikel Murphy Trilogy
Description:
The three shows follow the story of cobbler Pat Farnon’s journey in a most unexpected way.... A tender, heartfelt and joyously funny trilogy by one of Ireland’s most accomplished physical theatre performers.

Shows at 1pm, 4pm and 8pm

One show €20/ Two shows €35 / Three shows €50

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Mayo Genealogy Group
Description:
Get some help to research your family tree with the Mayo Genealogy Group.

Find out how to get started, or get some tips and advice on taking the next steps with an existing project. Join the friendly members of the Mayo Genealogy Group from 11:30 to 13:00 on Saturday, 14 February 2026.

This is a voluntary group brought together by a keen interest in genealogy. The members enjoy sharing their successes and pitfalls, collective experience and detective work to give other researchers a guiding hand. New researchers are welcome. No booking required.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Turlough Park, Turlough Park House, Gortnafolla, Castlebar, Mayo

Name: Johnny McEvoy, A Nostalgic Evening of Song & Story
Description:
Johnny has always been highly acclaimed for the quality of his live concert shows and when his Autumn 2025 Tour was announced it received a huge response from his legion of fans nationwide as with Covid, and its consequences, he hasn’t toured for the past number of years.

His current live show is called A Nostalgic Evening of ‘Song and Story’ and features all of Johnny’s ‘Hit’ songs, including Mursheen Durkin, Boston Burglar, Long before your Time, Rich Man’s Garden, Michael Collins etc, many of these songs penned by Johnny.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Theatre Royal Waterford, The Mall, Waterford

Name: Jeffrey Martin
Description:
He produced and engineered it himself, recalling, “There was a magic quality to the sounds I was getting in the shack with these two cheap microphones, some lucky recipe of time and place that allowed my voice and the way I play guitar and the shape of these new songs to come together with the kind of honesty I was craving.”

For ages 18+ only.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Whelan's, 25 Wexford St, Portobello, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair
Description:
Quentin Tarantino’s KILL BILL: THE WHOLE BLOODY AFFAIR unites Volume 1 and Volume 2 into a single epic – presented as he intended, complete with a new, never-before-seen anime sequence.

Uma Thurman stars as The Bride, left for dead after her former boss and lover Bill ambushes her wedding rehearsal, shooting her in the head and stealing her unborn child. To exact her vengeance, she must first hunt down the four remaining members of the Deadly Viper Assassination Squad before confronting Bill himself. With its operatic scope, relentless action, and iconic style, this is one of cinema’s definitive revenge sagas, rarely shown in its complete form and now presented with a classic intermission.

Listed runtime includes a 15 minute intermission.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: LGBTQIA+ Tour of Collins Barracks
Description:
This new guided LGBTQIA+ tour views the exhibitions through alternative lenses, bringing some hidden histories and perspectives to the fore. Focusing on objects and individuals from the 17th to the 21st century, the tour reveals a wide variety of identities, genders and sexualities along the way.

Sat 7th Feb with tours at 14:00 & 15:30.

Tours will begin in Reception at Collins Barracks. Admission is free and booking is not required. However, places are limited and will be offered on a first-come, first-served basis. Children must be fully supervised by a parent or adult who is in loco parentis.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks
Address: National Museum of Ireland, Collins Barracks, Collins Barracks, Benburb St, Stoneybatter, Dublin

Name: Monastic Ireland: Art History Reframed - Spring 2026
Description:
Art Historian Dr Matthew Whyte offers a new lecture series, which takes the audience on an art-filled journey through the often beautiful, sometimes scandalous, and always fascinating moments in the development of Western civilisation.

The lectures can be attended as a series, but are also designed as standalone talks, which can be attended individually.

In this lecture, we explore the history of Monastic Ireland, examining the visual culture associated with sites such as the Abbey of Kells and other centres of early Christian learning. The lecture considers how trade, travel, and conquest during the Viking raids fostered patterns of visual exchange between Ireland and neighbouring regions, shaping artistic forms, materials, and motifs.
Address: Triskel Arts Centre, Tobin St, Centre, Cork

Name: Paddy - The Life & Times of Paddy Armstrong
Description:
Don Wycherley brings unflinching honesty, pathos and humour to his portrayal of Paddy, a man failed and scarred by a flawed system yet refusing to be defined by it.

Paddy is a testament to the resilience of the human spirit and the power of forgiveness, reminding us that the privilege of freedom and love, family and everyday life can restore us and mend the scars of even the most savage injustice.

Inspired by Paddy Armstrong’s memoir Life After Life, a Guildford Four Memoir.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Theatre Royal Waterford, The Mall, Waterford

Name: One Night of Adele
Description:
One Night of Adele is more than just a tribute—it’s an experience, capturing the magic, the emotion, and the powerhouse vocals of one of the greatest artists of our time.

From the heartbreaking ballads to the anthems that make you want to dance, One Night of Adele brings every iconic hit to life, including Hello, Someone Like You, Rolling In The Deep, Easy On Me, and many more. With remarkable attention to detail, Naomi Johnson embodies Adele’s signature style—the hair, the dresses, the wit, and, most importantly, that unmistakable voice.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: University Concert Hall (UCH), Foundation Building, Sreelane, Limerick

Name: QUIET HOUR: Sensory Friendly Time at the Museum
Description:
Join us for a dedicated Quiet Hour on Saturday, 31 January, 10:00–11:00, designed to create a calmer environment for visitors who benefit from reduced sensory input. Lighting and audio-visual effects will be softened where possible, with signage for any unavoidable noise.

Our staff are trained to support neurodivergent visitors, and sensory aids (e.g., headphones, sunglasses) are welcome. While we don’t have quiet rooms, outdoor spaces are available for breaks.

Explore mindfulness resources, Museum Trails, and download our Visual Guide and Sensory Map. Synge & Byrne Café will also reduce noise and lighting.

Description:
After eccentric actor Daniel and his wife divorce, he is left seeing his children only once a week. When Daniel’s ex-wife places an ad for a nanny, he disguises himself as an older British woman in order to get the job and spend more time with his children. Name: Picasso: From the Studio
Description:
The exhibition places Picasso in the context of his studios, highlighting the various facets and phases of his art and life. It will explore the key locations that defined him, from his arrival in Paris at the start of the twentieth century to his studio in Mas Notre-Dame de Vie (1961-1973) in Mougins.

The exhibition is organised by the National Gallery of Ireland in collaboration with the Musée national Picasso-Paris. 

Proudly supported by KPMG Ireland, Exhibition Partner.

Name: Guided History Tour & Irish Coffee Demonstration
Description:
You will be guided throughout our incredibly preserved building which has been serving the local community in Dublin for over 300 years. From opening as the first galleried church in Dublin, the marriage of Arthur Guinness in 1761, George Frideric Handel practicing The Massiah on our Renatus Harris Organ up to modern times and its conversion into The Church Café Bar.

Your tour will finish off with a demonstration on how to make the perfect Irish Coffee!

Bookings are required for this tour which you can book online. The cost of this tour is €20 per person, a pre-payment in required. The tour is available in Irish or English.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Church Café Bar, Jervis St, North City, Dublin, Dublin

You will be guided throughout our incredibly preserved building which has been serving the local community in Dublin for over 300 years. From opening as the first galleried church in Dublin, the marriage of Arthur Guinness in 1761, George Frideric Handel practicing The Massiah on our Renatus Harris Organ up to modern times and its conversion into The Church Café Bar.

Your tour will finish off with a demonstration on how to make the perfect Irish Coffee!

Bookings are required for this tour which you can book online. The cost of this tour is €20 per person, a pre-payment in required. The tour is available in Irish or English.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Church Café Bar, Jervis St, North City, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Picasso: From the Studio
Description:
The exhibition places Picasso in the context of his studios, highlighting the various facets and phases of his art and life. It will explore the key locations that defined him, from his arrival in Paris at the start of the twentieth century to his studio in Mas Notre-Dame de Vie (1961-1973) in Mougins.

The exhibition is organised by the National Gallery of Ireland in collaboration with the Musée national Picasso-Paris. 

Proudly supported by KPMG Ireland, Exhibition Partner.

See National Gallery of Ireland website for details on dates / times / ticket prices.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: NYF Dublin 2025
Description:
What better way to say hello to 2026 than by attending one of the largest New Year’s Eve parties on the island of Ireland? The New Year’s Festival Dublin is a thrilling countdown experience filled with family friendly public events, incredible live performances and dazzling light shows.

This year’s festival promises an unforgettable experience as the city comes alive with music, entertainment, and a truly magical atmosphere. Bringing together some of the country’s best talents, the festival offers a host of music and entertainment guaranteed to entertain, with something for all ages.

​​Iconic Dublin venues Dublin Castle and Meeting House Square will host a thrilling lineup of events with Inhaler, BIIRD, Florence Road, Kila, Cliffords, Chasing Abbey, Madra Salach, Simple Things, Chubby Cat, Mundy, DUG, The Wran and The Awning and more all set to perform across the 3 day festival. The programme will also see the return of the NYF Dublin Fireworks Spectacular, with firework displays taking place in two locations across Dublin, in partnership with Fingal County Council and Dun Laoghaire-Rathdown County Council (Subject to licence).

Prepare to be captivated by the Echoes of Ireland Concerts, feel the excitement of the Countdown Concert with Inhaler and the amazing Midnight Moment celebration, enjoy the special early Countdown Concert Matinee with BIIRD, Florence Road and DJ Shelly Gray, explore the city and discover new music with the NYF Dublin Music Trail, be mesmerised by the dazzling  NYF Dublin Fireworks Spectacular and continue your celebrations with the New Year’s Day Concerts.
Address: Dublin

Name: Objects, War and Memory in Ireland past and present
Description:
'Objects, War and Memory in Ireland past and present' is a new exhibition at Kilmainham Gaol Museum exploring how special objects, brought to Ireland by refugees, tell stories of courage, resilience, love and hope in the face of conflict. They include items like the distinctive multi-coloured coat worn by Kateryna during the siege of Mariupol in Ukraine: a glimpse of the coat caught on camera during the Red Cross evacuation of the city told her family and friends that she had survived. 

Also featured are objects from Kilmainham Gaol’s War of Independence and Civil War collections, revealing resonances between the experiences of refugees today and experiences of war and displacement in Ireland’s recent past.
Address: Kilmainham, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Guided History Tour & Irish Coffee Demonstration
Description:
You will be guided throughout our incredibly preserved building which has been serving the local community in Dublin for over 300 years. From opening as the first galleried church in Dublin, the marriage of Arthur Guinness in 1761, George Frideric Handel practicing The Massiah on our Renatus Harris Organ up to modern times and its conversion into The Church Café Bar.

Your tour will finish off with a demonstration on how to make the perfect Irish Coffee!

Bookings are required for this tour which you can book online. The cost of this tour is €20 per person, a pre-payment in required. The tour is available in Irish or English.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Name: Ice Skating Blanchardstown
Description:
Ice Skating Blanchardstown returns to Millennium Park for the 2025/26 winter season! We are so excited to present Ireland’s most loved ice-skating experience, curated to make this Christmas one to remember!

Our rink is expertly maintained to ensure the highest quality ice for the best skating experience, so everyone can glide with ease. We can’t wait to welcome the same fantastic skaters who join us year after year with a special theme and world-class decoration!

Access the park from behind the McDonalds Drive-Thru. Each ice skating session lasts 50 minutes.

So get your skates on...we look forward to welcoming you!

Name: NYF Dublin 2025
Description:
What better way to say hello to 2026 than by attending one of the largest New Year’s Eve parties on the island of Ireland? The New Year’s Festival Dublin is a thrilling countdown experience filled with family friendly public events, incredible live performances and dazzling light shows.

This year’s festival promises an unforgettable experience as the city comes alive with music, entertainment, and a truly magical atmosphere. Bringing together some of the country’s best talents, the festival offers a host of music and entertainment guaranteed to entertain, with something for all ages.

​​Iconic Dublin venues Dublin Castle and Meeting House Square will host a thrilling lineup of events with Inhaler, BIIRD, Florence Road, Kila, Cliffords, Chasing Abbey, Madra Salach, Simple Things, Chubby Cat, Mundy, DUG, The Wran and The Awning and more all set to perform across the 3 day festival. The programme will also see the return of the NYF Dublin Fireworks Spectacular, with firework displays taking place in two locations across Dublin, in partnership with Fingal County Council and Dun Laoghaire-Rathdown County Council (Subject to licence).

Prepare to be captivated by the Echoes of Ireland Concerts, feel the excitement of the Countdown Concert with Inhaler and the amazing Midnight Moment celebration, enjoy the special early Countdown Concert Matinee with BIIRD, Florence Road and DJ Shelly Gray, explore the city and discover new music with the NYF Dublin Music Trail, be mesmerised by the dazzling  NYF Dublin Fireworks Spectacular and continue your celebrations with the New Year’s Day Concerts.
Address: Dublin

Name: IMMA Collection: Art as Agency
Description:
This ambitious exhibition allows for a rich durational experience of Ireland’s Modern and Contemporary Art Collection.
 
Through thematic, chronological, geographical, and media-based approaches, Art as Agency examines how artworks connect across time and contexts, fostering new interpretations and relevance. By interweaving historical and contemporary narratives, the exhibition invites audiences to reflect on the evolving meanings and possibilities of art in shaping our understanding of and action in the world.

Admission free. 

Tue, Thur - Sat 10:00-17:30 | Wed 11:30-17:30 | Sun/Bank Hol 12:00-17:30

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Irish Museum of Modern Art, Royal Hospital Kilmainham, Military Rd, Kilmainham, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Rose of Tralee International Festival 2026
Description:
The Rose of Tralee International Festival is one of Ireland’s largest and longest running festivals, that includes a televised competition where young Irish women from around the world are celebrated as they vie for the title of Rose of Tralee, based on the popular song of the same name. 

Every August, 32 International Roses travel from the UK, America, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, Europe, the Middle East and of course Ireland, supported by friends and family. 

The town’s streets are transformed into a feast of cultural events, including parades, live music, street entertainment, circus, carnival, fireworks, markets and traditional Irish entertainment, drawing visitors to Tralee for a diverse celebration of Irish culture.

Name: Picasso: From the Studio
Description:
The exhibition places Picasso in the context of his studios, highlighting the various facets and phases of his art and life. It will explore the key locations that defined him, from his arrival in Paris at the start of the twentieth century to his studio in Mas Notre-Dame de Vie (1961-1973) in Mougins.

The exhibition is organised by the National Gallery of Ireland in collaboration with the Musée national Picasso-Paris. 

Proudly supported by KPMG Ireland, Exhibition Partner.

See National Gallery of Ireland website for details on dates / times / ticket prices.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Picasso: From the Studio
Description:
The exhibition places Picasso in the context of his studios, highlighting the various facets and phases of his art and life. It will explore the key locations that defined him, from his arrival in Paris at the start of the twentieth century to his studio in Mas Notre-Dame de Vie (1961-1973) in Mougins.

The exhibition is organised by the National Gallery of Ireland in collaboration with the Musée national Picasso-Paris. 

Proudly supported by KPMG Ireland, Exhibition Partner.

Name: Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad Exhibition
Description:
Visitors will encounter remarkable objects and stories that reveal the responsibilities of journalism across the globe. 

Curator Dr Catherine Healy says, "Across the world, Irish journalists have worked to reveal human rights abuses and hold the powerful to account. Their reporting on conflict, famine, and hardship has shaped public opinion and provided an impetus for change. They have chronicled the lives of people on the margins and brought home the realities of inequality. In the major centres of Irish settlement, they have challenged negative stereotypes and helped immigrants adapt to their adopted homelands. And they have, in some cases, entirely refashioned what it means to be of Ireland.”
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Earagail Arts Festival
Description:
Earagail Arts Festival brings inspiring performers to this captivating European frontier, showcases artists native to the area and provides opportunities for cultural exchange, in venues from purpose built theatres and galleries, to village halls, forests and beaches, creating a unique cultural experience in the most unlikely of magical places.

Our diverse programme reaches out across the county, so wherever you are staying when you visit it us in 2026 you will be able to enjoy a feast of entertainment located in the heart of one of Ireland’s most outstanding areas of natural beauty, the hidden gem in the Irish crown.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Letterkenny, Donegal

Name: Emma Doran: Emmaculate
Description:
Irish comedy star Emma Doran is back on tour with her brand-new stand-up show Emmaculate, hitting stages across the UK and Ireland in 2025/26. Best known as a viral comic, best-selling author, and podcast host, Emma dives into the chaos of her blended family with sharp observations, outrageous stories, and laugh-out-loud moments. 

As seen on The Tommy Tiernan Show, LOL: Last One Laughing, Modern Love, and more, Emma brings her signature wit and wild energy for a night of comedy you won’t forget.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Féile Sheáin Mhic Pháidín - John McFadden Festival 2026
Description:
Féile Sheáin Mhic Pháidín–John McFadden Festival is a vibrant celebration of music, song, dance and culture, taking place February Bank Holiday weekend in the picturesque town of Newport, in the heart of the Great Western Greenway. The festival honours the legacy of John McFadden, the renowned 19th-century fiddle player born in Newport who later emigrated to Chicago. Newport comes alive with traditional music sessions, workshops, and cultural events, as local musicians, visiting artists, and the wider community gather for a weekend of festivities. 

Whether you're a traditional music enthusiast, or simply seeking authentic local culture, the festival offers an unforgettable experience in one of Mayo’s most charming and welcoming towns.
Address: Newport, Mayo

Name: Food on Foot
Description:
“To properly experience a culture one must experience their cuisine.”

Join us on a Dublin food tour that brings you on a delicious journey through one of the world’s friendliest cities, embracing Dublin’s history and local cuisine.

This Food on Foot tour focuses on the street eats enjoyed by locals every day. It’s a high-quality, authentic experience where you’ll eat just like Dubliners do.

This isn’t fine dining – no lobster or champagne. Instead, we explore the best fun street food the city has to offer! We’re all about casual, tasty, local favourites.

Come hungry – we’ll stop at five different spots for delicious street eats, including at least one dessert stop!

During Winter in Dublin 3/5 stops will have Christmas Themed Specials!
Address: St Catherine's Church of Ireland, Thomas St, The Liberties, Dublin, Dublin

Curator Dr Catherine Healy says, "Across the world, Irish journalists have worked to reveal human rights abuses and hold the powerful to account. Their reporting on conflict, famine, and hardship has shaped public opinion and provided an impetus for change. They have chronicled the lives of people on the margins and brought home the realities of inequality. In the major centres of Irish settlement, they have challenged negative stereotypes and helped immigrants adapt to their adopted homelands. And they have, in some cases, entirely refashioned what it means to be of Ireland.”
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Earagail Arts Festival
Description:
Earagail Arts Festival brings inspiring performers to this captivating European frontier, showcases artists native to the area and provides opportunities for cultural exchange, in venues from purpose built theatres and galleries, to village halls, forests and beaches, creating a unique cultural experience in the most unlikely of magical places.

Our diverse programme reaches out across the county, so wherever you are staying when you visit it us in 2026 you will be able to enjoy a feast of entertainment located in the heart of one of Ireland’s most outstanding areas of natural beauty, the hidden gem in the Irish crown.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Letterkenny, Donegal

Name: Cairde Sligo Arts Festival 2026
Description:
Cairde Sligo Arts Festival is an annual, 8-day celebration of imagination and shared experience that welcomes all. 

Inspired by the creative energy of Ireland’s Northwest, the festival brings artists and audiences together for music, theatre, dance, visual arts, spoken word and spectacle. 

From 4–11 July 2026, Sligo’s streets, theatres, galleries, landscapes and unique venues come alive with dynamic performances, site-specific works and participatory events. Cairde celebrates community, creativity and connection, creating unforgettable encounters and inspiring moments of joy throughout Sligo.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Sligo

Name: Ballydehob Traditional Music Festival 2026
Description:
Now in it's 21st year of existence, Ballydehob Traditional Music Festival has gone from strength to strength, welcoming some of the best Irish Traditional Musicians to the small village on the Mizen Head peninsula. 

The festival offers all-star concerts, and abundance of sessions and incredible welcoming experience you won't find anywhere else.  

We have been lucky enough to have bands like De Dennan, Four Men and a Dog, Danu, Goitse and many others.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Greenmount Road, Greenmount Rd, The Laurels, Cork

Name: Cork International Choral Festival 2026
Description:
Founded in 1954, the Cork International Choral Festival is one of Europe’s premier international choral festivals. It is held annually over the five days preceding the first Monday in May in venues across the city, centring around Cork City Hall, one of the most attractive and acoustically superb venues in Ireland. Overall, the Festival attracts some 5,000 participants and 50,000 people who bring Cork to life with song over the 5 days. Name: Earagail Arts Festival
Description:
Earagail Arts Festival brings inspiring performers to this captivating European frontier, showcases artists native to the area and provides opportunities for cultural exchange, in venues from purpose built theatres and galleries, to village halls, forests and beaches, creating a unique cultural experience in the most unlikely of magical places.

Our diverse programme reaches out across the county, so wherever you are staying when you visit it us in 2026 you will be able to enjoy a feast of entertainment located in the heart of one of Ireland’s most outstanding areas of natural beauty, the hidden gem in the Irish crown.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Letterkenny, Donegal

Full of wit and warmth, Dublin Gothic celebrates the glory and the grime of Dublin. In this spectacular production, more than a hundred unforgettable characters will dance through time as they make their way through life in the 20th century.

Tickets now on sale to see this glorious collection of endearing and dastardly Dubliners this winter – meet the Gatelys, the Cumminses, the Meehans, and their neighbours from the 22nd of November.

Times:
Mon – Fri 7pm / Sat matinees 1pm
Tues 23 Dec, 1pm / Sat 27 Dec, 7pm / Wed 31 Dec, 4pm

Ticket prices vary, see Abbey Theatre website for details.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Abbey Theatre, 26/27 Abbey Street Lower, North City, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Guided History Tour & Irish Coffee Demonstration
Description:
You will be guided throughout our incredibly preserved building which has been serving the local community in Dublin for over 300 years. From opening as the first galleried church in Dublin, the marriage of Arthur Guinness in 1761, George Frideric Handel practicing The Massiah on our Renatus Harris Organ up to modern times and its conversion into The Church Café Bar.

Your tour will finish off with a demonstration on how to make the perfect Irish Coffee!

Bookings are required for this tour which you can book online. The cost of this tour is €20 per person, a pre-payment in required. The tour is available in Irish or English.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Church Café Bar, Jervis St, North City, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad Exhibition
Description:
Visitors will encounter remarkable objects and stories that reveal the responsibilities of journalism across the globe. 

Curator Dr Catherine Healy says, "Across the world, Irish journalists have worked to reveal human rights abuses and hold the powerful to account. Their reporting on conflict, famine, and hardship has shaped public opinion and provided an impetus for change. They have chronicled the lives of people on the margins and brought home the realities of inequality. In the major centres of Irish settlement, they have challenged negative stereotypes and helped immigrants adapt to their adopted homelands. And they have, in some cases, entirely refashioned what it means to be of Ireland.”
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Galtee Challenge 2026
Description:
A self navigating event to cross the whole Galtee Mountain range. Participants tackle a strenuous route, typically covering about 30 kilometers and ascending over 2,000 meters, often in challenging weather conditions. The course traverses the main peaks, including Galtymore, the highest point in the range. 

It's a true test of endurance, navigation skills, and physical fitness, attracting experienced hillwalkers and offering breathtaking views of the surrounding countryside for those who conquer its rugged terrain.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Anglesborough, Lackendarragh, Anglesborough, Limerick

Name: Gaelforce Great River Swim
Description:
Starting upstream on the River Shannon. You will be carried by the gentle current through its sweeping bends to the finish line in Lanesborough. Throughout your swim, you’ll enjoy a safe and supported adventure in one of Ireland’s most spectacular settings.

With four distances to choose from 800m, 3.5km, 5.8km or 12km. There’s a place here for every swimmer – from first-timers to seasoned athletes, all are welcome!

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Lanesborough, Longford

Name: Galway Women's Mini Marathon 2026
Description:
Just 20 minutes from Galway City, this is a chance for women to get together and run, jog or walk past Europe's largest Turlough and celebrate being alive.

All funds raised are going towards the development of athletics facilities in the local community.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Crinnage Or Ballywulash, Galway

Name: Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad Exhibition
Description:
Visitors will encounter remarkable objects and stories that reveal the responsibilities of journalism across the globe. 

Curator Dr Catherine Healy says, "Across the world, Irish journalists have worked to reveal human rights abuses and hold the powerful to account. Their reporting on conflict, famine, and hardship has shaped public opinion and provided an impetus for change. They have chronicled the lives of people on the margins and brought home the realities of inequality. In the major centres of Irish settlement, they have challenged negative stereotypes and helped immigrants adapt to their adopted homelands. And they have, in some cases, entirely refashioned what it means to be of Ireland.”
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Gaelforce Great Fjord Swim
Description:
Take on the Great Fjord Swim – a bucket-list open water challenge in the crystal-clear Atlantic waters of Killary Fjord, surrounded by the soaring mountains of Connemara. This is the grand finale of the trilogy and a true West of Ireland experience.

Choose from three distances, with wetsuit and non-wetsuit swimmers welcome in all. Open water swimming doesn’t get more unforgettable than this.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Killary Adventure Company, Killary Adventure Co, Derrynacleigh, Leenaun, Galway

Name: Frontlines: Irish Journalists Abroad Exhibition
Description:
Visitors will encounter remarkable objects and stories that reveal the responsibilities of journalism across the globe. 

Curator Dr Catherine Healy says, "Across the world, Irish journalists have worked to reveal human rights abuses and hold the powerful to account. Their reporting on conflict, famine, and hardship has shaped public opinion and provided an impetus for change. They have chronicled the lives of people on the margins and brought home the realities of inequality. In the major centres of Irish settlement, they have challenged negative stereotypes and helped immigrants adapt to their adopted homelands. And they have, in some cases, entirely refashioned what it means to be of Ireland.”
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Sip and Sleigh Cocktail Experience
Description:
Discover the art of cocktail creation amidst the historic ambiance of our Pearse Lyons Distillery.

Led by our expert mixologists, you'll uncover the secrets behind crafting three signature, holiday-themed cocktails that pay homage to tradition yet embrace innovation. From classic favourites to unique concoctions, you'll learn the techniques and skills needed to shake, stir, and pour with finesse. 

Whether you're seeking a team-building activity, celebrating a special occasion, or simply looking for a memorable outing, our cocktail making class promises an unforgettable journey of taste, culture, and camaraderie.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Pearse Lyons Whiskey Distillery, 121-122, James's St, Saint James, Dublin

Name: Winter Irish Coffee Masterclass
Description:
Join us at the Irish Whiskey Museum for a unique and immersive Irish Coffee Masterclass, where the rich heritage of Irish Whiskey meets the warmth of freshly brewed coffee. Our Irish Coffee Masterclass invites you to indulge in the art of crafting the perfect Irish coffee, a beloved Irish tradition that has captured the hearts of many around the world. Our knowledgeable guides will tell the two tales of how the Irish Coffee was first created; we will let you decide who you think invented this iconic drink.

Available daily until the end of January (excluding 15, 16, 25 December) Booking advised.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Irish Whiskey Museum, 119 Grafton Street, Dublin

Name: Winter Irish Coffee Masterclass
Description:
Join us at the Irish Whiskey Museum for a unique and immersive Irish Coffee Masterclass, where the rich heritage of Irish Whiskey meets the warmth of freshly brewed coffee. Our Irish Coffee Masterclass invites you to indulge in the art of crafting the perfect Irish coffee, a beloved Irish tradition that has captured the hearts of many around the world. Our knowledgeable guides will tell the two tales of how the Irish Coffee was first created; we will let you decide who you think invented this iconic drink.

Available daily until the end of January (excluding 15, 16, 25 December) Booking advised.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Irish Whiskey Museum, 119 Grafton Street, Dublin

Name: DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off & EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum Combo Deal
Description:
DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off Tour
Discover the best of Dublin City with the DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off City Tour. With Fáilte Ireland trained guides who will provide you with all the information, facts, stories about all the places along our 2 tour routes. This city tour will give you the best overview of Dublin City with over 20 stops around the city.
EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum
EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum in Dublin is an interactive experience that explores the history and global impact of Irish emigration.
Situated in the historic CHQ Building along the River Liffey, Stop D on the DoDublin docklands route which is included in the ticket.
The museum features 20 themed galleries utilising advanced technology and multimedia exhibits
Address: 59 O'Connell Street Upper, North City, Dublin

Name: Consairtín
Description:
Treat yourself to the sweet sounds of the concertina and the world of Irish traditional music at the Consairtín 2026.

This national concertina convention celebrates the role of the concertina in traditional music, and it brings together some of the finest concertina performers to share their expertise. Over four days you will get to enjoy classes, workshops, lectures, concert performances, casual sessions and so much more.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Inis, Co. Contae an Chláir, Éire

Name: Gaelforce Great Lake Swim
Description:
This year’s swim begins and ends in Portumna, bringing you to the mystical Lough Derg, where small islands dotted with ancient ruins lend magic to your swim. Choose from three distances in this stunning setting - both wetsuit and non-wetsuit swimmers are welcome.

With three distances to choose from 750m, 2km or 3.9km. There’s a place here for every swimmer – from first-timers to seasoned athletes, all are welcome!

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Portumna Swimming Area, Shannon Rd, Fairyhill, Galway

Name: St. Patrick's Festival Waterford
Description:
St. Patrick’s Festival Waterford will take place from 13th to 17th March 2026. The main event is the Parade on 17th March which runs along the Quay and finishes on The Mall. There will be a diverse mix of festival events over the five days including outdoor amusements, live music stages, poetry and spoken word events, history talks, walking tours, river cruises, wobbly circus, Booley House variety show, story-telling and Irish dancing, children’s music workshops, Irish language events and a tricolour ceremony to commemorate T F Meagher and the introduction of the tricolour to Ireland.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Waterford

Name: AIB Portrait Prize 2025
Description:
The annual competition showcases contemporary portraiture and invites submissions from artists working in all media and disciplines, from across the island of Ireland and from Irish artists living abroad. The Prize aims to celebrate and encourage interest in contemporary portraiture, while raising the profile of the evolving National Portrait Collection at the National Gallery of Ireland. 

Monday: 11am – 5.30pm
Tuesday and Wednesday: 9.15am – 5.30pm 
Thursday: 9.15am – 8.30pm
Friday and Saturday: 9.15am – 5.30pm 
Sunday: 11am – 5.30pm

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: The Spare Prince
Description:
Fri Jan 23rd @ 7pm
Sat & Sun 24th & 25th @ 3pm
Fri 30th 7pm and Sat 31st @ 3pm

“When it comes to Princes, you need an Heir and a Spare”

In this version of the Cinderella Story, the Spare Prince is the real hero and Prince Charming is anything but.

Most everything else in this tale is as you remember it, with one or two Pantofications; Cinderella is still a cleaner for the Ugli Sisters, and their mother certainly has a way with Wordle. The Fairly Godmother is actually in training and the King has an inexplicable love for local GAA.

There is a ball, a lost slipper and a painstaking search for its owner … and the Queen is a bit evil. Almost forgot that bit!

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Live Aid Exhibition
Description:
Through a series of striking displays, the exhibition brings audiences on a visual journey of the Live Aid concerts, capturing the behind-the-scenes preparations, atmosphere and iconic performances by bands and artists on both sides of the Atlantic as the day unfolded.

From Status Quo, U2 and David Bowie in London, to Black Sabbath and Run DMC in Philadelphia Live Aid at the National Library of Ireland immerses visitors in the scale and spirit of this iconic event from the intimacy of the National Photographic Archive.

This free exhibition opens to the public daily (10.00am – 4.00pm)

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Photographic Archive, Meeting House Square, Temple Bar, Dublin

Name: Lisdoon-Nirvana
Description:
For anyone who’s ever been young and dreamed of going to a festival and finding Nirvana…

Travelling on the pillion of his friend’s two-stroke motorbike, they encounter ’80s Ireland and young people from all over making the same pilgrimage.

Laden with camping gear and cheap alcohol smuggled across the border, wearing a converted Garda riot control helmet stuffed with bin bags, and a pair of swimming goggles, our hero undergoes a rite of passage as he ventures out into the big bad beautiful world.

Through his eyes we see the Folk and Rock glory of the Irish Glastonbury, and a country in transition, with a backdrop of recession, church oppression, emigration and Troubles.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Walking Tour: The Houses of Guinness
Description:
This tour will add superb context to the TV series House of Guinness. Finish the tour with a pint of the ‘black stuff’ in a surprise venue closely linked to the start of the Guinness dynasty.
(Does not include visit to the Guinness Brewery)

Name: Shortt Stuff
Description:
Bringing belly laughs and brilliantly bonkers characters to stages across Ireland and beyond, The Shortts – an irreverent and endearing Irish father-daughter sketch comedy act – are redefining what it means to be a comedic double act.

Pat Shortt is a veteran of the comedy circuit, while his daughter Faye is a theatre-trained firecracker with a razor-sharp wit; together this duo has built a cult following with their blend of absurd characters and lightning-quick banter.

Whether skewering small-town politics or turning family feuds into full-blown musical numbers, their shows are as unpredictable as they are unforgettable.

Now touring with their new live show, Shortt Stuff, the Shortts are taking their comedy to theatres!
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: Féile na mBan 2026
Description:
Ireland's festival for the February Bank Holiday weekend is back for 2026! Dedicated to showcasing women in all walks of life, the festival will once again provide a platform to local musicians, performers, speakers and more across multiple venues in the town. 

Both free and ticketed events will take place over the weekend, with some fantastic Irish acts due to be announced in the full lineup in the new year.
 
We have some fantastic Headliners this year, with unmissable events from Niamh Regan, and another edition of ‘Gliondar an Earraigh’- an audience with some of Ireland's best trad musicians, Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh (of Altan), The Henry Girls, Roisin McGrory and Denise Boyle.
Address: Bundoran, Drumacrin, Bundoran, Donegal

Name: St. Patrick's Festival Waterford
Description:
St. Patrick’s Festival Waterford will take place from 13th to 17th March 2026. The main event is the Parade on 17th March which runs along the Quay and finishes on The Mall. There will be a diverse mix of festival events over the five days including outdoor amusements, live music stages, poetry and spoken word events, history talks, walking tours, river cruises, wobbly circus, Booley House variety show, story-telling and Irish dancing, children’s music workshops, Irish language events and a tricolour ceremony to commemorate T F Meagher and the introduction of the tricolour to Ireland.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Waterford

Name: Adam Kay - This Is Going to Hurt 2026
Description:
BAFTA-winning comedian Adam Kay shares his uniquely hilarious diaries as a junior doctor in this “electrifying” (Guardian) evening of stand-up. Over 350,000 tickets sold. Based on the multi-million selling book and multi-award-winning TV series. 

"Intersperses horror stories from the NHS frontline with a catalogue of sublimely silly spoof songs, and some blissfully brilliant wordplay" ★★★★★ Mail on Sunday

"Hilarious and heartbreaking" Charlie Brooker
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: The Tumbling Paddies
Description:
Rapidly becoming one of Ireland’s most popular entertainment acts The Tumbling Paddies have grown from strength to strength particularly in the past two years. It is no surprise that their music is played in many venues throughout the world. 

Originally starting out in 2014 as a five-piece band performing at local weddings and pubs in there beloved County Fermanagh, the band have continuously evolved to where they are today. Song writing and original material is a major part of The Tumbling Paddies. It is no surprise that the band is in hot demand with there exuberant energy filled live show. Their mix of original material along with many classics brings you one of the best nights entertainment out there.
Address: National Opera House, High St, Townparks, Wexford

Name: Fastnet Film Festival
Description: Each May, the seaside village of Schull in West Cork, Ireland, transforms into a vibrant hub for film lovers from around the world. Over five days, more than 100 short films, 20 feature films, and 7,000 visitors gather to celebrate cinema in unique settings, from the village hall and local hotels to cafés, pubs, restaurants, and even screenings on Cape Clear Island. Renowned film industry experts host seminars, workshops, and masterclasses, creating opportunities for learning and collaboration. By connecting emerging filmmakers with seasoned professionals, the festival fosters creativity and dialogue from script to screen. Beyond film, the Festival Fringe brings the village alive with live music, family fun, quizzes, and exhibitions.
Address: Meenvane, Cork

Name: Dublin from Above
Description:
Discover Dublin from a whole new perspective with our exclusive package. Stay for one or more nights and enjoy a delicious 2-course dinner in The Sideline with a glass of house wine on an evening of your choice, and tickets to the iconic Kellogg's Skyline Croke Park tour. Perfect for those seeking a memorable city escape, with a relaxing stay, good food and breathtaking views of Dublin's skyline.

Dublin From Above, hotel offer includes:
Beautifully appointed guest room   
Full Irish breakfast  
2-course meal with a glass of house wine in The Sideline 
Tickets to Kellogg's Skyline Walking Tour

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Jones' Road, Jones' Rd, Drumcondra, Dublin

Name: Rebel Rising Tour
Description:
This tour tells the story of the key events which shaped modern Irish history including the 1916 Easter Rising, the Irish War of Independence, the Anglo-Irish Treaty, the Irish Civil War, and the
Troubles, including the resulting peace process in Northern Ireland and the signing of the Good Friday Agreement in 1998.

The Rebel Rising tour is approximately one hour in duration. Afterwards visitors are also welcome to enjoy the centrepiece film and the museum at their own leisure.

Available: Monday, Thursday & Saturday at 2.30 pm (please note that we are closed on public holidays)

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here
Address: GPO Museum, O'Connell Street Lower, North City, Dublin, Dublin

Name: The Fade Street Market
Description:
Dublin’s busiest and best bespoke market providers, Unique Markets, have hit gold once again with the Fade Street Market, a brand new artisan market in the heart of Dublin's Creative Quarter. It's a match made in market heaven as the much loved, long established Market Bar venue opens its doors to house a weekly market that brings together an eclectic mix of artisan goods, crafts, vintage finds, rare vinyl and unique creations in the heart of Dublin’s busiest shopping area. With a lively atmosphere, a fantastic selection of stalls and a mouth watering tapas menu this market is set to become a weekly highlight for locals and visitors alike.

Every Sunday. 12.30pm - 6.30pm.
Address: 14A Fade St, Dublin, Dublin
