Name: Tuft Your Own Rug
Description:
Join us in our studio to learn the art of rug tufting in this 3 hour workshop. Choose from a wide selection of yarn colours and learn how to tuft, glue and shave your own rug. Decorate your home by hanging your masterpiece on a wall for display, or leave it proudly on the floor the choice is yours!

You can bring your own snacks and have a fun time, this workshop is designed for absolute beginners.
Learn a new skill, get creative with your friends and even bring your rug home with you that very same day.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: 3 Queen St, Smithfield, Dublin

Name: Winter Irish Coffee Masterclass
Description:
Join us at the Irish Whiskey Museum for a unique and immersive Irish Coffee Masterclass, where the rich heritage of Irish Whiskey meets the warmth of freshly brewed coffee. Our Irish Coffee Masterclass invites you to indulge in the art of crafting the perfect Irish coffee, a beloved Irish tradition that has captured the hearts of many around the world. Our knowledgeable guides will tell the two tales of how the Irish Coffee was first created; we will let you decide who you think invented this iconic drink.

Available daily until the end of January (excluding 15, 16, 25 December) Booking advised.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Irish Whiskey Museum, 119 Grafton Street, Dublin

Name: EcoTrail Wicklow 2026
Description:
EcoTrail Wicklow is a spectacular trail running experience set in the heart of Ireland’s “Garden County.” This event invites runners from across Ireland and beyond to explore Wicklow’s stunning natural landscapes, mountain trails, forest paths, glacial valleys & pristine coastline.

Offering multiple race distances, EcoTrail Wicklow welcomes both seasoned trail runners and enthusiastic newcomers. The carefully designed routes highlight Wicklow’s natural beauty and rich heritage while maintaining a strong focus on environmental responsibility.

From the vibrant start/finish area in Bray to panoramic summits and peaceful woodland tracks, every KM delivers an unforgettable sense of adventure.
Address: Bray, Wicklow

Our knowledgeable guides will tell the two tales of how the Irish Coffee was first created; we will let you decide who you think invented this iconic drink.

Available daily until the end of January (excluding 15, 16, 25 December) Booking advised.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Irish Whiskey Museum, 119 Grafton Street, Dublin

Name: EcoTrail Wicklow 2026
Description:
EcoTrail Wicklow is a spectacular trail running experience set in the heart of Ireland’s “Garden County.” This event invites runners from across Ireland and beyond to explore Wicklow’s stunning natural landscapes, mountain trails, forest paths, glacial valleys & pristine coastline.

Offering multiple race distances, EcoTrail Wicklow welcomes both seasoned trail runners and enthusiastic newcomers. The carefully designed routes highlight Wicklow’s natural beauty and rich heritage while maintaining a strong focus on environmental responsibility.

From the vibrant start/finish area in Bray to panoramic summits and peaceful woodland tracks, every KM delivers an unforgettable sense of adventure.
Address: Bray, Wicklow

Name: The Fade Street Market
Description:
Dublin’s busiest and best bespoke market providers, Unique Markets, have hit gold once again with the Fade Street Market, a brand new artisan market in the heart of Dublin's Creative Quarter. It's a match made in market heaven as the much loved, long established Market Bar venue opens its doors to house a weekly market that brings together an eclectic mix of artisan goods, crafts, vintage finds, rare vinyl and unique creations in the heart of Dublin’s busiest shopping area. With a lively atmosphere, a fantastic selection of stalls and a mouth watering tapas menu this market is set to become a weekly highlight for locals and visitors alike.

Every Sunday. 12.30pm - 6.30pm.
Address: 14A Fade St, Dublin, Dublin

Name: The 502s
Description:
Often called “the happiest band on Earth,” the Orlando-bred five piece have earned a passionate following for their kaleidoscopic beach-folk, bringing their exhilarating live show to headline tours around the world and turning out timeless hits like their 2021 viral smash “Just A Little While.”

Their fourth album, Easy Street marks the most fully realized work yet from The 502s, whose lineup includes Ed Isola, horn player Joe Capati, drummer Sean Froelich, bassist Nick Dallas, and guitarist Graci Phillips.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Vicar Street, 58-59, Thomas St, The Liberties, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Mon Rovîa
Description:
"I’m Mon, born in Mon Rovia, Liberia. My life can be summed up by being born into a civil war, escaping the life of a child soldier, rescued by the hands of missionaries. Spent a lot of my developmental years living in a variety of places with a number of life experiences. And then layered on top of all of this is the assimilation I felt being a transracially adopted refugee. My music in a nutshell is a culmination of these experiences blended with the empathy I feel for the experiences with others. The mission of my music is to heal with others - with every nation and tongue, in due time."

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Vicar Street, 58-59, Thomas St, The Liberties, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Dan McCabe
Description:
He has since established himself as one of Ireland’s most compelling voices, blending the soul of traditional Irish ballads with contemporary folk.

With a devoted home following and an ever-expanding international audience, Dan McCabe continues to redefine modern folk music.

With very special guest, Gearoid McCarthy
Gearoid McCarthy is gaining huge recognition for his rendition of the great Irish/Folk ballads. He has over one million followers on Social Media, especially so on Tik Tok. Gearoid showcases his unique vocal ability with a mesmerising performance. Not to be missed.

Name: Galway International Arts Festival 2026
Description:
The world-class Galway International Arts Festival returns from 13-26 July 2026 with a thrilling mix of local & global talent across a multidisciplinary programme that includes theatre, music, circus, dance, opera, visual art, spectacle & bold ideas through its First Thought series. 

The festival announces many of its Heineken Big Top acts the November before the festival each year & big name acts already announced for 2026 include pop icons James; six-time GRAMMY®-nominated The Flaming Lips, supported by Mercury Rev; Block Rockin Beats; Emeli Sandé & James Morrison; BELL X1; and the legendary Saw Doctors, with tickets on sale now.  

More acts will be announced before end 2025, while the full Festival programme announced in May 2026.
Address: Galway City, Galway

Name: Dublin from Above
Description:
Discover Dublin from a whole new perspective with our exclusive package. Stay for one or more nights and enjoy a delicious 2-course dinner in The Sideline with a glass of house wine on an evening of your choice, and tickets to the iconic Kellogg's Skyline Croke Park tour. Perfect for those seeking a memorable city escape, with a relaxing stay, good food and breathtaking views of Dublin's skyline.

Dublin From Above, hotel offer includes:
Beautifully appointed guest room   
Full Irish breakfast  
2-course meal with a glass of house wine in The Sideline 
Tickets to Kellogg's Skyline Walking Tour

Name: Welcoming Spring: St. Brigid’s Day Afternoon Tea at Wells House
Description:
The February Bank Holiday weekend will mark the gentle beginning of spring at Wells House & Gardens, and what better way to welcome the season than with a delightful Open Afternoon Tea Weekend?

Step into the elegance of the house and indulge in a beautifully presented afternoon tea, complete with freshly baked treats, delicate finger sandwiches, and perfectly brewed tea served on classic china.

Whether you’re catching up with friends, treating someone special, or simply embracing the charm of Wells House as nature begins to bloom, our Afternoon Tea offers the perfect blend of warmth, relaxation, and seasonal celebration.

Join us for a memorable start to spring in one of Wexford’s most enchanting settings.
Address: Wells House & Gardens, Ballyedmond, Gorey, Wexford

Name: Galway International Arts Festival 2026
Description:
The world-class Galway International Arts Festival returns from 13-26 July 2026 with a thrilling mix of local & global talent across a multidisciplinary programme that includes theatre, music, circus, dance, opera, visual art, spectacle & bold ideas through its First Thought series. 

The festival announces many of its Heineken Big Top acts the November before the festival each year & big name acts already announced for 2026 include pop icons James; six-time GRAMMY®-nominated The Flaming Lips, supported by Mercury Rev; Block Rockin Beats; Emeli Sandé & James Morrison; BELL X1; and the legendary Saw Doctors, with tickets on sale now.  

More acts will be announced before end 2025, while the full Festival programme announced in May 2026.
Address: Galway City, Galway

Name: The Irish Food Trail
Description:
Discover the heart of Dublin through its culinary delights with Flavour Trails. As you embark on the Irish Food Trail tour, you’ll be introduced to the authentic tastes and tales that have shaped Dublin’s rich food heritage. Immerse yourself in the story of Irish food, learn about Irish produce and the dishes we are best known for in Ireland. 

Enjoy a delicious traditional 3 course meal over 3 unique venues. The first 2 courses  will be paired with a local Irish beer or cider and in the final venue, dessert will be paired with an Irish Coffee making experience.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Powerscourt Townhouse Centre, 59 William St S, Centre, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Turner as Inspiration
Description:
Since their bequest by Henry Vaughan in 1901, these works have been exhibited every January, offering audiences a glimpse into Turner’s evolving artistry - from English parkland scenes to his expressive visions of Venice and the Alps.

This year’s exhibition is enriched by the inclusion of watercolours and prints by artists who found inspiration in Turner’s mastery of light, colour, and atmosphere.

Curator: Anne Hodge, Curator of Prints and Drawings at the National Gallery of Ireland.

1 - 31 January 2025 | Print Gallery | Free admission

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Treetop Night Lights at Beyond the Trees Avondale
Description:
Experience the forest after dark in a completely novel way with the dazzling and award winning Treetop Night Lights and experience an array of 3D light installations and animations, in a whole new show for 2025/2026! Travel the beautifully lit treetop walkway, tunnel and viewing tower and meet an array of lit up forest creatures that make Avondale Forest Park their home. 

This is an experience that all ages and all abilities will enjoy together getting up close and personal with nature after dark. Less than one hour’s drive from Dublin city in the heart of the Wicklow countryside, Treetop Night Lights provides a family-friendly, age-friendly and truly accessible spectacle.

See website for opening days and hours from 14th November 2025.
Address: Avondale, Wicklow

Name: The Jeanie Johnston - Discover Ireland's Dark History
Description:
Step aboard the Jeanie Johnston and journey back to the 19th century, when thousands fled the Irish Famine in search of a new life. On this 50-minute guided tour, explore the tall ship’s upper deck, admire its majestic masts, and take in the sights of Dublin’s Docklands. 

Then venture below deck to witness the cramped quarters where 250 emigrants once endured perilous voyages. Through powerful stories of passengers and crew, you’ll uncover the hardships, hopes, and resilience that defined their journey across the Atlantic.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Venue Name: The Jeanie Johnston: An Irish Famine Story
Address: The Jeanie Johnston: An Irish Famine Story, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Tinsel and Tonic Gin School Experience
Description:
Don an apron and join our distillery experts as they guide you through the intricate process of gin distillation. With access to a wide selection of botanicals, you'll have the freedom to create a blend that matches your personal taste or the preferences of someone special. Whether you prefer citrusy, floral, or spicy notes, our experts will help you achieve the perfect balance.

As you craft your bespoke gin, enjoy sipping on expertly mixed gin and tonics paired with delightful seasonal bites. Join us for Tinsel and Tonic Gin School, where craftsmanship meets holiday cheer. Book your spot now and create a gin that’s uniquely yours, perfect for celebrating the season or gifting to someone special.
Address: Pearse Lyons Whiskey Distillery, 121-122, James's St, Saint James, Dublin

Name: The Irish Food Trail
Description:
Discover the heart of Dublin through its culinary delights with Flavour Trails. As you embark on the Irish Food Trail tour, you’ll be introduced to the authentic tastes and tales that have shaped Dublin’s rich food heritage. Immerse yourself in the story of Irish food, learn about Irish produce and the dishes we are best known for in Ireland. 

Enjoy a delicious traditional 3 course meal over 3 unique venues. The first 2 courses  will be paired with a local Irish beer or cider and in the final venue, dessert will be paired with an Irish Coffee making experience.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Powerscourt Townhouse Centre, 59 William St S, Centre, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Specialised Daily Guided Tours - EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum
Description:
Join our daily guided tour at 1pm, Monday to Friday, and explore 20 interactive galleries in the company of one of our expert guides. In this engaging one-hour experience, you’ll uncover the personal stories behind Ireland’s global journey - stories of adventure, struggle, and legacy that bring history to life. Ideal for first-time visitors looking to get the most out of EPIC with the insight only a guide can offer.

Daily tours Monday to Friday from 2 - 6 February 2026.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum, The Chq Building, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Specialised Daily Guided tour - CHQ Tour
Description:
Built in 1817 as a whiskey, wine and tobacco warehouse – Stack A, as it was previously known, was designed by Sir John Rennie and was Dublin’s first fireproof warehouse. Let your guide bring you the stories of yesteryear and how Custom House Quay and Georges Dock played key roles in Ireland’s trade with the outside world. Join us as we show you around 200 years of architectural history.

Daily tour from Mon 26 - Fri 30 January 2026 at 1pm.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: CHQ Dublin, Custom House Quay, North Wall, Dublin, Dublin

Discover the art of cocktail creation amidst the historic ambiance of our Pearse Lyons Distillery.

Led by our expert mixologists, you'll uncover the secrets behind crafting three signature, holiday-themed cocktails that pay homage to tradition yet embrace innovation. Name: The Fureys Farewell Concert
Description:
After 13 unforgettable shows, THE FUREYS return to County Longford for their final concert there on Saturday 7th February as part of their FAREWELL TOUR. Legends of Irish music, they’re known for hits like "I Will Love You", "When You Were Sweet 16", and "The Green Fields of France". Eddie Furey began his journey in the 1960s folk revival, sharing a flat with Billy Connolly and Gerry Rafferty—who later wrote "Her Father Didn’t Like Me Anyway" for him. Their UK chart success helped bring Irish folk to wider audiences, including a 1981 "Top of the Pops" debut. 

With songs that evoke joy and sorrow, The Fureys’ music has inspired generations, even teaching Dave Stewart of The Eurythmics his first guitar chords.
Address: Backstage Theatre, Farneyhoogan, Farranyoogan, Longford

Name: The Dead by James Joyce
Description:
Following two completely sold-out runs, The Dead by James Joyce, a thrilling immersive theatre experience, returns by popular demand to Museum of Literature Ireland from 20 November 2026 - 31 January 2027.

The sell-out sensation The Dead, adapted and directed by Louise Lowe, is currently running from 21 Nov 2025 - 1 Feb 2026. 

This unforgettable production of Joyce's masterpiece takes place in the rich historical interior and unseen rooms of UCD Newman House. 

Audiences are greeted by the maid Lily – who is literally run off her feet – before taking the journey up the opulent staircase, to embark on an evening of laughter, music and dance – ending in an epiphany like no other.

Produced by ANU and Landmark Productions, in association with MoLI.
Venue Name: UCD Newman House, 85 St Stephen's Green
Address: 85 St Stephen's Green, Saint Kevin's, Dublin

Name: St Brendan the Navigator Community Festival 2026
Description:
Following on from last year's success of the Navigator Community Festival, the spirit of exploration comes alive once again as Fenit and surrounding communities proudly host the St. Brendan Navigator Festival, an inspiring celebration of heritage, adventure and community in honour of Kerry’s legendary patron saint. 

Taking place from Thursday 14 to Sunday 17 May 2026, with the majority of events centred around St Brendan’s feast day 16 May, the festival pays tribute to one of Ireland’s most iconic voyagers. The St. Brendan Navigator Festival invites locals and visitors alike to reconnect with Kerry’s rich seafaring roots and celebrate the enduring legacy of curiosity and courage that St. Brendan represents.
Address: Fenit Without, Fenit, Kerry

Name: The Brandon Bay Run 2026
Description:
So, what exactly is The Brandon Bay Run? This is a half marathon, 10k & 5k event which is run entirely on the golden sandy beach that stretches around Brandon Bay in the Kingdom of Kerry - all the way from Fahamore in the Maharees, to Fermoyle point under Mt Brandon itself. 

The event starts and finishes at Fahamore in the Maharees and competitors doing the half marathon effectively run from one point of the 7 mile beach to the other and back on an 'out and back' course. The event is being organised and run by Castlegregory Celtic F.C. and is a non-profit event. It is Ireland's original running event ENTIRELY run on the beach!!

Entry fees vary.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: The Brandon Bay Run, Fahamore, Kerry

As you embark on the Irish Food Trail tour, you’ll be introduced to the authentic tastes and tales that have shaped Dublin’s rich food heritage. Immerse yourself in the story of Irish food, learn about Irish produce and the dishes we are best known for in Ireland. 

Enjoy a delicious traditional 3 course meal over 3 unique venues. The first 2 courses  will be paired with a local Irish beer or cider and in the final venue, dessert will be paired with an Irish Coffee making experience.

Readers are advised to check with the organiser before relying on the details published here.
Address: Powerscourt Townhouse Centre, 59 William St S, Centre, Dublin, Dublin

Name: DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off & EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum Combo Deal
Description:
DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off Tour
Discover the best of Dublin City with the DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off City Tour. With Fáilte Ireland trained guides who will provide you with all the information, facts, stories about all the places along our 2 tour routes. This city tour will give you the best overview of Dublin City with over 20 stops around the city.
EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum
EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum in Dublin is an interactive experience that explores the history and global impact of Irish emigration.
Situated in the historic CHQ Building along the River Liffey, Stop D on the DoDublin docklands route which is included in the ticket.
The museum features 20 themed galleries utilising advanced technology and multimedia exhibits
Address: 59 O'Connell Street Upper, North City, Dublin

Name: Whiskey and Warmth Experience
Description:
Join us at our distillery for an Irish coffee masterclass that promises to warm your soul and elevate your cocktail crafting.

Guided by our whiskey experts, you'll embark on a sensory journey through a tasting of our finest Irish whiskeys. Discover the unique notes and flavours that make each whiskey distinct, and learn the art of crafting the perfect Irish coffee.

Whether you're a whiskey connoisseur or a coffee enthusiast, Whiskey & Warmth is the ideal Winter activity to share with friends, family, or fellow aficionados. Dive into the rich traditions of Irish hospitality and create a memorable experience that will keep you warm all season long.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Pearse Lyons Whiskey Distillery, 121-122, James's St, Saint James, Dublin

Name: Wild Lights at Dublin Zoo
Description:
Discover a spectacular world where colour and wildlife collide! Spot enchanting red pandas and shimmering peacocks on a magical journey through radiant lanterns. Be sure to witness our largest lantern EVER - one of the biggest in all of Europe.

Your adventure continues at our brand-new Winter Glow Village! Savour festive delights like crêpes, pizza, and mulled wine under a canopy of enchanting lanterns. It’s the perfect place to warm up and soak in the wonder.

Plus, a limited number of Santa's Grotto experiences at Dublin Zoo remain. Create cherished Christmas memories by booking a day or night visit with Santa before it’s too late!

 Step into The Wild Kaleidoscope at Dublin Zoo this winter!

See Dublin Zoo website for dates/times/ticket prices.
Address: Dublin Zoo, Phoenix park, Saint James, Dublin

Name: Turner as Inspiration
Description:
Since their bequest by Henry Vaughan in 1901, these works have been exhibited every January, offering audiences a glimpse into Turner’s evolving artistry - from English parkland scenes to his expressive visions of Venice and the Alps.

This year’s exhibition is enriched by the inclusion of watercolours and prints by artists who found inspiration in Turner’s mastery of light, colour, and atmosphere.

Curator: Anne Hodge, Curator of Prints and Drawings at the National Gallery of Ireland.

1 - 31 January 2025 | Print Gallery | Free admission

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: National Gallery of Ireland, Merrion Square W, Dublin, Dublin

Name: Whiskey and Warmth Experience
Description:
Join us at our distillery for an Irish coffee masterclass that promises to warm your soul and elevate your cocktail crafting.

Guided by our whiskey experts, you'll embark on a sensory journey through a tasting of our finest Irish whiskeys. Discover the unique notes and flavours that make each whiskey distinct, and learn the art of crafting the perfect Irish coffee.

Whether you're a whiskey connoisseur or a coffee enthusiast, Whiskey & Warmth is the ideal Winter activity to share with friends, family, or fellow aficionados. Dive into the rich traditions of Irish hospitality and create a memorable experience that will keep you warm all season long.

Readers are advised to check with the venue before relying on the details published here.
Address: Pearse Lyons Whiskey Distillery, 121-122, James's St, Saint James, Dublin

Name: Westport Folk & Bluegrass Festival 2026
Description:
Westport Folk & Bluegrass Festival (WFBF) was established in 2007 to broaden public access to the traditional 
music of Ireland & the USA. Not only is it now considered the leading annual Bluegrass event in Ireland, it is also recognised as a vital showcase for Irish musicians in the west. It is a unique event where people from all parts of the performer-audience spectrum can connect, collaborate, and evolve their musical traditions. 

This excellent track record is reflected in the willingness of prime-calibre musical performers to travel to this relatively remote place to play, the number of return visitors to the festival from the USA, UK, Europe and Ireland, and the fondness with which it is viewed by the local community.
Address: Westport, Mayo

Name: Wild Lights at Dublin Zoo
Description:
Discover a spectacular world where colour and wildlife collide! Spot enchanting red pandas and shimmering peacocks on a magical journey through radiant lanterns. Be sure to witness our largest lantern EVER - one of the biggest in all of Europe.

Your adventure continues at our brand-new Winter Glow Village! Savour festive delights like crêpes, pizza, and mulled wine under a canopy of enchanting lanterns. It’s the perfect place to warm up and soak in the wonder.

Plus, a limited number of Santa's Grotto experiences at Dublin Zoo remain. Create cherished Christmas memories by booking a day or night visit with Santa before it’s too late!

 Step into The Wild Kaleidoscope at Dublin Zoo this winter!

See Dublin Zoo website for dates/times/ticket prices.
Address: Dublin Zoo, Phoenix park, Saint James, Dublin

Name: DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off & EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum Combo Deal
Description:
DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off Tour
Discover the best of Dublin City with the DoDublin Hop-on Hop-off City Tour. With Fáilte Ireland trained guides who will provide you with all the information, facts, stories about all the places along our 2 tour routes. This city tour will give you the best overview of Dublin City with over 20 stops around the city.
EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum
EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum in Dublin is an interactive experience that explores the history and global impact of Irish emigration.
Situated in the historic CHQ Building along the River Liffey, Stop D on the DoDublin docklands route which is included in the ticket.
The museum features 20 themed galleries utilising advanced technology and multimedia exhibits
Address: 59 O'Connell Street Upper, North City, Dublin

Name: The Dublin Double: EPIC and Dublin Zoo Winter Experience
Description:
This winter, Dublin comes alive with story, light, and family memories as EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum and Dublin Zoo unite for The Dublin Double - a one-of-a-kind city adventure that takes visitors from the vibrant heart of the Docklands to the glowing trails of Phoenix Park, all in a single, unforgettable day.

Start your day at EPIC museum, Dublin’s award-winning interactive experience that celebrates the stories of Irish people who changed the world through powerful storytelling and immersive technology. Then, step into the glowing world of Wild Lights: Kaleidoscope at Dublin Zoo - a spectacular celebration of colour, wildlife and creativity that lights up the night.
Address: Dublin Zoo, Phoenix park, Saint James, Dublin
